NXT-71249.summary: FIN — Opening Balance Import: Data Exchange
NXT-71249.ac:
*AC1 — Import Type Available* Given an authorized Finance user opens Import Monitor, When they view Import Types, Then Opening Balance Import is available regardless of whether prerequisites are met.

*AC2 — Prerequisite Check Blocks Import When Not Met* Given the chart of accounts contains no Asset, Liability, or Equity accounts (or no sites exist in Site Management), When the user uploads a file for Opening Balance Import, Then the system displays a blocking message explaining the missing prerequisite with a link to the relevant configuration area, and does not proceed to Fiscal Year Selection.

*AC3 — Fiscal Year Selection Required* Given a user uploads a file for Opening Balance Import and prerequisites are met, When they proceed past file upload, Then they must select a Fiscal Year before reaching Amount Format Selection or column mapping.

*AC4 — Amount Format Selection Drives Mapping UI* Given a user selects "My file has separate Debit and Credit columns," When the column mapping UI loads, Then Debit and Credit appear as required target fields and Amount does not appear. Given a user selects "My file has a single Amount column," When the column mapping UI loads, Then Amount appears as the required target field and Debit/Credit do not appear.

*AC5 — Account Type Restriction Enforced* Given an upload contains rows referencing Revenue or Expenditure GLAccounts, When preview runs, Then those rows are flagged invalid with an error stating opening balances cannot be set for those account types.

*AC6 — Required Fields Enforced* Given an upload contains rows missing GLAccount, SiteCode, or the mapped amount field(s), When preview runs, Then those rows are flagged invalid and cannot be imported until corrected. Comments must be mapped but blank values are accepted and auto-filled with the default placeholder.

*AC7 — Zero Amount Blocked (Scheme B) / Both Debit and Credit Populated (Scheme A)* Given an upload using Scheme B contains rows with Amount = 0, or an upload using Scheme A contains rows where both Debit and Credit are populated or both are zero, When preview runs, Then those rows are flagged invalid with an error indicating the amount entry is not valid.

*AC8 — Batch Must Balance (strict equality)* Given total debit-side amounts ≠ total credit-side amounts by any amount, When the user attempts import, Then import is blocked and totals are displayed with the imbalance amount.

*AC9 — Row Errors Block Entire Import* Given an upload contains one or more rows with validation errors, When the user views the preview, Then the confirm action is disabled and the system must not allow importing only the valid rows. All errors must be resolved by correcting and re-uploading the file.

*AC10 — Import Is Atomic* Given the user confirms an import and a system-level error occurs during processing, When the error is encountered, Then the entire import rolls back, no opening balance data is written, and the user is notified that the import failed.

*AC11 — Duplicate GLAccount + SiteCode Blocked* Given an upload contains more than one row for the same GLAccount and SiteCode combination, When preview runs, Then those rows are flagged invalid with an error identifying the duplicate, and the import cannot proceed until the user consolidates to one row per account per site.

*AC12 — Existing Import Replacement Warning* Given an opening balance import already exists for the selected fiscal year, When the user attempts to confirm a new import, Then the system warns that existing opening balances will be replaced and requires explicit confirmation before proceeding. _(Hard reversal of previous entries is handled by the business logic layer.)_

*AC13 — Saved Mapping Does Not Persist Fiscal Year* Given a saved mapping template exists, When the user uploads a new file with the same layout, Then the amount format and column mapping are auto-applied, but the user must still select a Fiscal Year before proceeding to preview.

*AC14 — Results Visible* Given an import completes, When the user opens import results, Then they can see status, the selected Fiscal Year, record counts, totals, and row-level errors if any.

*AC15 — SiteCode Validated Against Site Management* Given an upload contains a SiteCode that does not exist in Site Management, When preview runs, Then that row is flagged invalid with a clear error consistent with Import Monitor patterns.

----

h2. Edge / Empty / Error States

||Scenario||Behavior||
|No Asset/Liability/Equity accounts in chart of accounts|Import blocked after file upload with message and link to GL Accounts configuration|
|No sites configured in Site Management|Import blocked after file upload with message and link to Site Management|
|Empty file or header-only|Import blocked: _"No data rows found."_|
|Non-numeric Amount field|Row invalid with parsing error|
|Both Debit and Credit populated in same row (Scheme A)|Row invalid: _"Exactly one of Debit or Credit must have a value."_|
|Negative value in Debit or Credit column (Scheme A)|Row invalid: _"Debit and Credit values must be positive."_|
|Unknown GLAccount|Row invalid with clear error consistent with Import Monitor patterns|
|Unknown SiteCode|Row invalid with clear error consistent with Import Monitor patterns|
|Multiple FiscalYear values in one file|Import blocked; opening balance import must target a single fiscal year|
|Revenue or Expenditure GLAccount|Row invalid with explicit account type error|
|Duplicate GLAccount + SiteCode in same file|Rows flagged invalid: _"Duplicate entry for account [GLAccount] at site [SiteCode]."_ User must consolidate to one row per account per site.|
|Re-import for a fiscal year that already has opening balances|System warns user, requires explicit confirmation before proceeding|
|File contains mix of valid and invalid rows|Confirm action disabled; all errors must be resolved before import. No partial import allowed.|
|System error during import processing|Entire import rolls back; no data written; user notified of failure|

----

h2. Defaults

* *Fiscal Year:* Selected at the import level after file upload. Not mapped from the file. Not saved as part of mapping templates. Selected fresh for each import.
* *Amount format selection:* The user selects their amount format after Fiscal Year selection and before column mapping. The import UI must surface the appropriate guidance text based on the selection. For Scheme B: _"Positive values = debit, negative values = credit."_ For Scheme A: _"Enter a positive value in exactly one column per row."_
* For district-wide entries, the user should use the site code designated as the district-level site in their Site Management configuration. The import UI guidance text should read: _"For district-wide entries, use your district-level site code as configured in Site Management."_
* *Comments default:* Comments is a required field in the column mapping. If a row's value is blank, the system auto-fills with _"Opening Balance Import"_. The database requires a description on every entry.
* AccountName defaults to blank/null when not mapped.
NXT-71249.desc:
As a district finance user, I want to import opening account balances into Financials at the start of a fiscal year or when going live on the system, so that my balance sheet and trial balance reflect accurate starting positions for permanent accounts before any period transactions are recorded.

----

h2. Scope

This story covers the Import Monitor pipeline for the Opening Balance Import type: import type setup, file upload, prerequisites check, amount format selection, column mapping, validation, preview, saved mapping, and import results. On successful confirmation, validated data is handed off to the business logic layer for ledger entry creation.

----

h2. Requirements

h3. 1) Import Type

1.1 A new Import Monitor Import Type exists named *Opening Balance Import* with help text: _"Used to establish beginning account balances for Assets, Liabilities, and Equity at fiscal year start or system go-live."_

1.2 Import Type supports the same upload formats as other Import Monitor types (XLSX/CSV per current platform behavior).

1.3 Import Type is available only to users with appropriate Financials permissions.

h3. 2) Prerequisites

2.1 The Opening Balance Import type is always visible in Import Monitor for authorized users. However, after file upload, the system must check that the following prerequisites are met before proceeding to Amount Format Selection:

* *Chart of Accounts:* At least one Asset, Liability, or Equity account must exist in the chart of accounts.
* *Site Management:* At least one site must be configured in Site Management.

2.2 If either prerequisite is not met, the system must display a blocking message after file upload explaining what is missing and how to resolve it. The message must include a link to the relevant configuration area (Chart of Accounts import or Site Management).

2.3 Example blocking message: _"Opening Balance Import requires a chart of accounts with at least one Asset, Liability, or Equity account. Please import your chart of accounts first."_ With a link to Configuration > General Ledger > GL Accounts.

h3. 3) Fiscal Year Selection

3.1 After file upload and prerequisite check, the user must select a *Fiscal Year* from a dropdown of valid fiscal years in the system.

3.2 All rows in the imported file are assigned to the selected Fiscal Year. This value is not mapped from the file.

3.3 Each fiscal year may have only one active opening balance import. If an import already exists for the selected fiscal year, the system must warn the user that proceeding will replace it (see Section 7.3).

3.4 Fiscal Year is *not* persisted as part of saved mapping templates. It must be selected fresh for each import.

h3. 4) Amount Format Selection

4.1 After Fiscal Year selection and before column mapping, the user must select an *Amount Format* from two options:

* _"My file has separate Debit and Credit columns"_ → Scheme A
* _"My file has a single Amount column (positive = debit, negative = credit)"_ → Scheme B

4.2 The selection determines which amount target fields appear in the column mapping UI. Scheme A displays Debit and Credit as required target fields. Scheme B displays Amount as the required target field. The non-selected fields must not appear in the mapping UI.

4.3 The Amount Format selection is persisted as part of saved mapping templates.

h3. 5) Required Target Fields

The import mapping UI must allow mapping uploaded columns to these target fields:

||Field||Notes||
|GLAccount|Must reference an Asset, Liability, or Equity account|
|SiteCode|Site the balance applies to. For district-wide entries, use the site code designated as the district-level site in Site Management.|
|Comments|Free text reference field; max 50 characters. If blank, the system auto-fills with _"Opening Balance Import"_.|

*Amount fields (determined by the Amount Format selection in Section 4):*

||Amount Format||Target Fields Shown||Convention||
|Separate Debit and Credit columns|Debit, Credit|Separate positive values in each column|
|Single Amount column|Amount|Positive = debit, negative = credit|

h3. 6) Optional Target Fields

||Field||Notes||
|AccountName|Descriptive label for the account|

h3. 7) Validation Rules (row-level)

7.1 GLAccount, SiteCode, and Comments must be present in the column mapping. Amount fields must be present per the selected amount format. If a row's Comments value is blank, the system auto-fills with _"Opening Balance Import"_.

7.2 Amount validation must follow the selected amount format:

*If Scheme A (Debit/Credit):*

* Exactly one of Debit or Credit must be a positive number per row.
* Negative values are not allowed.

*If Scheme B (Amount):*

* Amount must be non-zero.
* Negative amounts are allowed (treated as credits by convention).

7.3 Zero values are not allowed in either scheme and will be flagged as invalid.

7.4 GLAccount must correspond to an Asset, Liability, or Equity account in the chart of accounts. Rows referencing Revenue or Expenditure accounts are invalid and blocked with the error: _"Opening balances cannot be set for Revenue or Expenditure accounts."_

7.5 GLAccount must exist in the chart of accounts. Unknown accounts display a warning consistent with Import Monitor patterns.

7.6 SiteCode must reference a valid site in Site Management. Unknown site codes are flagged as invalid.

7.7 Each GLAccount + SiteCode combination must appear only once in the import file. Duplicate combinations are flagged as invalid with the error: _"Duplicate entry for account [GLAccount] at site [SiteCode]. Each account may only appear once per site."_ The user must consolidate to a single net balance per account per site in their file before uploading.

h3. 8) Validation Rules (import-level)

8.1 The system must calculate and display:

* Total rows
* Total debit-side amounts and total credit-side amounts (computed from the selected amount format)

8.2 Import must be blocked if total debits ≠ total credits. No variance is permitted — the batch must balance exactly. Totals must be displayed to the user alongside the blocking message.

8.3 If an opening balance import already exists for the selected fiscal year, the system must warn the user that proceeding will replace the existing opening balances and require explicit confirmation before proceeding. _(The reversal and re-creation of ledger entries is handled by the business logic layer — see companion story.)_

8.4 The import must be *atomic* — either all rows are imported successfully, or no rows are imported. Partial imports are not allowed. If any system-level error occurs during import processing, the entire import must roll back and the user must be notified that no data was written.

h3. 9) Preview and Confirm

9.1 After file upload, prerequisite check, Fiscal Year selection, amount format selection, and column mapping, the user must see a preview showing the selected Fiscal Year, totals, the auto-assigned effective date (provided by the business logic layer), a sample of parsed rows, and any validation errors before confirming.

9.2 The user must explicitly confirm to perform the import.

9.3 If any rows contain validation errors, the confirm action must be disabled. All row-level errors must be resolved (by correcting and re-uploading the file) before the import can proceed. The system must not allow the user to import only the valid rows.

9.4 The preview must clearly indicate which accounts will receive opening balances and the direction (debit/credit) of each.

h3. 10) Saved Mapping Behavior

10.1 The user can save a column mapping as a named template for the Opening Balance Import type. The saved template includes the selected amount format.

10.2 Future imports using the same template automatically apply the saved amount format and column mapping and proceed directly to preview.

10.3 The user can edit or update the saved mapping and amount format at any time.

h3. 11) Import Results and Auditability

11.1 Each import must produce an import result record consistent with Import Monitor patterns (status, counts, timestamps, user).

11.2 The user can view import results including:

* Success/failed status
* Total debits and credits
* Record counts
* Row-level error messages for invalid records

11.3 The import record must be visible in audit history so administrators can see when opening balances were set or replaced and by whom.

h3. 12) Handoff to Business Logic

12.1 On successful confirmation, the data exchange layer passes the validated import data to the business logic layer, which is responsible for: assigning the effective date, creating the ledger entry and lines, handling hard reversal of any previous import, and making the data available to downstream reports.

NXT-71240.summary: FIN — Opening Balance Import: Business Logic
NXT-71240.ac:
*AC1 — Ledger Entry Created with Correct Entry Type* Given the data exchange layer passes validated import data after user confirmation, When the business logic layer processes the data, Then one ledger entry is created with entry type "Opening Balance" containing one line per imported row.

*AC2 — Effective Date Is Day Before Fiscal Year Start* Given the user imports opening balances for a fiscal year starting July 1, When the ledger entry is created, Then the effective date is June 30.

*AC3 — Scheme A Amounts Mapped Directly* Given the import uses Scheme A (Debit/Credit), When the ledger entry lines are created, Then each line carries the debit or credit value from the file and zero on the opposite side.

*AC4 — Scheme B Amounts Converted Correctly* Given the import uses Scheme B (signed Amount), When the ledger entry lines are created, Then positive values are stored as debits and negative values are stored as credits (absolute value), with zero on the opposite side.

*AC5 — Entry Is Read-Only* Given an opening balance ledger entry exists, When a user attempts to edit or reverse it through the UI, Then the system blocks the action — the entry can only be replaced by a new opening balance import.

*AC6 — Hard Reversal on Re-Import* Given an opening balance import already exists for the selected fiscal year and the user confirms a re-import, When the business logic layer processes the new import, Then all previous opening balance entry lines are reversed (hard reversal, not soft delete) with reversal entries dated the same day as the originals, and new entry lines are written from the new import.

*AC7 — Reversal + Re-Creation Is Atomic* Given a re-import is in progress and a system error occurs during the reversal or re-creation, When the error is encountered, Then no data is changed — the previous opening balance entries remain intact and the user is notified.

*AC8 — Opening Balances Exempt from Financial Period Constraints* Given the first period of the specified fiscal year is closed or has never been opened, When the business logic layer processes the import, Then the ledger entry is created successfully regardless of the period's open/close status.

*AC9 — Trial Balance Reflects Imported Opening Balance* Given an opening balance import has been completed for a fiscal year, When the user runs a Trial Balance for any period in that fiscal year, Then the Opening Balance column reflects the imported fiscal year starting position.

*AC10 — Balance Sheet Beginning Balance Computed Correctly for Period 1* Given an opening balance import has been completed for a fiscal year, When the user runs a Balance Sheet for the first period of the fiscal year (or fiscal YTD), Then the beginning balance equals the imported opening balance.

*AC11 — Balance Sheet Beginning Balance Computed Correctly for Later Periods* Given an opening balance import has been completed for a fiscal year and transactions exist in prior periods, When the user runs a Balance Sheet for a period after Period 1, Then the beginning balance equals the imported opening balance plus cumulative transaction activity from all prior periods in that fiscal year.

*AC12 — Missing Import Surfaced in Reports* Given no opening balance import exists for a fiscal year, When the user runs a Trial Balance or Balance Sheet, Then the Opening Balance column shows {{$0.00}} with a visual indicator that no opening balance has been imported — the column must not contain a text string.

*AC13 — Normal Balance Not Enforced by Base Type* Given an import contains an asset account with a credit balance (abnormal balance), When the business logic layer processes the import, Then the entry is created as-is — the system does not reject or modify the balance direction based on account type.

----

h2. Edge / Empty / Error States

||Scenario||Behavior||
|Re-import: reversal fails mid-process|Entire operation rolls back; previous entries remain intact; user notified|
|Re-import: new entry creation fails after successful reversal|Entire operation rolls back; previous entries are restored; user notified|
|Opening balance entry already exists and user re-imports with fewer accounts|Previous entry fully reversed; new entry has only the accounts in the new file — missing accounts from the previous import are not carried forward|
|No opening balance import exists for a fiscal year|Reports show $0.00 with visual indicator|
|Abnormal balance (e.g., asset with credit balance)|Accepted — system does not enforce normal balance direction|

----

h2. Data Model Summary

||Attribute||Value||
|Entry type|"Opening Balance" (dedicated, identifiable)|
|Effective date|Day before first day of fiscal year (e.g., June 30 for July 1 FY)|
|Structure|One entry, N lines (one per account per site)|
|Amount storage|Debit or credit per line; opposite side = zero|
|Editable|No — read-only, immutable|
|Reversible via UI|No — only via re-import (hard reversal)|
|Reversal date|Same date as original entries|
|Period constraints|Exempt — not subject to open/close rules|
NXT-71240.desc:
As a district finance user, I want to import opening account balances into Financials at the start of a fiscal year or when going live on the system, so that my balance sheet and trial balance reflect accurate starting positions for permanent accounts before any period transactions are recorded.

----

h2. Scope

This story covers the business logic layer for the Opening Balance Import: ledger entry creation, effective date assignment, entry type behavior, amount conversion, hard reversal on re-import, and downstream report behavior. The data exchange layer (companion story) handles file upload, validation, and preview, including the Fiscal Year selection (chosen at the import level, not mapped from the file). This story receives validated data and the selected Fiscal Year after the user confirms the import.

----

h2. Requirements

h3. 1) Ledger Entry Creation

1.1 On receiving validated import data from the data exchange layer, the system must create *one ledger entry* with:

* *Entry type:* "Opening Balance" (or equivalent dedicated type)
* *Effective date:* The day before the first day of the specified fiscal year (see Section 2)
* *Description:* The Comments value from each row (defaulted to "Opening Balance Import" if blank)

1.2 Each row in the import file becomes a *line* within that single ledger entry — one account, one side (debit or credit) per line.

1.3 Each GLAccount + SiteCode combination appears only once (enforced by the data exchange layer).

h3. 2) Effective Date

2.1 The effective date of the opening balance entry must be the *day before the first day of the specified fiscal year* (e.g., if the fiscal year starts July 1, the effective date is June 30).

2.2 This places the opening balance before any fiscal year activity in the ledger, ensuring date-based beginning balance calculations (e.g., {{date < period start}}) work without special-case logic.

2.3 The effective date must be communicated back to the data exchange layer for display in the preview.

2.4 Opening balance entries are *exempt from financial period open/close constraints*. The import must succeed regardless of whether any period is open, closed, or has never been opened.

h3. 3) Amount Conversion

3.1 For *Scheme A* (Debit/Credit): the debit and credit values from the validated data map directly to the ledger entry line. The opposite side is stored as zero.

3.2 For *Scheme B* (signed Amount): positive values are stored as debits, negative values are stored as credits (absolute value). The opposite side is stored as zero.

3.3 The system does *not* enforce normal balance direction by base type. Assets may carry credit balances and liabilities may carry debit balances (abnormal balances are legitimate). Correctness is enforced by the batch-level balance check in the data exchange layer.

h3. 4) Entry Behavior

4.1 Opening balance ledger entries are *read-only*. They cannot be edited or reversed through the UI.

4.2 If the user needs to correct individual accounts after import, they can create manual adjustment entries. The opening balance entry itself is immutable.

4.3 The "Opening Balance" entry type must be identifiable in the ledger so that reports, calculations, and the replacement/reversal logic can distinguish opening balance entries from period transaction activity.

h3. 5) Hard Reversal on Re-Import

5.1 If an opening balance import already exists for the selected fiscal year (detected by the data exchange layer, confirmed by the user), the business logic layer must *reverse all ledger entry lines* from the previous import before writing the new import's entries.

5.2 This is a *hard reversal*, not a soft delete. Reversal entries are written to the ledger to maintain a complete audit trail.

5.3 The reversal entries must be dated the *same date as the original entries* (day before fiscal year start), so that the original and reversal net to zero at the same point in the ledger.

5.4 After reversal, the new import's entry and lines are written per Sections 1–3.

5.5 The entire reversal + re-creation must be *atomic* — if any part fails, no data is changed and the user is notified.

h3. 6) Downstream Report Behavior

6.1 Imported opening balances establish the *fiscal year starting position* for permanent accounts. This is the position as of the first day of the specified fiscal year.

6.2 *Trial Balance:* Imported opening balances must populate the Opening Balance column for the corresponding fiscal year. This column always reflects the fiscal year starting position, regardless of which period the user is viewing.

6.3 *Balance Sheet:* The imported opening balance feeds into the beginning balance calculation. The relationship depends on the reporting period:

* If the reporting period begins on the first day of the fiscal year (e.g., Period 1 or fiscal YTD), the beginning balance equals the imported opening balance.
* If the reporting period begins after the first period (e.g., October in a July fiscal year), the beginning balance equals the imported opening balance plus all cumulative transaction activity from prior periods within that fiscal year.

6.4 Opening balances must be clearly labeled as imported opening balances in all reports and must be visually distinguishable from period transaction activity.

6.5 If no opening balance import exists for a fiscal year, the Opening Balance column in affected reports must display {{$0.00}} with a visual indicator (e.g., icon, highlight, or footnote) communicating that no opening balance has been imported for this fiscal year. The indicator must not interfere with numeric sorting or Excel export behavior — the underlying value must always be zero, not a text string.

NXT-71184.ac:
*AC1 — Landing Page Tile* Given an authorized Finance user opens the Reports section, When the landing page loads, Then a "Trial Balance" tile is displayed with the description "Verify that total debits equal total credits across all accounts for a selected period." and tiles are sorted A–Z.

*AC2 — Configuration Page* Given the user clicks the Trial Balance tile, When the configuration page loads, Then the page title reads "Trial Balance Report," helper text is displayed under the title, and the tooltip about Document Center notification is present.

*AC3 — Default Parameter Values* Given the configuration page loads, When the user has not changed any parameters, Then Fiscal Year defaults to the current fiscal year, Period defaults to the current open period, Period Range toggle is off, and Site defaults to All.

*AC4 — District-Level Rollup* Given the user runs the report with Site = All, When the report renders, Then each GL account row shows values summed across all sites, grouped under base type and account category headers.

*AC5 — Site-Level Filter* Given the user runs the report with a specific Site selected, When the report renders, Then each GL account row shows values for that site only, with the same report structure and hierarchy.

*AC6 — Correct Column Structure* Given the report renders, When the user views any GL account row, Then they can see Year Beginning Balance (Debit/Credit), Net Beginning Balance, Prior Periods (Debit/Credit), Period Activity (Debit/Credit), Ending Balance (Debit/Credit), and Net Ending Balance columns.

*AC7 — Column Math* Given the report renders, When the user views any GL account row, Then the Ending Balance equals (Year Beginning Debit − Year Beginning Credit) + (Prior Periods Debit − Prior Periods Credit) + (Period Activity Debit − Period Activity Credit), displayed in the Ending Balance — Debit column when the result is positive or in the Ending Balance — Credit column when the result is negative, with the opposite column left blank.

*AC8 — Prior Periods in Single Period Mode* Given the district has configured FY 2025–2026 starting September 1 and the user runs the report with Period = November (Period 3), When the report renders, Then Prior Periods columns show total activity from Period 1 (September) through Period 2 (October), and Period Activity shows November only.

*AC9 — Prior Periods in Range Mode* Given the district has configured FY 2025–2026 starting September 1 and the user enables the Period Range toggle with From Period = October (Period 2) and To Period = December (Period 4), When the report renders, Then Prior Periods columns show total activity from Period 1 (September) only, and Period Activity shows October through December.

*AC10 — Prior Periods Zero When Period Is Period 1* Given the user runs the report with Period set to Period 1 of the fiscal year (or From Period = Period 1 in range mode), When the report renders, Then Prior Periods columns show 0.00 for all accounts.

*AC11 — Net Column Calculation* Given the report renders for an Asset account with Year Beginning Debit = 100,000 and Year Beginning Credit = 0, When the user views the Net Beginning Balance column, Then the value is 100,000. And for a Liability account with Year Beginning Debit = 0 and Year Beginning Credit = 50,000, the Net Beginning Balance is −50,000. The formula is always Debit minus Credit regardless of base type.

*AC12 — Base Type Grouping* Given the report renders, When the user views the report, Then GL accounts are grouped under base types in order: Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Revenue, Expenditures, each with a subtotal row.

*AC13 — Account Category Grouping* Given the report renders, When the user views GL accounts within a base type, Then they are grouped under the user-defined account categories, each with a subtotal row.

*AC14 — Beginning Balance Not Set Indicator* Given no opening balance import exists for the selected fiscal year, When the report renders, Then Year Beginning Balance columns show _"Not set"_ rather than 0.00 for permanent accounts.

*AC15 — Revenue and Expenditure Beginning Balance Zero* Given the report renders for any period, When the user views Revenue or Expenditure accounts, Then Year Beginning Balance columns show 0.00 with a clear label indicating these accounts reset at fiscal year start.

*AC16 — Grand Total Balances* Given the report renders for a period with complete data, When the user views the grand total row, Then total debits equal total credits.

*AC17 — Zero Balance Accounts Hidden by Default* Given GL accounts with zero beginning balance, zero period activity, and zero ending balance exist, When the report renders, Then those GL accounts are hidden by default with an option to show them.

*AC18 — Parameter Filters* Given the user applies a Base Type, Account Category, or Account Code Range filter, When the report renders, Then only GL accounts matching the filter criteria are displayed, with subtotals reflecting the filtered data.

*AC19 — Excel Export* Given the report has rendered, When the user exports to Excel, Then the export contains all visible rows and columns as a flat table with subtotals and no empty or merged cells.

*AC20 — PDF Export* Given the report has rendered, When the user exports to PDF, Then the PDF reflects the currently visible state of the report.

----

h2. Edge / Empty / Error States

||Scenario||Behavior||
|No transactions exist for selected period range|Report renders with zero Period Activity; beginning and ending balances shown if available|
|No opening balance import for fiscal year|Year Beginning Balance columns show _"Not set"_ with visual indicator|
|Period has not yet closed|Report renders with available data; indicator shown if period is still open|
|GL account has no account category assigned|GL account appears under the base type's default category|
|From Period is after To Period (range mode)|Validation error; report does not run|
|Account Code Range returns no accounts|Report renders empty with a message indicating no accounts match the filter|

----

h2. Notes

* *Oracle alignment:* The column structure and parameters are modeled after Oracle's General Ledger Trial Balance Report to ensure familiarity for districts exporting to Oracle.
NXT-71184.desc:
{quote}*For QA context:* A trial balance lists _every_ GL account with its debit and credit balances to verify that total debits equal total credits; a balance sheet only shows Assets, Liabilities, and Equity as a financial snapshot — no individual accounts, no Revenue or Expenditure detail, and no debit/credit columns.{quote}

h2. User Story

As a district finance user, I want to run a trial balance report that shows year beginning balances, prior period activity, current period activity, and ending balances for each GL account, organized by base type and account category, so that I can verify my books balance and assess the district's financial position at any point in time.

----

h2. Context

The Financials 2.0 trial balance is designed around the job to be done: _verify my books balance and assess the district's financial position at any point in time, in a way that aligns with standard accounting practice._

The report is modeled after Oracle's General Ledger Trial Balance Report to ensure familiarity for districts that export to Oracle and to align with industry-standard reporting conventions.

The key design principles:

* *Structured hierarchy* — GL accounts are organized under base types and user-defined account categories, with subtotals at each level and a balancing grand total, giving users an immediate read on financial health.
* *Site as a filter, not a drilldown* — the user runs the report at the district level or for a specific site. Same report, same structure, different data scope.
* *Period-based with range support* — the report ties to fiscal year and period as configured in Periods. Beginning balances come from the Opening Balance Import, which is faster, auditable, and consistent with how finance teams actually close their books.
* *Oracle-aligned column structure* — year beginning balances, net beginning balance, prior periods, period activity, ending balances, and net ending balance — so the math is fully traceable: Year Beginning + Prior Periods + Period Activity = Ending Balance.
* *Visibility of all accounts* — GL accounts with a non-zero beginning or ending balance appear even if they have zero period activity, so nothing is silently hidden.

The report will be built using *Power BI Report Builder* (paginated reports), which natively supports row group hierarchies with toggle visibility.

----

h2. Requirements

h3. 1) Reports Landing Page Integration

The Trial Balance report must be added to the existing Reports landing page as a tile.

1.1 The Reports landing page must be updated to display the following tiles (sorted A–Z):

||Report Name||Description||
|General Ledger|Export detailed debits and credits with per-account worksheets.|
|Journal|Export a list of all financial postings in transaction order for a selected date range.|
|Revenue & Expenditure (P&L)|View revenue and expense activity summarized by category for a selected period.|
|Trial Balance|Verify that total debits equal total credits across all accounts for a selected period.|

1.2 Each tile must include the report name and the description stated above. The entire tile is clickable and opens the report's configuration page.

1.3 Hover state uses elevated shadow. Click leads to the specific report's configuration screen.

h3. 2) Report Configuration Page

2.1 Page title: *"Trial Balance Report"*

2.2 Helper text directly under title: _"Choose a fiscal year and reporting period to verify that total debits equal total credits across all accounts. Use the period range toggle to view activity across multiple periods."_

2.3 Tooltip: _"You'll be notified once your file is ready and can download it from the Document Center."_

h3. 3) Report Parameters

3.1 The user must be able to specify:

||Parameter||Notes||
|Fiscal Year|Required; defaults to the current fiscal year as configured in Periods|
|Period|Required; single period selection. Defaults to the current open period.|
|Period Range toggle|Optional; when enabled, replaces the single Period selector with From Period / To Period. Defaults to off.|
|From Period|Visible only when Period Range is enabled. Required; defaults to Period 1 of the fiscal year.|
|To Period|Visible only when Period Range is enabled. Required; defaults to the current open period.|
|Site|Optional; defaults to All (district-level rollup). Single selection.|
|Base Type|Optional filter; defaults to All. Allows selection of a specific base type (e.g., Assets only)|
|Account Category|Optional filter; defaults to All. Filters to GL accounts within a specific user-defined account category|
|Account Code Range (From / To)|Optional; filters to a specific range of GL account codes|

3.2 When Site is set to All, the report shows GL account balances summed across all sites (district-level rollup).

3.3 When a specific Site is selected, the report shows GL account balances for that site only. The report structure and hierarchy remain the same.

3.4 *Single period mode (default):* When the user selects a single Period, the report treats it as From Period = To Period. See section 6.6 for how each column group is calculated.

3.5 *Period range mode:* When the user enables the Period Range toggle and selects From Period / To Period, the report covers a multi-period span. See section 6.6 for how each column group is calculated.

3.6 When the selected period (or From Period in range mode) is Period 1 of the fiscal year, the Prior Periods columns show 0.00 (there are no prior periods).

h3. 4) Outcome

4.1 A district finance user can run a trial balance report that shows all GL accounts with year beginning balance, prior period activity, period activity, and ending balance in a single view.

4.2 The report covers all five base types: Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Revenue, and Expenditures.

4.3 Total debits must equal total credits at the grand total level — this is the primary proof that the books balance.

h3. 5) Report Structure and Hierarchy

The report hierarchy has three levels:

*Base Type → Account Category → GL Account*

* *Base Type* is system-defined: Assets, Liabilities, Equity / Fund Balance, Revenue, Expenditures.
* *Account Category* is user-defined: groupings of similar GL accounts created by the district (e.g., within Assets a district might create Current Assets, Investments, Receivables, Fixed Assets).
* *GL Account* is the individual account (e.g., Cash on Deposit - Banks, Inventory of Food).

5.1 The report must be organized by base type in the following order:

# Assets
# Liabilities
# Equity / Fund Balance
# Revenue
# Expenditures

5.2 Within each base type, GL accounts are grouped under the user-defined account categories.

5.3 *Debits and credits are kept separate at every level of the report — they are never netted against each other in the debit/credit column pairs.* All subtotal and grand total rows independently sum the debit column and the credit column. The only place debits and credits are _compared_ is the grand total row, where they must be equal as a proof that the books balance. Net columns (Net Beginning Balance, Net Ending Balance) are provided as a convenience alongside the debit/credit pairs, consistent with Oracle's GL Trial Balance Report.

5.4 Each account category must display a subtotal row (sum of debits, sum of credits — not netted).

5.5 Each base type section must display a subtotal row (sum of debits, sum of credits — not netted).

5.6 The report must display a grand total row at the bottom showing total debits and total credits across all base types. These two totals must be equal for a balanced set of books.

5.7 Each level of the hierarchy — base type, account category, GL account — must be independently expandable and collapsible.

5.8 The user can expand all or collapse all with a single control.

h3. 6) Column Structure

||Column||Description||
|Account Code|GL account code (e.g., {{5.1010.000.000}})|
|Account Description|GL account name (e.g., "Cash on Deposit - Banks")|
|Year Beginning Balance — Debit|Opening debit balance as of the fiscal year start date configured in Periods|
|Year Beginning Balance — Credit|Opening credit balance as of the fiscal year start date configured in Periods|
|Net Beginning Balance|Year Beginning Debit minus Year Beginning Credit (single column; see 6.2)|
|Prior Periods — Debit|Total debit activity from Period 1 through the period immediately before the selected period (or before From Period in range mode)|
|Prior Periods — Credit|Total credit activity from Period 1 through the period immediately before the selected period (or before From Period in range mode)|
|Period Activity — Debit|Total debit activity during the selected period (or From Period to To Period in range mode)|
|Period Activity — Credit|Total credit activity during the selected period (or From Period to To Period in range mode)|
|Ending Balance — Debit|Net ending balance, populated only for GL accounts with a net debit position as of the end of the selected period (or To Period in range mode)|
|Ending Balance — Credit|Net ending balance, populated only for GL accounts with a net credit position as of the end of the selected period (or To Period in range mode)|
|Net Ending Balance|Ending Debit minus Ending Credit (single column; see 6.2)|

6.1 The ending balance for each GL account is calculated as a single net value and displayed on its natural side:

*Ending Balance = (Year Beginning Debit − Year Beginning Credit) + (Prior Periods Debit − Prior Periods Credit) + (Period Activity Debit − Period Activity Credit)*

* If the result is positive, the amount is displayed in the Ending Balance — Debit column; Ending Balance — Credit is blank.
* If the result is negative, the absolute value is displayed in the Ending Balance — Credit column; Ending Balance — Debit is blank.
* If the result is zero, both columns show 0.00 (subject to the zero-balance hide rule in 7.4).

This matches Oracle's convention of printing one side per account for beginning and ending balances.

6.2 *Net columns are always calculated as Debit minus Credit, regardless of base type.* Net Beginning Balance = Year Beginning Debit − Year Beginning Credit. Net Ending Balance = Ending Debit − Ending Credit. The sign of the result indicates the natural side of the balance: positive means a net debit balance (typical for Assets and Expenditures), negative means a net credit balance (typical for Liabilities, Equity, and Revenue). The formula does not change per base type — the sign itself conveys which side the balance sits on.

6.3 Year Beginning Balance columns must be populated from the Opening Balance Import for the fiscal year. The year beginning balance represents the financial position as of the fiscal year start date configured in Periods. If no opening balance import exists, the Year Beginning Balance columns display _"Not set"_ with a visual indicator rather than zero.

6.4 Revenue and Expenditure accounts always show 0.00 in Year Beginning Balance columns, as these accounts reset at fiscal year start. This must be clearly indicated in the UI to avoid user confusion.

6.5 When the selected period (or From Period in range mode) is Period 1 of the fiscal year, Prior Periods columns show 0.00 for all accounts.

h3. 6.6) Column Calculations by Date Range Selection

The fiscal year start date, periods, and their date ranges are defined by the district in the Periods configuration. The examples below assume a district has configured FY 2025–2026 to start September 1, 2025 with 12 monthly periods (Period 1 = September, Period 2 = October, etc.).

*Example A — Single period mode, Period = November (Period 3):*

||Column Group||What it contains||
|Year Beginning Balance|Opening Balance Import — the district's balances as of the fiscal year start date (September 1, 2025)|
|Prior Periods|All activity from Period 1 (September) through Period 2 (October)|
|Period Activity|Activity for Period 3 (November) only|
|Ending Balance|Year Beginning + Prior Periods + Period Activity — the balance as of the end of November|

*Example B — Range mode, From Period = October (Period 2), To Period = December (Period 4):*

||Column Group||What it contains||
|Year Beginning Balance|Opening Balance Import — the district's balances as of the fiscal year start date (September 1, 2025)|
|Prior Periods|All activity from Period 1 (September) only — everything before From Period|
|Period Activity|Activity from Period 2 (October) through Period 4 (December)|
|Ending Balance|Year Beginning + Prior Periods + Period Activity — the balance as of the end of December|

*Example C — Range mode, From Period = September (Period 1), To Period = December (Period 4):*

||Column Group||What it contains||
|Year Beginning Balance|Opening Balance Import — the district's balances as of the fiscal year start date (September 1, 2025)|
|Prior Periods|0.00 — there are no periods before Period 1|
|Period Activity|Activity from Period 1 (September) through Period 4 (December)|
|Ending Balance|Year Beginning + Period Activity — the balance as of the end of December|

h3. 7) Display and Formatting

7.1 All currency values must be formatted consistently with two decimal places.

7.2 Negative values must be clearly distinguishable (e.g., parentheses or color).

7.3 GL accounts with zero activity in the selected period range must still appear in the report if they have a non-zero beginning or ending balance.

7.4 GL accounts with zero beginning balance, zero period activity, and zero ending balance may be hidden by default with an option to show them.

7.5 The report header must display: District Name, Report Title, Fiscal Year, Period (or From Period / To Period if range mode), Site (or "All Sites"), and the date and time the report was generated.

h3. 8) Export

8.1 The user can export the report to Excel (XLSX) and PDF.

8.2 The Excel export must produce a flat table of the currently visible data with all subtotals included. No row grouping outlines, no merged cells, no empty cells. Ready for immediate use without cleanup.

8.3 The PDF export must render the currently visible level of the hierarchy.

NXT-71182.ac:
h3. Parameters Page

*AC1 — Report Accessible* Given an authorized Finance user opens the Reports section, When they navigate to Balance Sheet, Then the report parameters page is displayed with the title "Balance Sheet Report" and helper text "Choose a fiscal year, reporting period, and sites. When multiple sites are selected, each site gets its own Balance Sheet worksheet in the Excel file."

*AC2 — Export Tooltip* Given the user hovers over the generate/export button, When the tooltip appears, Then it reads "You'll be notified once your file is ready and can download it from the Document Center."

*AC3 — Reporting Period Options* Given the user clicks the Reporting Period dropdown, When the dropdown opens, Then it displays four options: Single month, Quarter, Fiscal YTD, and Custom date range.

*AC4 — Single Month Resolves Correctly* Given the user selects Single month and picks a month, When the report generates, Then the date range spans the first day through the last day of that month.

*AC5 — Quarter Resolves Correctly* Given the user selects Quarter and picks a quarter, When the report generates, Then the date range spans the first day of the quarter's first month through the last day of the quarter's last month.

*AC6 — Fiscal YTD Resolves Correctly* Given the user selects Fiscal YTD, When the report generates, Then the date range spans the first day of the fiscal year through the last day of the most recent closed period (or today if no periods are closed).

*AC7 — Custom Date Range* Given the user selects Custom date range and enters a start and end date, When the report generates, Then the date range matches exactly what the user entered.

*AC8 — Site Type Defaults to All and Supports Multiselect* Given the user opens the report parameters, When the Site Type dropdown loads, Then "All" is selected by default and the user can select multiple types.

*AC9 — Site Defaults to All and Supports Multiselect* Given the user opens the report parameters, When the Site dropdown loads, Then "All" is selected by default and the user can select multiple sites.

*AC10 — Site Dropdown Cascades from Site Type* Given the user selects one or more Site Types (e.g., Elementary + Middle School), When the Site dropdown refreshes, Then it shows only sites belonging to the selected types plus the "All" option.

*AC11 — Site Deselects on Site Type Change* Given the user has selected specific sites, When they change Site Type such that one or more selected sites no longer belong to the selected type(s), Then those sites are deselected; if no sites remain selected, Site resets to "All".

*AC12 — Selecting All Clears Individual Selections* Given the user has selected specific Site Types or Sites, When they select "All", Then all individual selections are cleared and "All" becomes the only selection.

*AC13 — Selecting a Specific Value Clears All* Given "All" is currently selected in Site Type or Site, When the user selects a specific value, Then "All" is automatically deselected.

h3. Report Content

*AC14 — Site Type and Site Filter Scopes Data* Given the user selects one or more Site Types and/or specific Sites, When the report generates, Then all values (Beginning Balance, Period Activity, Ending Balance, Net Income) reflect only ledger entries from the matching sites.

*AC15 — Correct Section Structure* Given the report generates, When the user views the report, Then it displays Assets, Liabilities, and Equity sections in order with category groups, category subtotals, section totals, and a Net Income line within Equity.

*AC16 — Category Groups Expandable* Given the report renders with category groups collapsed, When the user expands a category group, Then individual account rows within that category are revealed with the category subtotal row visible.

*AC17 — Category Subtotals Correct* Given a category group contains multiple accounts, When the user views the category subtotal row, Then each column (Beginning Balance, Period Activity, Ending Balance) equals the sum of the individual account values in that category.

*AC18 — Uncategorized Accounts in Other Group* Given an account does not have a category assigned, When the report generates, Then the account appears under an "Other" group at the bottom of its section with the same expandable and subtotal behavior.

*AC19 — Expanded Equation Satisfied* Given the report generates for a period with complete and correct data, When the user views the grand totals, Then Total Assets = Total Liabilities + Total Equity + Net Income.

*AC20 — Out of Balance Warning* Given Total Assets does not equal Total Liabilities and Equity, When the report generates, Then a prominent warning is displayed showing the out-of-balance amount.

*AC21 — Revenue and Expenditure Not Shown as Line Items* Given the report generates, When the user views the report, Then no individual Revenue or Expenditure accounts appear as rows; their net effect appears only as the Net Income line within the Equity section.

*AC22 — Net Income Correct* Given the report generates for any reporting period within a fiscal year, When the user views the Equity section, Then Net Income = (Revenue Credits − Revenue Debits) − (Expenditure Debits − Expenditure Credits), scoped from the fiscal year start date (of the selected Fiscal Year) through the report end date inclusive.

*AC23 — Period Activity Sign Convention* Given the report generates, When the user views Period Activity for any account, Then Asset accounts show Debits − Credits, and Liability and Equity accounts show Credits − Debits.

*AC24 — Beginning Balance Period-Relative* Given the user selects any reporting period, When the report generates, Then Beginning Balance reflects the cumulative ledger position of each account as of the day before the report start date.

*AC25 — Ending Balance Correct* Given the report generates, When the user views any account row, Then Ending Balance = Beginning Balance + Period Activity.

*AC26 — Total Equity Includes Net Income* Given the report generates, When the user views Total Equity, Then Total Equity = Sum of all Equity account ending balances + Net Income.

*AC27 — Negative Values in Parentheses* Given an account has a balance opposite to its normal balance, When the report generates, Then the value is displayed in parentheses.

*AC28 — Report Header Shows Filters* Given the user has selected specific Site Type(s) and/or Site(s), When the report generates, Then the report header displays the selected filter values alongside the other header fields.

h3. Export and Document Center

*AC29 — Async Generation* Given the user clicks the generate/export button, When the report is submitted, Then the report is queued for processing and the user is notified when it is ready in the Document Center.

*AC30 — Excel Single Site or All* Given the user selects "All" sites or a single site and exports to Excel, When the file is generated, Then the Excel file contains one worksheet with the full balance sheet.

*AC31 — Excel Multiple Sites* Given the user selects multiple specific sites and exports to Excel, When the file is generated, Then the Excel file contains one worksheet per site, each named with the site name, each containing a complete balance sheet scoped to that site's data.

*AC32 — Excel via Site Type* Given the user selects a Site Type (e.g., Elementary) with Site left as "All" and exports to Excel, When the file is generated, Then the Excel file contains one worksheet per site within that type.

*AC33 — Excel Formatting* Given the Excel file is generated, When the user opens any worksheet, Then it contains all sections, category subtotals, section totals, column values, report header, and correct number formatting.

*AC34 — PDF Single Site or All* Given the user selects "All" sites or a single site and exports to PDF, When the file is generated, Then the PDF contains one balance sheet.

*AC35 — PDF Multiple Sites* Given the user selects multiple specific sites and exports to PDF, When the file is generated, Then the PDF contains one balance sheet per site with page breaks between sites.

----

h2. Edge / Empty / Error States

||Scenario||Behavior||
|No transactions for selected date range|Report generates with zero Period Activity; beginning and ending balances shown based on prior cumulative ledger activity|
|No ledger entries exist at all|Report generates with all zeros; no error|
|Balance sheet does not balance|Prominent out-of-balance warning displayed with the difference amount|
|Negative Net Income|Displayed in parentheses within the Equity section; Total Equity is reduced accordingly|
|Single site district|Site Type and Site filters available but effectively show one site; Excel export has one worksheet|
|Period not yet closed|Report generates with available data; indicator shown that the period is still open|
|Custom date range crosses fiscal years|Report runs using the entered dates; Beginning Balance reflects all activity prior to the start date regardless of fiscal year|
|Custom date range start date equals end date|Report runs for a single day; Beginning Balance reflects all activity prior to that day|
|Account category has no accounts with activity|Category group is hidden if all accounts have zero balances (per 5.4); visible if "show zero balances" is enabled|
|All accounts in a section are uncategorized|The "Other" group is the only group in that section; section total equals the "Other" subtotal|
|Selected Site Type(s) have no sites with ledger entries|Report generates with all zeros for the selected scope; no error|
|Site Type changed after specific Sites were selected|Sites not belonging to the new type(s) are deselected; if none remain, Site resets to "All"|
|Multiple Site Types selected|Site dropdown shows the union of all sites belonging to any of the selected types|
|50+ sites selected|Excel file generates with 50+ worksheets; Document Center handles async delivery|

----

h2. Notes

* This report covers Assets, Liabilities, and Equity only. For Revenue and Expenditure detail, see the P&L report.
* Accounts are grouped first by section (Asset, Liability, Equity), then by their account category as defined in the chart of accounts. The system does not require the user to manually configure which accounts belong to which category group on this report — it reads from the chart of accounts.
* The balance sheet is a net-amount report — not split into debit/credit columns — consistent with standard financial statement presentation.
* The report is built using Power BI Report Builder (paginated reports). Category grouping uses row groups with toggle visibility for expand/collapse behavior.
* Site-level breakdown with drilldown is handled in the Trial Balance report, not here.
* The multi-site worksheet pattern (one worksheet per site) is consistent with the P&L report export behavior.
NXT-71182.desc:
As a district finance user, I want to run a balance sheet report that shows the district's assets, liabilities, and equity/fund balance for a selected period, with beginning balances, period activity, and ending balances clearly presented, so that I can confirm the financial position of the nutrition fund and reconcile with my bank statement.

{quote}*A sample report is attached with this story for visual reference.*{quote}

----

h2. Context

The balance sheet is a formal financial statement that presents permanent accounts (Assets, Liabilities, Equity) alongside the net effect of temporary accounts (Revenue and Expenditures) in a structured, stakeholder-readable format.

The report is governed by the *Expanded Accounting Equation*:

{noformat}Assets = Liabilities + Equity + Revenue − Expenditures{noformat}

Which can also be expressed as:

{noformat}Assets = Liabilities + Equity + Net Income{noformat}

Where:

||Term||Definition||
|*Assets*|What the district owns (Cash, Receivables, Inventory, Fixed Assets, etc.)|
|*Liabilities*|What the district owes (Accounts Payable, Accrued Liabilities, Unearned Income, etc.)|
|*Equity*|Fund Balance accounts — the district's accumulated net position from prior periods|
|*Revenue*|Income earned during the selected period (meal sales, reimbursements, etc.)|
|*Expenditures*|Costs incurred during the selected period (food, labor, supplies, etc.)|
|*Net Income*|Revenue − Expenditures from the fiscal year start date through the report end date|

Revenue and Expenditure accounts do not appear as individual line items on the balance sheet. Their net effect flows into the Equity section as a single calculated line: *Net Income*.

The report can be built and run regardless of whether an opening balance import has been performed. All values are derived from ledger entries for the selected date range.

----

h2. Requirements

h3. 1) Outcome

1.1 A district finance user can run a balance sheet report showing Assets, Liabilities, and Equity/Fund Balance for a selected period.

1.2 The report shows beginning balance, period activity, and ending balance for each account so the user can trace movement during the period.

1.3 The report satisfies the expanded accounting equation at the ending balance level:

{noformat}Total Assets = Total Liabilities + Total Equity + Net Income{noformat}

If this equation does not balance, the report must display a prominent warning.

h3. 2) Report Parameters Page

h4. 2.1 — Page Title and Guidance

* *Page title:* "Balance Sheet Report"
* *Helper text* (directly under title): "Choose a fiscal year, reporting period, and sites. When multiple sites are selected, each site gets its own Balance Sheet worksheet in the Excel file."
* *Tooltip* (on the export/generate button): "You'll be notified once your file is ready and can download it from the Document Center."

h4. 2.2 — Parameters

||Parameter||Required||Default||Selection||Notes||
|Fiscal Year|Yes|—|Single select|Dropdown of available fiscal years|
|Reporting Period|Yes|—|Single select|Determines the date range for the report; see 2.3|
|Site Type|No|All|Multiselect|Filters which sites are included; see 2.4|
|Site|No|All|Multiselect|Filters to specific sites within the selected Site Type(s); see 2.4|

h4. 2.3 — Reporting Period Options

The Reporting Period dropdown offers four options. Each option resolves to a specific start date and end date that defines the report's date range.

||Option||Additional Input Required||Start Date||End Date||
|*Single month*|Month selector|First day of the selected month|Last day of the selected month|
|*Quarter*|Quarter selector (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4)|First day of the quarter's first month|Last day of the quarter's last month|
|*Fiscal YTD*|None|First day of the selected fiscal year|Last day of the most recent closed period, or today if no periods are closed|
|*Custom date range*|Start date and end date pickers|User-selected start date|User-selected end date|

All calculations throughout this report (Beginning Balance, Period Activity, Ending Balance, Net Income) use the resolved *start date* and *end date* from the selected Reporting Period option.

* *Beginning Balance* = cumulative ledger position as of the day before the start date
* *Period Activity* = net change from start date through end date (inclusive)
* *Ending Balance* = Beginning Balance + Period Activity
* *Net Income* = Revenue − Expenditures from start date through end date (inclusive)

h4. 2.4 — Site Type and Site (Cascading Multiselect Filters)

Site Type and Site are both *multiselect* dropdowns that operate as a *cascading filter pair*. Site Type narrows the pool of available sites; Site optionally selects specific sites within that pool.

*Filter behavior:*

||Site Type||Site||Report Scope||
|All (default)|All (default)|All sites in the district — full district rollup|
|All|One or more specific sites selected|Only the selected sites|
|One or more types selected (e.g., Elementary + Middle School)|All|All sites belonging to any of the selected types|
|One or more types selected|One or more specific sites selected|Only the selected sites (which must belong to the selected types)|

*Cascading logic:*

* When the user changes the Site Type selection, the Site dropdown refreshes to show only sites that belong to the selected type(s), plus the "All" option.
* When Site Type is "All", the Site dropdown shows all sites in the district.
* If specific sites were previously selected and the user changes Site Type such that one or more of those sites no longer belong to the selected type(s), those sites are deselected. If no sites remain selected, Site resets to "All".
* The user can select multiple Site Types (e.g., Elementary + Middle School) and then further narrow to specific sites within those types.

*"All" behavior:*

* "All" is the default for both dropdowns.
* Selecting "All" in Site Type clears any previously selected types and shows all sites in the Site dropdown.
* Selecting "All" in Site clears any previously selected individual sites.
* Selecting a specific value when "All" is currently selected automatically deselects "All".

h3. 3) Report Structure

3.1 The report lists accounts grouped into three top-level sections in order:

*Assets*

* Account category groups (expandable), each with a subtotal row
* *Total Assets*

*Liabilities*

* Account category groups (expandable), each with a subtotal row
* *Total Liabilities*

*Equity / Fund Balance*

* Account category groups (expandable), each with a subtotal row
* *Net Income* — a calculated line (not a GL account); see formula in Section 4
* *Total Equity* — sum of all Equity account ending balances + Net Income

*Total Liabilities and Equity* = Total Liabilities + Total Equity

h4. 3.2 — Account Category Grouping

Within each top-level section (Assets, Liabilities, Equity), accounts are organized into *account category groups* based on the category assigned to each account in the chart of accounts (e.g., Current Assets, Fixed Assets, Current Liabilities, Long-Term Liabilities, etc.).

Each category group:

* Is *expandable/collapsible* — collapsed by default, showing only the category name and its subtotal row
* When expanded, reveals the individual account rows belonging to that category
* Displays a *subtotal row* showing the sum of Beginning Balance, Period Activity, and Ending Balance for all accounts in the category

Example when expanded:

{noformat}Assets
  ▼ Current Assets
      1010  Cash                          10,000.00    2,000.00    12,000.00
      1020  Accounts Receivable            5,000.00    1,000.00     6,000.00
      Total Current Assets               15,000.00    3,000.00    18,000.00
  ▼ Fixed Assets
      1510  Equipment                     25,000.00        0.00    25,000.00
      Total Fixed Assets                  25,000.00        0.00    25,000.00
  Total Assets                            40,000.00    3,000.00    43,000.00{noformat}

h4. 3.3 — Accounts Without a Category

If an account does not have a category assigned in the chart of accounts, it appears under an *"Other"* group at the bottom of its section (e.g., "Other Assets", "Other Liabilities", "Other Equity"). This group behaves identically to any other category group — expandable with a subtotal row.

h4. 3.4 — Balance Check

The report must satisfy this check at the ending balance level:

{noformat}Total Assets = Total Liabilities and Equity{noformat}

If they are not equal, a prominent warning is displayed showing the difference.

h4. 3.5 — Net Income Placement

Revenue and Expenditure accounts do not appear as individual line items. Their net effect, cumulative from the fiscal year start date through the report end date, is:

{noformat}Net Income = (Revenue Credits − Revenue Debits) − (Expenditure Debits − Expenditure Credits){noformat}

This value appears as a single line within the Equity section, below all Equity category groups and above Total Equity. Net Income is not part of any category group.

h3. 4) Column Structure

||Column||Description||Formula||
|Account Code|GL account code|—|
|Account Description|Account name|—|
|Beginning Balance|Cumulative ledger position as of the day before the report start date|See 4.2|
|Period Activity|Net change from report start date through report end date|See 4.3|
|Ending Balance|Position as of the report end date|Beginning Balance + Period Activity|

h4. 4.1 — Balance Presentation

The balance sheet expresses values as net amounts per account — not split into separate debit and credit columns.

h4. 4.2 — Beginning Balance

Beginning Balance is the cumulative net position of the account from all ledger entries *prior to* the report start date.

||Account Type||Beginning Balance Formula||
|Assets|Sum of all Debits − Sum of all Credits for all entries before the report start date|
|Liabilities|Sum of all Credits − Sum of all Debits for all entries before the report start date|
|Equity / Fund Balance|Sum of all Credits − Sum of all Debits for all entries before the report start date|

When Site Type(s) or specific Site(s) are selected, Beginning Balance is scoped to ledger entries for the matching sites only.

A positive Beginning Balance means the account carries its expected (normal) balance. A negative Beginning Balance means the account carries a balance opposite to its normal balance.

h4. 4.3 — Period Activity

Period Activity is the net change in the account from the report start date through the report end date (inclusive).

||Account Type||Normal Balance||Period Activity Formula||Positive Value Means||
|Assets|Debit|Period Debits − Period Credits|Asset increased|
|Liabilities|Credit|Period Credits − Period Debits|Liability increased|
|Equity / Fund Balance|Credit|Period Credits − Period Debits|Equity increased|

When Site Type(s) or specific Site(s) are selected, Period Activity is scoped to ledger entries for the matching sites only.

h4. 4.4 — Ending Balance

{noformat}Ending Balance = Beginning Balance + Period Activity{noformat}

This applies to every account row individually.

h4. 4.5 — Net Income (calculated line)

Net Income is not a GL account. It is a calculated value that appears in the Equity section.

{noformat}Net Income = (Revenue Credits − Revenue Debits) − (Expenditure Debits − Expenditure Credits){noformat}

Scoped from the fiscal year start date (of the selected Fiscal Year) through the report end date, inclusive. This captures the unclosed result of Revenue and Expenditure activity — the portion that has accumulated inside Asset and Liability account balances but has not yet been swept into Fund Balance by Year End Close.

Where:

* *Revenue Credits* / *Revenue Debits* = Sum of all credit / debit entries posted to Revenue accounts from the fiscal year start date through the report end date
* *Expenditure Debits* / *Expenditure Credits* = Sum of all debit / credit entries posted to Expenditure accounts from the fiscal year start date through the report end date

When Site Type(s) or specific Site(s) are selected, Net Income is scoped to ledger entries for the matching sites only.

A positive Net Income means revenues exceeded expenditures. A negative Net Income (displayed in parentheses) means expenditures exceeded revenues.

h4. 4.6 — Category Subtotals

Each account category group displays a subtotal row:

{noformat}Category Subtotal (Beginning Balance) = Sum of Beginning Balances for all accounts in the category
Category Subtotal (Period Activity)   = Sum of Period Activity for all accounts in the category
Category Subtotal (Ending Balance)    = Sum of Ending Balances for all accounts in the category{noformat}

h4. 4.7 — Section Totals

{noformat}Total Assets         = Sum of Ending Balances for all Asset accounts
Total Liabilities    = Sum of Ending Balances for all Liability accounts
Total Equity         = Sum of Ending Balances for all Equity accounts + Net Income{noformat}

Note: Section totals must equal the sum of their category subtotals. Total Assets = Sum of all Asset category subtotal ending balances.

h4. 4.8 — Grand Total and Balance Check

{noformat}Total Liabilities and Equity = Total Liabilities + Total Equity{noformat}

Balance check:

{noformat}Total Assets = Total Liabilities and Equity{noformat}

If this check fails, display a warning with the difference:

{noformat}Out of Balance Amount = Total Assets − Total Liabilities and Equity{noformat}

h4. 4.9 — Negative Value Display

All negative values must be displayed in parentheses. Example: $(1,234.56)

h3. 5) Display and Formatting

5.1 All currency values must be formatted with two decimal places and thousands separators. Example: $12,345.67

5.2 The report header (on the rendered report and on each export page/worksheet) must display: District Name, Report Title, Fiscal Year, Reporting Period (with resolved date range), Site Type(s) (if not "All"), Site(s) (if not "All"), and the date and time the report was generated.

5.3 If the balance sheet does not balance, a prominent warning must appear at the top of the report showing the out-of-balance amount.

5.4 Accounts with zero beginning balance, zero period activity, and zero ending balance may be hidden by default with an option to show them.

5.5 Account category groups are collapsed by default. The user can expand any group to see its individual accounts.

h3. 6) Export

h4. 6.1 — General

Report generation is *asynchronous*. When the user clicks the generate/export button, the report is queued for processing. The user is notified once the file is ready and can download it from the *Document Center*.

h4. 6.2 — Excel (XLSX) Export

6.2.1 When *"All" sites or a single site* is selected, the Excel file contains one worksheet with the full balance sheet for that scope.

6.2.2 When *multiple specific sites* are selected, each site gets its own worksheet within the same Excel file. Each worksheet:

* Is named with the site name
* Contains a complete balance sheet for that site (same section structure, category groups, subtotals, and formulas — all scoped to that site's ledger entries)
* Includes the report header with the site name

6.2.3 When multiple sites are selected via Site Type (e.g., "All Elementary sites"), each individual site within that type gets its own worksheet — the same as selecting those sites individually.

6.2.4 All worksheets must preserve the section groupings, category subtotals, and all column values with correct number formatting.

h4. 6.3 — PDF Export

6.3.1 When *"All" sites or a single site* is selected, the PDF contains one balance sheet for that scope.

6.3.2 When *multiple specific sites* are selected, each site gets its own page (or page group) within the same PDF, with a page break between sites.

6.3.3 The PDF must render in standard balance sheet format suitable for sharing with administrators or auditors.

NXT-70909.summary: Update Remaining Legacy “Prepaid Account” Rules After POS/Online Liability Split
NXT-70909.ac:
h3. Rule 1 — Returned Checks (POS-only; Automated First Leg)

||Field||Value||
|*Name*|Returned Check – POS Liability|
|*Backend Code*|{{RTNCHCKPOS}}|
|*Rule*|Debit POS Prepaid Liability; Credit Returned Checks (Liability)|

Checks are physical instruments — POS only, no online check flow. The original deposit credited POS Prepaid Liability, so the reversal debits the same account.

{quote}*Trigger:* The returned check reversal is logged by a district user in the back-office account management module, not at the POS terminal. The engineering team should attach the system listener to account management, not expect a POS transaction payload.{quote}

{quote}*Second leg:* Debit Returned Checks Liability → Credit Cash in Bank is a manual journal entry, scoped to the manual/template entries story.{quote}

----

h3. Rule 2 — Sales Tax on Prepaid Consumption (POS-only)

||Field||Value||
|*Name*|Sales Tax on Prepaid Payment|
|*Backend Code*|{{TAXCLCTDPP}}|
|*Rule*|Debit POS Prepaid Liability; Credit Sales Tax Owed|

Sales tax is collected only at POS (confirmed in refinement). Always debits POS Prepaid Liability. If this contributes to a negative balance, the reclassification in Rule 4 corrects it.

{quote}Previously proposed {{TAXCLCTDPPONL}} (Online) rule removed — no online sales tax flow exists.{quote}

----

h3. Rule 3 — Account Adjustments (Manual; User Selects Data Point)

||Field||Value||
|*Name (POS)*|Account Adjustment – POS Liability|
|*Backend Code (POS)*|{{ACCTADJPOS}}|
|*Name (Online)*|Account Adjustment – Online Liability|
|*Backend Code (Online)*|{{ACCTADJONL}}|
|*Rule*|Debit Account Adjustments (Expense); Credit POS Prepaid Liability *or* Online Prepaid Liability|

Manual entries — the user selects which data point.

* *Positive adjustment* (adding funds): Debit Account Adjustments (Expense); Credit the selected Prepaid Liability data point.
* *Negative adjustment* (reducing funds): Debit the selected Prepaid Liability data point; Credit Account Adjustments (Expense). Accounts reverse; all amounts are positive values.

Note: Manual adjustments affect the ending ledger balance that the reclassification reads. For example, if a CN Director manually reduces Online Prepaid Liability by $5,000 in December, January's reclassification will see that reduced balance.

----

h3. Rule 4 — Meal Sales with Period-End Reclassification

||Field||Value||
|*Name*|Meal Sale|
|*Backend Code*|{{MEALSLS}}|
|*Reclassification Code*|{{PREPLIAB-RECLASS}}|

*Step 1 — Meal Sales Entry (stateless, per transaction aggregation):*

* Debit *Cash in Bank* — cash-paid portion (as reported by POS; never modified by Financial Oversight)
* Debit *POS Prepaid Liability* — full prepaid portion used (not split by funding source)
* Credit *Meal Sales Revenue* — total excluding sales tax
* Unused debit components may be $0

*Step 2 — Reclassification (system-generated, runs once per posting level per period):*

After all deposit rules (NXT-70340) and all consumption rules (Rules 1, 2, and Step 1 above) have completed for a posting level and period:

# Read the *ending ledger balance* of POS Prepaid Liability at that posting level. This is the cumulative balance including all prior periods, all automated postings in the current period, and any manual adjustments (Rule 3) already posted. It is not limited to net activity from the current posting job.
# If zero or positive: no entry generated.
# If negative: generate one entry — Debit Online Prepaid Liability, Credit POS Prepaid Liability. *The amount is capped at the ending ledger balance of Online Prepaid Liability at the same posting level* — never the full absolute value of the negative.
# Any remaining negative balance on POS Prepaid Liability after reclassification is real — it reflects student meal debt (charge meals permitted under USDA regulations and state anti-lunch-shaming laws). This is accurate and expected. The CN Director must reconcile this through their normal debt collection process. *This story does not create or post to a separate accounts receivable or student debt data point.*

Entry description: {{Prepaid Liability Reclassification | [Period Month Year]}}

The reclassification entry does not appear in the template entries dropdown — it is system-generated only and must be visually distinguishable in the journal and Posting Activity Log.

*Reposting:* If a prior period is reposted, only that period's reclassification entry is regenerated based on the recalculated ending ledger balances. Consumption entries in that period are stateless and do not change. Subsequent periods' reclassification entries may also need regeneration since they read cumulative balances — but their consumption entries remain untouched.

*Example A — Online covers the shortfall:*

Lincoln Elementary, January 2026. After all deposit and consumption rules have posted:

||Account||Balance Before Reclass||
|POS Prepaid Liability|-$1,200|
|Online Prepaid Liability|+$4,800|

System generates:

||Entry||Debit||Credit||Amount||
|Prepaid Liability Reclassification | Jan 2026|Online Prepaid Liability|POS Prepaid Liability|$1,200|

||Account||Balance After Reclass||
|POS Prepaid Liability|$0|
|Online Prepaid Liability|+$3,600|

*Example B — Online cannot fully cover the shortfall (charge meals):*

Washington Middle School, January 2026. High charge meal volume this period:

||Account||Balance Before Reclass||
|POS Prepaid Liability|-$3,000|
|Online Prepaid Liability|+$800|

Sweep is capped at Online's available balance of $800:

||Entry||Debit||Credit||Amount||
|Prepaid Liability Reclassification | Jan 2026|Online Prepaid Liability|POS Prepaid Liability|$800|

||Account||Balance After Reclass||
|POS Prepaid Liability|-$2,200|
|Online Prepaid Liability|$0|
