The remaining -$2,200 on POS Prepaid Liability is student meal debt — real, expected, and the CN Director's responsibility to reconcile. The meal sales entries themselves were never modified — they recorded exactly what the POS reported.

----

h2. Applies to All Rules

* *Aggregation:* One entry per configured site level (Enrollment Site or Central Office, per district setting), per Period. Meal sales follow the same site-level flag as deposit rules.
* *Execution Order:* Deposits (NXT-70340) → Consumption (Rules 1, 2, 4 Step 1) → Reclassification (Rule 4 Step 2). Account Adjustments (Rule 3) are manual and do not participate in the automated posting job.
* *Business Rules Drawers:* New rules must reflect in the business rules drawers of associated data points.
* *Legacy Cleanup:* "Prepaid Account" (PPACC) is no longer referenced in active posting rules, business rules drawers, dropdowns, template entries, or new configuration/UI surfaces. Historical journal entries and migration artifacts are not in scope.
* *Template Entries:* Dropdown updated to reflect these changes. {{PREPLIAB-RECLASS}} excluded from dropdown.
NXT-70909.desc:
h2. 

Updates the remaining legacy rules that referenced the single "Prepaid Account" (PPACC) following its replacement by POS Prepaid Liability and Online Prepaid Liability (NXT-70340). Covers returned checks, sales tax on prepaid consumption, account adjustments, and meal sales.

h3. Context for Developers

*Pooled Balance:* At consumption time, the POS has a single pooled student balance. It does not track whether funds originated from POS or Online deposits. Financial Oversight does not receive student-level source attribution for prepaid consumption.

*Design Decision:* All consumption rules are stateless — they always debit POS Prepaid Liability. If this causes POS Prepaid Liability to go negative at a posting level, the system generates a reclassification entry at the end of the posting job to sweep funds from Online Prepaid Liability. This follows the [Oracle Multifund Cash Pooling|https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/financials/25d/fairp/cash-pooling-for-multifund-accounting.html#Example-of-Cash-Pooling-Methods-for-Multifund-Accounting] pattern (Intercompany Balancing Entries method).

Why this approach

* Posting engine stays stateless — no balance queries during transaction processing
* Journal is honest — consumption entries reflect what occurred; the reclassification reflects what the system calculated
* No dirty period cascading — reposting a prior period only regenerates that period's reclassification entry, not every consumption entry through year-end
* CN Directors routinely reopen past periods for USDA claim adjustments; a stateful waterfall would break the audit trail across all subsequent periods

NXT-70866.ac:
*Bell Notification*

* When the readiness gate blocks a posting job, a bell notification is created
* Notification appears in POS, Inventory, and Financial Oversight modules
* Notification text: _"Financial postings could not run. [X] required GL mappings are incomplete."_ where [X] = current count of incomplete mappings
* Tapping the notification navigates to Account Mapping page filtered to show only incomplete mappings
* Only one notification per gate activation — do not create a new notification for each blocked job
* If the gap count changes (e.g., user fixes one mapping but a new gap appears), fire a new notification
* If the user resolves all gaps, do not send a "resolved" notification — postings resume silently on the next cycle

*On-Screen Banner*

* When the readiness gate is active, display a yellow informational banner pinned to the top of the Posting Activity Log page
* Banner text: same message and dynamic count as the bell notification
* Banner includes a link to Account Mapping page, pre-filtered to incomplete mappings only
* Banner has no dismiss button — it remains visible as long as gaps exist
* Banner disappears automatically when all required mappings are complete

*Edge Cases*

* If the user has no access to the Account Mapping page, the notification and banner still appear but the link is not shown
* If multiple posting jobs are blocked in the same cycle, only one notification fires — not one per job
* If the system is in Not Ready state at the time of the very first posting job after module setup, the notification fires immediately — do not wait for a second cycle
NXT-70866.desc:
*As a* district finance manager, *I want to* be notified when automated postings cannot run due to incomplete GL mappings, *so that* I can resolve the gaps immediately and keep my financial data complete.

*Bell Notification*

* Fires once when a posting job is blocked by the readiness gate
* Appears in POS, Inventory, and Financial Oversight — wherever the user is
* Message: _"Financial postings could not run. 2 required GL mappings are incomplete."_
* Count is dynamic — always reflects current number of gaps
* Tapping navigates to Account Mapping page, pre-filtered to show only incomplete mappings
* Does not re-fire for the same gap state — one notification per gate activation
* New notification fires only if the gap count changes

*On-Screen Banner (Posting Activity Log)*

* Yellow informational banner pinned to top of Posting Activity Log while gate is active
* Same message, same count, same one-click link to filtered Account Mapping
* Disappears automatically when all required mappings are complete — no dismiss button while gaps remain

*What we are NOT building*

* No email alerts or escalation chains
* No toast that auto-dismisses
* No notification per individual missing mapping
* No notification when the gate clears — postings just resume silently
* No inline warnings on other pages beyond the banner on Posting Activity Log

NXT-70777.summary: FIN — Import Monitor: Journal Entry Lines Import (Payroll Use Case)
NXT-70777.ac:
*AC1 — Import Type Available* Given an authorized Finance user opens Import Monitor, When they view Import Types, Then Payroll Import is available.

*AC2 — First Import Requires Template Setup* Given no import template exists for Payroll Import, When the user uploads a file, Then they must select an amount format and configure column mapping before proceeding to Fiscal Year/Period selection.

*AC3 — Subsequent Imports Skip Template Setup* Given an import template already exists for Payroll Import, When the user uploads a file, Then the saved column mapping and amount format are automatically applied, the system displays a reminder of the amount format, and the user proceeds directly to Fiscal Year/Period selection.

*AC4 — Template Editable* Given a saved import template exists, When the user chooses to edit the template, Then they can modify the amount format and column mapping, and the updated template is used for future imports.

*AC5 — Period Displayed as Month Names* Given the fiscal year runs July 1 – June 30, When the user selects a Period, Then the dropdown displays "July" through "June" — not numeric values 1–12.

*AC6 — Fiscal Year and Period Selection Required* Given a user uploads a file for Payroll Import, When they proceed past file upload (and template reminder, if template exists), Then they must select a Fiscal Year and Period before reaching preview.

*AC7 — Closed Period Blocks Import* Given the user selects a Period that is in a closed status, When they attempt to proceed, Then the system blocks with a message identifying the closed period and does not allow the import to continue.

*AC8 — Required Fields Enforced* Given an upload contains rows missing GLAccount, SiteCode, Description, PostingDate, or the mapped amount field(s), When preview runs, Then those rows are flagged invalid and cannot be imported until corrected.

*AC9 — PostingDate Must Fall Within Selected Period* Given the selected Period spans October 1–31 and a row has PostingDate = July 15, When preview runs, Then that row is flagged invalid with an error indicating the posting date does not fall within the selected period.

*AC10 — Debit/Credit Rule Works (Scheme A)* Given Scheme A is used and a row has both Debit and Credit populated with > 0, When preview runs, Then the row is invalid with an error indicating only one side is allowed.

*AC11 — Signed Amount Rule Works (Scheme B)* Given Scheme B is used and a row has Amount = 0, When preview runs, Then the row is invalid with an error indicating zero is not allowed.

*AC12 — Batch Must Balance (within $0.01)* Given the absolute difference between total debit-side amounts and total credit-side amounts exceeds $0.01, When the user attempts import, Then import is blocked and totals are displayed with the imbalance amount.

*AC13 — Row Errors Block Entire Import* Given an upload contains one or more rows with validation errors (excluding GLAccount warnings), When the user views the preview, Then the confirm action is disabled and the system must not allow importing only the valid rows. All errors must be resolved by correcting and re-uploading the file.

*AC14 — Import Is Atomic* Given the user confirms an import and a system-level error occurs during processing, When the error is encountered, Then the entire import rolls back, no journal entry data is written, and the user is notified that the import failed.

*AC15 — Unknown GLAccount Imports with Warning* Given an upload contains a GLAccount that is not recognized in the chart of accounts, When the import completes, Then the row is imported successfully and the account displays with a warning sign under GL Accounts in Configuration > General Ledger.

*AC16 — SiteCode Validated Against Site Management* Given an upload contains a SiteCode that does not exist in Site Management, When preview runs, Then that row is flagged invalid with a clear error consistent with Import Monitor patterns.

*AC17 — Results Visible* Given an import completes, When the user opens import results, Then they can see status, the selected Fiscal Year and Period, record counts, totals, and row-level errors if any.

*AC18 — Duplicate Import Detected and Warned* Given a payroll import has already been completed for a Fiscal Year and Period, When the user attempts to import another file for the same Fiscal Year and Period, Then the system warns the user that a previous import exists for that period and requires explicit acknowledgment before proceeding.

----

h2. Edge / Empty / Error States

||Scenario||Behavior||
|No template configured and user attempts import|User is guided through template setup (amount format + column mapping) before proceeding|
|Empty file or header-only|Import blocked: _"No data rows found."_|
|Selected Period is closed|Import blocked at Fiscal Year/Period selection step with message identifying the closed period|
|Non-numeric amount fields|Row invalid with parsing error|
|Both Debit and Credit populated in same row (Scheme A)|Row invalid: _"Exactly one of Debit or Credit must have a value."_|
|Negative value in Debit or Credit column (Scheme A)|Row invalid: _"Debit and Credit values must be positive."_|
|PostingDate outside selected Period date range|Row invalid: _"Posting date does not fall within the selected period."_|
|Unknown GLAccount|Row imports with warning; account flagged in Configuration > General Ledger|
|Unknown SiteCode|Row invalid with clear error consistent with Import Monitor patterns|
|File contains mix of valid and invalid rows|Confirm action disabled; all errors must be resolved before import. No partial import allowed.|
|System error during import processing|Entire import rolls back; no data written; user notified of failure|
|Import attempted for a FY/Period that already has a completed import|System warns user of potential duplicate; requires explicit acknowledgment before proceeding|
|Uploaded file columns don't match saved template mapping|Preview flags unmapped/missing columns; user must fix file or edit template|

----

h2. Defaults

* *Fiscal Year and Period:* Selected at the import level after file upload. Period is displayed as the month name corresponding to the fiscal year calendar. These values are selected fresh for each import and are not part of the saved template.
* *Amount format reminder:* On subsequent imports, the system displays the saved amount format as a reminder (e.g., _"Your template uses separate Debit and Credit columns."_). The user does not re-select unless editing the template.
* For Scheme A, the unmapped side (Debit or Credit) is treated as 0 for totals.
* For district-wide entries, the user should use the site code designated as the district-level site in their Site Management configuration. The import UI guidance text should read: _"For district-wide entries, use your district-level site code as configured in Site Management."_
* Comments field: max 50 characters.
* Optional fields default to blank/null when not mapped.
NXT-70777.desc:
As a district finance user, I want to import journal entry lines into Financials via Import Monitor, so that recurring payroll and other month-end journal entries can be loaded efficiently without manual data entry.

----

h2. Requirements

h3. 1) Outcome

1.1 A district finance user can import journal entry lines into Financials via Import Monitor.

1.2 After a one-time template setup, recurring monthly imports require only upload → select fiscal year and period → preview → import.

1.3 The import flow supports month-end reconciliation by providing totals, errors, and import results, reducing risk of duplicate or missed entries.

h3. 2) Import Type

2.1 A new Import Monitor Import Type exists named *Payroll Import* with help text: _"Used for payroll and other month-end journal imports in Financials."_

2.2 Import Type supports the same upload formats as other Import Monitor types (XLSX/CSV per current platform behavior).

2.3 Import Type is available only to users with appropriate Financials permissions (same pattern as GL Accounts import access).

h3. 3) Import Template (one-time setup)

3.1 On the first import for this import type, the user must configure an *Import Template* consisting of:

* *Amount Format* — one of:
** _"My file has separate Debit and Credit columns"_ → Scheme A
** _"My file has a single Amount column (positive = debit, negative = credit)"_ → Scheme B
* *Column Mapping* — mapping of uploaded file columns to target fields (see Sections 5 and 6).

3.2 The Amount Format selection determines which amount target fields appear in the column mapping UI. Scheme A displays Debit and Credit as required target fields. Scheme B displays Amount as the required target field. The non-selected fields must not appear in the mapping UI.

3.3 The template is saved after the first successful configuration. The user can edit the template (amount format and column mapping) at any time.

3.4 Only one template exists per import type. Configuring a new template replaces the existing one.

h3. 4) Fiscal Year and Period Selection

4.1 After file upload, the user must select:

* *Fiscal Year* — must be a valid fiscal year in the system.
* *Period* — displayed as the *month name* corresponding to each period relative to the fiscal year start (e.g., if the fiscal year runs July 1 – June 30, the dropdown shows "July" through "June," not 1–12).

4.2 All rows in the imported file are assigned to the selected Fiscal Year and Period. These values are not mapped from the file.

4.3 The selected Period must be in an *open* status. If the period is closed, the system must block the import at this step with a message: _"[Month] for Fiscal Year [YYYY] is closed. Please open the period before importing."_

4.4 Fiscal Year and Period are selected fresh for each import and are *not* part of the saved template.

h3. 5) Import Flow

5.1 *First import (no template exists):* Upload → Amount Format Selection → Column Mapping → Save Template → Select Fiscal Year/Period → Preview → Confirm.

5.2 *Subsequent imports (template exists):* Upload → System displays a reminder of the saved amount format (e.g., _"Your template uses separate Debit and Credit columns."_) → Select Fiscal Year/Period → Preview → Confirm.

5.3 On subsequent imports, the saved column mapping and amount format are automatically applied. The user does not re-map columns or re-select the amount format unless they choose to edit the template.

h3. 6) Required Target Fields

The import mapping UI (during template setup or editing) must allow mapping uploaded columns to these target fields:

||Field||Notes||
|GLAccount|Account identifier per Financials rules|
|SiteCode|Site the entry applies to. Must reference a valid site in Site Management. For district-wide entries, use the site code designated as the district-level site in Site Management.|
|Description|Free text description for the journal entry line|
|PostingDate|Date the entry should post to the ledger. Must fall within the date range of the selected Period.|

*Amount fields (determined by the Amount Format in the template):*

||Amount Format||Target Fields Shown||Convention||
|Separate Debit and Credit columns|Debit, Credit|Separate positive values in each column|
|Single Amount column|Amount|Positive = debit, negative = credit|

h3. 7) Optional Target Fields

||Field||Notes||
|AccountName|Descriptive label for the account|
|Comments|External reference identifier; free text; max 50 characters|

h3. 8) Validation Rules (row-level)

8.1 GLAccount, SiteCode, Description, and PostingDate must be present for each row. Amount fields must be present per the template's amount format.

8.2 Amount validation must follow the template's amount format:

*If Scheme A (Debit/Credit):*

* Exactly one of Debit or Credit must be a positive number per row.
* Negative values are not allowed.

*If Scheme B (Amount):*

* Amount must be non-zero.
* Negative amounts are allowed (treated as credits by convention).

8.3 Zero values are not allowed in either scheme and will be flagged as invalid.

8.4 PostingDate must fall within the start and end dates of the selected Period for the selected Fiscal Year. Rows where PostingDate falls outside the period date range are flagged invalid with the error: _"Posting date does not fall within the selected period."_

8.5 GLAccount should be a valid account identifier per Financials rules. If the account is not recognized, the row is imported with a warning, and the account displays with a warning sign under GL Accounts in Configuration > General Ledger. This is not a blocking error.

8.6 SiteCode must reference a valid site in Site Management. Unknown site codes are flagged as invalid.

h3. 9) Validation Rules (import-level)

9.1 The system must calculate and display:

* Total rows
* Total debits and total credits (computed from the template's amount format)

9.2 Import must be blocked if the absolute difference between total debits and total credits exceeds *$0.01*. A difference of $0.01 or less is allowed to accommodate rounding across line items. Totals and any imbalance amount must be displayed to the user alongside the blocking message.

9.3 The import must be *atomic* — either all rows are imported successfully, or no rows are imported. Partial imports are not allowed. If any system-level error occurs during import processing, the entire import must roll back and the user must be notified that no data was written.

h3. 10) Preview and Confirm

10.1 The preview must display the selected Fiscal Year and Period, the amount format from the template, totals, and a sample of parsed rows.

10.2 The user must explicitly confirm to perform the import.

10.3 If any rows contain validation errors (excluding GLAccount warnings per 8.5), the confirm action must be disabled. All errors must be resolved by correcting and re-uploading the file before the import can proceed. The system must not allow the user to import only the valid rows.

h3. 11) Import Results and Auditability

11.1 Each import must produce an import result record consistent with Import Monitor patterns (status, counts, timestamps, user).

11.2 The user can view import results including:

* Success/failed status
* Selected Fiscal Year and Period
* Total debits and credits and record counts
* Row-level error messages for invalid records

h3. 12) Duplicate Risk (business requirement)

12.1 The system must detect and warn the user when an import appears to be a duplicate of a previous import for the same Fiscal Year and Period. The warning must occur before the user confirms the import.

12.2 If a potential duplicate is detected, the user must explicitly acknowledge the warning before the import can proceed. The system must not silently allow duplicate imports.

12.3 The specific detection mechanism is a technical design decision. The business requirement is: if a user accidentally uploads the same payroll file twice for the same period, the system must catch it and intervene before the second import writes to the ledger.

NXT-70682.summary: School Nutrition Profit & Loss Promotional Report
NXT-70682.ac:
# The report shows header information: district name, report title, reporting period, fiscal year, and “generated by” name/date-time.
# The report includes an *Executive Summary* table with columns {{Line Item}} and {{Amount}}, containing:
#* Net Gain/(Loss)
#* Total Revenue
#* Total Expenditures
#* Beginning Fund Balance
#* Ending Fund Balance
# The report includes a *Revenue* table with columns:
#* Description
#* Current Period
#* YTD
#* Budget
#* % of Revenue
#* % Change
#* Variance
# The Revenue table includes grouped line items and a {{Total Revenue}} row.
# The report includes an *Expenditures* table with columns:
#* Description
#* Current Period
#* YTD
#* Budget
#* % of Expenses
#* % Change
#* Variance
# The Expenditures table includes grouped line items and a {{Total Expenditures}} row.
# The report includes a *Summary* table with columns:
#* Line Item
#* Current Period
#* YTD
#* Budget
#* Variance
#* Prior Year
# The Summary table contains:
#* Total Revenue
#* Total Expenditures
#* Net Gain/(Loss)
#* Beginning Fund Balance
#* Ending Fund Balance
# The report includes a *Compliance Checks* table with columns:
#* Metric
#* Value
#* Status
#* Benchmark

# The report includes a *KPI Analysis* table with columns:
#* Metric
#* Current
#* vs Prior Year
#* Signal
# The report includes a legend defining data source markers: {{System-generated}} and {{Manually entered}}.
# The report includes a disclosure statement that this is not a substitute for a fully audited financial statement.

*Requirements*

# Support report metadata inputs: district, period, fiscal year, generated-by name, generated date-time.
# Support line-item source classification (system-generated vs manually entered).
# Support grouped financial categories within Revenue and Expenditures.
# Support subtotal/total rows in financial statements.
# Support benchmark/status reporting for compliance metrics.
# Support explanatory “Signal” text for KPI rows.
# Support inclusion of formula notes on applicable line items (for example, indirect costs formula).
# Generate output suitable for stakeholder distribution as a printable report/PDF.
NXT-70682.desc: As a district finance stakeholder, I want a printable Profit & Loss report with executive summary, detailed revenue and expenditure statements, and supporting analysis so I can review performance and share a clear example with internal teams and external stakeholders.

NXT-70620.summary: FIN — Post Inventory Purchases (PURCH) by Valuation Category × Vendor (Level 2)
NXT-70620.ac:
h3. AC1 — Amount basis is used (not Market Value)

*Given* Purchases report (or underlying data) shows Amount ≠ Market Value for a vendor in a period
*When* PURCH posts
*Then* the posted total equals *Total Receipt Amount / Amount*, not Market Value. Purchases

h3. AC2 — VC × Vendor aggregation produces correct number of entries

*Given* Site A, Period P has:

* Vendor V1: VC-Food $10,000; VC-NonFood $1,000
* Vendor V2: VC-Food $5,000
*When* posting runs
*Then* exactly 3 debit/credit pairs are created (V1/Food, V1/NonFood, V2/Food), and no receipt/item-level pairs exist.

h3. AC3 — Debit/Credit accounts are correct

*Given* any PURCH posting line for (Vendor, VC)
*Then* Debit = VC’s Inventory Asset GL
*And* Credit = configured Cash in Bank GL.

h3. AC4 — Missing VC mapping blocks posting and lists all missing

*Given* receipts include VC1 and VC2
*And* VC2 Asset mapping is missing
*When* posting runs
*Then* nothing posts
*And* the error lists VC2 as missing (and any other missing mappings).

h3. AC5 — Missing vendor uses “Unknown Vendor”

*Given* at least one receipt has vendor null
*When* posting runs
*Then* amounts from those receipts are included under Vendor = “Unknown Vendor”
*And* totals still tie to the period’s Total Receipt Amount.

h3. AC6 — No receipts means no entries (success)

*Given* a closed inventory period with zero receipts
*When* posting runs
*Then* zero PURCH entries are created
*And* job is marked successful with “0 entries created.”

h3. AC7 — Reopen/reclose causes reversal + repost

*Given* period P previously posted PURCH
*When* period P is reopened and closed again
*Then* reversal entries are created and new entries are posted
*And* reversal + repost succeed or fail together.

----

h2. Edge / empty / error states

* Do not create $0 entries for any Vendor×VC combination.
* Vendor name changes do not affect grouping; grouping is by vendor ID (if available).
* If item-to-VC mapping changes after posting, repost uses the current mapping only upon reclose (by design).

----

h2. Defaults

* Posting detail level: *Level 2 (VC × Vendor)*.
* Posting basis: *Amount / Total Receipt Amount* (not Market Value). 
* Posted Date: *period end date*.
* Credit GL: single configured *Cash in Bank*.
NXT-70620.desc:
As a Child Nutrition Director, I want inventory purchases to post automatically to the ledger at *Valuation Category × Vendor* detail (per site, per closed inventory period), so that postings remain GL-manageable while still reconcilable to vendor spend.

h2. Revised Requirements (Purchase posting uses Purchase Report as source)

h3. R1 — Source of truth (no custom derivation)

For each *Site + Inventory Period*, Financial Oversight must ingest the *Purchase Report dataset* generated by the Inventory module.

* Financial Oversight must *not* calculate purchases by traversing receipt tables or re-deriving totals.
* The Purchase Report dataset is the authoritative source for: *Vendor*, *Valuation Category*, and *Purchase totals* for the period.

h3. R2 — Amount field to use (new finding)

When ingesting the Purchase Report dataset, the posting amount must use *Amount / Total Receipt Amount*, not *Market Value*. Purchases

h3. R3 — Level 2 grouping (as reported, not recomputed)

Financial Oversight must post purchases at *Valuation Category × Vendor* detail *exactly as represented in the Purchase Report dataset* for the site-period.

Output grain:

* One total per *Site + Period + Vendor + Valuation Category*

h3. R4 — Posting output

For each (Site, Period, Vendor, Valuation Category) total from the Purchase Report dataset, create one debit/credit pair:

||Debit||Credit||
|Valuation Category → Inventory Asset GL|Cash in Bank GL|

h3. R5 — Missing vendor handling

If the Purchase Report dataset returns a row with no vendor identifier, group it under *“Unknown Vendor”* and still post.

h3. R6 — Mapping gates

If any required mappings are missing (VC Asset GL, Cash in Bank), the posting job fails and *creates zero entries*.

NXT-70426.summary: Add Segment Column Checkbox to Financials Report Configuration
NXT-70426.ac:
* The checkbox appears on the Reports tab, under the General Ledger & Journal section, as a toggle labeled *"Split account code by segment."*
* Below the toggle, display helper text in small, muted text: _"When enabled, each segment of the account code appears as its own column in the export, using the segment name as the column header. The full account code is also included as a reference column."_
* The toggle defaults to *ON* for all districts.
* *When ON:* Every ledger and journal export includes one column per segment (using the segment names defined in the district's account code structure configuration as column headers), plus one column labeled "Account Code" containing the full combined code.
* *When OFF:* Every ledger and journal export includes only the single "Account Code" column. No segment columns are produced.
* Changes to the toggle take effect on the next export generated. Previously exported files in the Document Center are not affected.
* *Validation gate:* The toggle is *disabled* (greyed out, non-interactive) if any *active* GL account does not conform to the configured account code structure. On hover over the disabled toggle, display a tooltip: *"[X] active account(s) do not match the account code structure. Go to GL Accounts to correct them."* where [X] is the count of non-conforming active accounts. "GL Accounts" is a link that navigates the user to *Configuration > General Ledger Accounts > GL Accounts*. Once all active accounts are valid, the toggle becomes interactive and retains its last-set state (or the default if never set).
* This checkbox setting only applies to ledger and journal exports. It does not affect other export types or on-screen display.
NXT-70426.desc:
*As a* district administrator configuring the Financial Oversight module, *I want* to control whether GL account codes are split into individual segment columns in exports, *So that* my finance team receives data in the exact column structure they need to import into Oracle or Tyler Munis without manual reformatting.

Suggested mockup:



!image-20260311-184033.png|width=818,alt="image-20260311-184033.png"!

NXT-70423.summary: FIN - Add "Select All Accounts" Shortcut and Export Format Choice to the General Ledger Report
NXT-70423.ac:
* The account selection dropdown must include a select all checkbox that instantly selects all active, mapped GL accounts currently visible within the user's search filter.
* The system must display the selected accounts as removable chips alongside a label that accurately counts the total number of selected accounts.
* The asynchronous Document Center generation must successfully process and export the report without timing out, even when the maximum number of district GL accounts are selected.
NXT-70423.desc:
*As a* Child Nutrition Director running a General Ledger Report, *I want* to be able to export all accounts in one action and choose how my export is packaged, *So that* I don't have to manually select every account one by one and can receive my data in the format that works best for my finance team.

Suggested mockup:



!image-20260311-184051.png|width=818,alt="image-20260311-184051.png"!
NXT-70423.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-70422.summary: FIN - Enforce Universal GL Mapping Integrity Rules
NXT-70422.ac:
h2. AC-1 — Debit-Credit Collision Prevention

Within any single GL entry template, no data point on the debit side may be mapped to the same GL account as any data point on the credit side of that same template.

This rule prevents a self-canceling journal entry — a net-zero posting that silently omits a transaction from the district's GL export, violating GAAP's requirement that every transaction be faithfully represented in the financial record.

*A mapping only saves if AC-1 is satisfied AND both prerequisites are satisfied. All three, every time, for every template type.*

h3. Enforcement Points

*At mapping save or update:* If saving or updating a data point's GL account mapping would create a debit-credit collision in any template that includes that data point, block the save and display an error.

*At template creation or update:* If a user creates or edits a template and selects a debit-side data point and a credit-side data point that are already mapped to the same GL account, block the save and display an error.

In both cases, the check must evaluate *every* debit-credit combination within the template, not just the pair being edited, to account for templates that carry multiple debit or credit lines.

h3. *Error Message*

"This mapping cannot be saved. Data points [Data Point A] and [Data Point B] are both mapped to the same GL account [GL Account Name] on opposite sides of the [Template Name] entry template. This would cause the journal entry to cancel itself out. Please resolve the conflict before continuing."

If more than two data points collide, list all of them: "Data points [A], [B], and [C] are all mapped to…"

*Visual Indicator*

When a collision is detected, highlight every conflicting data point row with a warning icon and a red/amber left border. The indicator stays until the conflict is resolved.

*Blocking Behavior*

Once a collision exists on a template, block all further mapping additions to that template — not just the conflicting rows. The user must resolve the existing conflict before adding or editing any other mappings. This prevents stacking multiple collisions that obscure the root problem.

Updated on {{2026-04-09}}

----

h2. AC-2 — Select All Accounts for Manual Entry

* Given a user is on the Manual Entry page and needs to add GL accounts,
* When they want to make all accounts available,
* Then the system must provide a {{Select all accounts}} option that adds all GL accounts in a single action, rather than requiring each account to be added one row at a time.
* When {{Select all accounts}} is enabled, all GL accounts should be added as entry rows and the {{+ ADD ROW}} action should no longer be shown.
* When {{Select all accounts}} is turned off after rows have already been loaded, the system must display a confirmation warning before clearing the loaded rows and any entered amounts.
* The Manual Entry page must also provide an always-visible {{Filter entry rows}} field so users can filter the GL accounts already loaded into the entry.

----

h2. Edge Cases

||#||Scenario||Same Template?||Expected Behavior||
|1|Data Point A (debit) and Data Point B (credit) are in the same template (e.g., Pre Payment). Both mapped to GL 1010.|Yes|*Blocked* by AC-1.|
|2|Data Point A (debit) is in the Pre Payment template. Data Point C (credit) is in the Refund template. Both mapped to GL 1010.|No|*Allowed* — AC-1 only checks within a single template.|
|3|Data Point A and Data Point B are both on the debit side of the same template. Both mapped to GL 4100.|Same template, same side|*Allowed* — many-to-one on the same side is permitted (prerequisite).|
|4|User changes Data Point A's mapping from GL 1010 to GL 1020. Data Point B (opposite side, same template) is already mapped to GL 1020.|Yes|*Blocked* by AC-1 at mapping save.|
|5|A template has 3 debit data points and 2 credit data points. Only one debit-credit pair shares a GL account.|Yes|*Blocked* — any single collision within a template is enough.|
|6|Data point is inactive but mapped to a GL account that would collide with the opposite side of the same template.|Yes, but inactive|*Not blocked* — inactive data points do not generate entries.|
|7|User clicks Select All on the Manual Entry page.|N/A|All GL accounts added as rows in one action (AC-2).|
|8|User creates a manual entry and enters the same GL account on both the debit and credit rows.|N/A|*Allowed* — manual entries are intentional, user-controlled journal entries. AC-1 only applies to automated entry templates.|

Update on 3/31/2026:

AC-2 now includes the Manual Entry interaction details for {{Select all accounts}}. When unchecked, the page stays in normal manual-entry mode with {{+ ADD ROW}}; when checked, all GL accounts are added in one action and {{+ ADD ROW}} is hidden. If the user turns {{Select all accounts}} off after rows have been loaded, the system should show a short confirmation before clearing loaded rows and entered amounts; once confirmed, the page returns to manual mode. AC-2 also now includes an always-visible {{Filter entry rows}} field to filter accounts already in the entry.
NXT-70422.desc:
*As a* Child Nutrition Director configuring the Financial Oversight module, *I want* the system to automatically prevent me from creating a mapping that would corrupt the integrity of a journal entry, *So that* my exported ledger data is always audit-ready and I never produce self-canceling or misclassified transactions in Oracle or Tyler Munis.

h2. Prerequisites (already implemented — included for context)

*Base Type Integrity Rule:* A data point may only be mapped to a GL account whose base type matches its own (Revenue → Revenue GL, Expense → Expense GL, Asset → Asset GL, Liability → Liability GL). This is enforced at the dropdown level — when a user opens the GL account selector for a data point, only accounts matching that data point's base type appear. No cross-type account is ever selectable.

*Many-to-One Mapping is Permitted:* Multiple data points of the same base type may be mapped to the same GL account. This is not blocked, not warned on, and not restricted in any way. Districts commonly map all meal sales revenue data points to a single revenue GL account — this is valid and expected.
NXT-70422.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-70372.summary: FIN - Configure Prepaid Liability Posting Level (Enrollment vs. Central)
NXT-70372.ac:
h3. AC1 — Configuration UI

* Given a user with Director/SchoolCafe Administrator permissions navigates to Financial Oversight › Configuration › Settings
* When they view the *Posting Settings* section,
* Then they see a control labeled *"Prepaid Liability, Bonus & Refund Posting"* with two mutually exclusive options:
*# *Distributed (Enrollment Site)* — _Per-site postings_ | Tooltip: Posts Prepaid Liability, Bonus, and Refund transactions at each enrollment site. 
*# *Centralized (District Office)* — _One district-level posting_ | Tooltip: Posts one district total for Prepaid Liability, Bonus, and Refund transactions to the Central Office.

h3. AC2 — Logic: Distributed (Default)

* *Given* "Distributed" is selected,
* *When* Period Close generates entries,
* *Then* the debit and credit sides of the entry use the relevant site’s sub-accounts and entries are made per enrollment site. 

h3. AC3 — Logic: Centralized

* *Given* *"Centralized"* is selected,
*When* Period Close generates entries,
*Then* the debit and credit sides of the transaction use the *Central Office sub-account* and *one entry* is made combining amounts for all sites in that district, where the Central Office site code is determined as follows:
*# *Primary:* Fetch the district’s *Central Office site code* from *Site Configuration* (district/site setup).
*# *Fallback:* If no Central Office site code exists in configuration, use a *default site code* of *all zeros*, where *zero-count = district site code length*.

h3. *AC4 — Warning*

* Given a district switches from Centralized to Distributed or vice-versa,
* When they toggle the option,
* Then the system must:
*# Display a confirmation dialog: _"Are you sure? This will change how Future Prepaid Liability, Bonus, and Refund ledger entries are posted."_
*# Upon confirmation, auto-toggle the opposite option off.
*# Show a success toast: _"Future Prepaid Liability, Bonus, and Refund ledger entries will now post at the [enrollment site / central office] level."_

Note: This includes prepaid liability, refunds and bonuses. Only post sites that have a *non-zero net amount* for the period.
NXT-70372.desc:
As a Child Nutrition Director, I want to toggle my Prepaid Liability posting strategy between "Student Enrollment Site" and "Central Office," So that I can align the system's automated ledger entries with my district's balance sheet strategy (Site-Based vs. Pooled).

*Requirements*

* *Settings Page Scope and Structure* Add a Settings page (Financials › Settings) with the tab *Posting Rules*.
* *Posting Rules Content and Help Text* In Posting Rules, show a *Prepaid Liability, Bonus & Refund Posting* control with two mutually exclusive options:
** *Distributed (Enrollment Site)* — _Per-site postings_ | Tooltip: Posts Prepaid Liability, Bonus, and Refund at each enrollment site.
** *Centralized (District Office)* — _One district-level posting_ | Tooltip: Posts one district total for Prepaid Liability, Bonus, and Refund transactions to the Central Office. 

*Decision Behavior and Warning* Changing posting mode must require confirmation, auto-toggle the opposite option off, and show a success toast:

{quote}_"Future Prepaid Liability, Bonus, and Refund ledger entries will now post at the [enrollment site / central office] level."_{quote}

Update on {{2026-03-06}}: Clarification: *Prepaid Liability, Bonus & Refund Posting is a radio-button choice* with two options, *Distributed (Enrollment Site)* and *Centralized (District Office)*; users can select only one at a time, and the existing card layout, confirmation prompt, and success toast remain unchanged.

!Screenshot 2026-03-05 at 7.22.34 PM.png|width=818,alt="Screenshot 2026-03-05 at 7.22.34 PM.png"!

[^Financial Oversight Draft_2026-03-05_Settings_Configuration.html]

NXT-70340.summary: FIN — Prepaid Liability Posting Rules for All Payment Types
NXT-70340.ac:
h1. AC1 — Posting Setting: Distributed vs. Centralized

This story respects the "Prepaid Account, Bonus & Refund Posting" setting configured in Financials › Configuration › Settings › Posting Settings. The setting has two mutually exclusive options:

||Option||Behavior||
|*Distributed (Enrollment Site) — Default*|Entries post per enrollment site. Each site with a non-zero net amount for the period gets its own entry.|
|*Centralized (District Office)*|All transactions across the district are combined into one entry posted to the Central Office site. Central Office site code is pulled from Site Configuration; if none exists, falls back to a default code of all zeros matching the district's site code length.|

*Scope:* This setting controls posting for Prepaid Account (AC2), Bonuses (AC3), and Refunds (AC4). All other automated payment types in this story (AC5–AC8) follow the same setting for consistency.

*Switching:* When a district toggles between Distributed and Centralized, the change applies to future postings only. Previously posted entries are not restated.

Throughout this story, *[Site]* in entry descriptions and aggregation rules means: the enrollment site (if Distributed) or the Central Office (if Centralized).

----

h1. AC2 — Pre Payment (POS and Online Deposits)

*Entry Type Name:* Pre Payment

Parents load money onto student accounts either at the register (cash/check) or online (web/app). Both post to the same single Prepaid Account — there is no split into separate POS and Online buckets at the GL level.

The system generates two separate entries per site per period — one for POS deposits and one for Online deposits. The distinct entry descriptions give the district visibility into how much came from each source through the ledger, without requiring separate GL accounts.

*POS Deposits (Cash/Check):*

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Cash in Bank|
|Credit|Prepaid Account|
|Amount|Sum of all POS prepayment transactions for the site and period|
|Entry Description in Posting Activity Log|POS Pre Payments for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{POS Pre Payments for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

*Online Deposits:*

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Cash in Bank|
|Credit|Prepaid Account|
|Amount|Sum of all online prepayment transactions for the site and period|
|Entry Description in Posting Activity Log|Online Pre Payments for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{Online Pre Payments for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

*Why one account, two entries:* The district's GL has one Prepaid Account line — simple to map, simple to export. But the CN Director or finance officer can filter the ledger by entry description at any time to see exactly how much came from the register vs. online. The reconciliation workflow is preserved: POS entries match to the bank deposit slip, Online entries match to the processor settlement report.

----

h1. AC3 — Bonus

*Entry Type Name:* Bonus

District offers a bonus when parents load money (e.g., load $50, get $5 bonus). The system generates one entry per site per period that consolidates all bonus transactions regardless of source (POS or Online).

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Bonus|
|Credit|Prepaid Account|
|Amount|Sum of all bonus transactions (POS + Online) for the site and period|
|Entry Description in Posting Activity Log|Bonus for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{Bonus for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

_AC3 modified on Mar 19, 2026_

----

h1. AC4 — Refunds (Automatic | Source: POS Period Close)

*Entry Type Name:* Refunds

Records the financial impact when a student's prepaid balance is refunded to the parent/guardian. Since the actual cash disbursement is handled by the district's ERP (Oracle, Tyler Munis, etc.), this entry records the net effect in a single step rather than creating an intermediate refund liability.

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Prepaid Account|
|Credit|Cash in Bank|
|Amount|Sum of all refund transactions for the site and period|
|Entry Description|Refunds for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{Refunds for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

⚠️ *Data Point Removal:* The legacy "Refunds (Liability)" system account/data point should be removed from the Financials 2.0 chart of accounts. It is no longer needed since we are not using a two-step refund posting model. This means one fewer account for districts to map during GL configuration and one fewer manual ledger entry type to maintain.

_Updated on Mar 19, 2026_

----

h1. AC5 — Returned Checks (Automatic | Source: POS Period Close)

*Entry Type Name:* Returned Checks

A check previously deposited at POS bounces. The original deposit credited Prepaid Account, so the reversal reduces the prepaid liability and records the cash impact in a single step. The actual bank reconciliation is handled by the district's ERP.

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Prepaid Account|
|Credit|Cash in Bank|
|Amount|Sum of all bounced check transactions for the site and period|
|Entry Description|Returned Checks for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{Returned Checks for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

⚠️ *Data Point Removal:* The legacy "Returned Checks (Liability)" system account/data point should be removed from the Financials 2.0 chart of accounts. Same rationale as Refunds — no intermediate liability needed when settlement happens in the ERP.

_Updated on Mar 19, 2026_

----

h1. AC6 — Returned Checks Fees

*Entry Type Name:* Returned Checks Fees

Fee charged to the student account for a bounced check. The bank charges the district a fee for processing the returned check, and the district passes that cost to the parent by deducting it from the student's prepaid balance. Classified as Revenue, consistent with the legacy PrimeroEdge treatment (BNKFEE).

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Prepaid Account|
|Credit|Bank Fees|
|Amount|Sum of all bounced check fee transactions for the site and period|
|Entry Description in Posting Activity Log|Returned Checks Fees for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{Returned Checks Fees for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

----

h1. AC7 — Account Adjustments

*Entry Type Name:* Account Adjustments

Manual adjustment to a student's prepaid balance. Can be positive (adding funds) or negative (reducing funds).

*Positive adjustment (adding funds):*

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Account Adjustments (Expense)|
|Credit|Prepaid Account|

*Negative adjustment (reducing funds):*

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Prepaid Account|
|Credit|Account Adjustments (Expense)|

||Field||Value||
|Amount|Sum of all adjustment transactions for the site and period|
|Entry Description in Posting Activity Log|Account Adjustments for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{Account Adjustments for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

All amounts are positive values. Accounts reverse based on the direction of the adjustment.

----

h1. AC8 — Account Transfer

*Entry Type Names:* Account Transfer Out, Account Transfer In

Money moves between student accounts — commonly principal account to student (covering meal costs) or parent transferring between siblings at different schools. Prepaid Account as a whole doesn't change at the district level, but in Distributed mode, site-level balances shift when the students are enrolled at different sites.

*Important for developers:* The principal account is tied to the serving site, not the enrollment site. Financials posts at the enrollment site level. This means when a principal covers a meal for a visiting student (enrolled elsewhere), it is always a cross-site transfer — even though the POS transaction happened at a single physical location.

*New Entry Types:*

||Entry Type Name||Purpose||Source||
|Account Transfer Out|Records prepaid funds leaving a site when a student enrolled elsewhere receives a transfer (e.g., principal covers a visiting student's meal, parent transfers between siblings)|POS Close|
|Account Transfer In|Records prepaid funds arriving at a site when a student enrolled there receives a transfer from another site|POS Close|

*Rules Drawer Updates:* These two entry types must appear in the business rules drawers of all data points involved in the transactions:

* Due From Other Sites — Account Transfer Out
* Due To Other Sites — Account Transfer In
* Prepaid Account — Account Transfer Out, Account Transfer In

*New Data Points Required:*

||Data Point||Base Type||Description||Source||Type||
|Due From Other Sites|Asset|Receivable from another site when prepaid funds transfer out to a student enrolled elsewhere|POS Close|Automatic|
|Due To Other Sites|Liability|Payable to another site when prepaid funds transfer in from a student enrolled elsewhere|POS Close|Automatic|

*Centralized mode:* No GL entry. All Prepaid Account posts to one Central Office account, so transfers between students have no GL impact regardless of enrollment site.

*Distributed mode, same enrollment site:* No GL entry. Prepaid Account at that site is unchanged.

h3. *Distributed mode, different enrollment sites:*

*Site losing funds (source student's enrollment site):*

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Due From Other Sites|
|Credit|Prepaid Account|
|Entry Description in Posting Activity Log|Account Transfer Out for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{Account Transfer Out for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

*Site gaining funds (destination student's enrollment site):*

||Field||Value||
|Debit|Prepaid Account|
|Credit|Due To Other Sites|
|Entry Description in Posting Activity Log|Account Transfer In for [Closed POS Period]|

{quote}Description Example: {{Account Transfer In for Period Start Date - Period End Date}}{quote}

||Field||Value||
|Amount|Sum of all transfer transactions between the site pair for the period|

Each entry is self-balancing at the site level. At the district level, Due From Other Sites and Due To Other Sites net to zero.

{quote}*Note:* These entries only generate when the source and destination students are enrolled at different sites. Same-site transfers (e.g., principal account to student at the same school) produce no GL entry.{quote}

----

h2. Note: Transaction Types With No Automated Posting

The following transaction types affect Prepaid Account but have no system-generated entries. They are documented here for completeness — there is nothing for QA to test.

*Balance Import (BALIMPORT):* One-time migration event. POS handles student-level imports. Financials picks up the site-level total at period close and it flows into Prepaid Account. The offsetting entry is the district's responsibility — they post it in their ERP using whatever clearing or suspense account they already have.

*Balance Override (OLDBALOVERIDE):* Migration cleanup when the initial import file was incorrect. POS reverts and reprocesses. Financials picks up the net difference at the site level at period close. Same as balance import — the offsetting entry is on the district.

----

h2. Aggregation Rules (Applies to All)

*Distributed mode:* One entry per enrollment site, per period, per payment type. Only sites with a non-zero net amount for the period generate an entry.

*Centralized mode:* One entry per district (posted to Central Office), per period, per payment type. All site-level transactions are summed into a single district-level entry.

----

h2. Validation Rules (Applies to All)

All new data points are created by the system and require mapping. Existing rules for validation apply.

----

h2. Dependencies

* "Prepaid Account, Bonus & Refund Posting" setting in Financials › Configuration › Settings (Distributed vs. Centralized) must be implemented and available
* Existing "Cash in Bank" account mapping
* Core POS and Online Payment transaction data availability
* Posting Activity Log must display entries by payment type
* Due From Other Sites and Due To Other Sites data points must be added to account mapping list
* Manual/Template Entries Story (TBD): Returned Checks second leg
NXT-70340.desc:
As a Child Nutrition Director, I want all prepayment-related transactions to automatically post to the general ledger on Period Close, so that my Prepaid Liability balance is accurate and reconcilable before exporting to the district ERP.

*Current State:* All prepayment transactions post to a single "Prepaid Liability" account but the posting rules are not defined for all payment types.

*Solution:* Define the debit and credit accounts for every payment type that touches Prepaid Liability. One GL account. One story. Each rule maps directly to a transaction type already captured by POS.

Financials is a consumer of POS data. It does not create, modify, or enrich transaction data. It reads what POS provides and posts accordingly.

{quote}*Note on data points:* All data points referenced in this story already exist in the account mapping list. No new data points are required. One-time or rare events (balance imports, balance overrides, intersite transfers) do not warrant dedicated data points — districts handle those as manual journal entries using the GL accounts they already have in their ERP.{quote}

NXT-70312.summary: FIN - Universal Bell Notifications for Data Changes & Pending Entries
NXT-70312.ac:
h3. 1. Scenario A: Source Data Changed After Posting

* When a POS or Inventory period is re-closed and the corresponding Financial period already has posted entries, the system fires a bell notification.
* This applies universally to all automated entry types from front office.
* This covers cases where entries already exist and need correction.

*Auto-adjust ON:*

* _"[Entry Type] at [Site] for [Period] was automatically adjusted. Original: $[Old Amount] → New: $[New Amount]. View details."_
* Tapping navigates to the journal showing the reversed transactions and the new entries.

*Auto-adjust OFF (default):*

* _"Source data changed for [Entry Type] at [Site] for [Period]. Original: $[Old Amount] → New: $[New Amount]. Review and adjust."_
* Tapping navigates to the Manage Periods page with the affected period expanded, showing the Data Changed warning badge, entry types, and amounts (posted vs. updated with delta).
* From there, the user can reopen the period if closed and trigger the repost.

h3. 2. Scenario B: Posting Blocked by Closed Financial Period

* When a posting is triggered but the destination Financial period is closed and no entries exist yet for that period/site/entry type, the entry is held as pending.
* Pending entries must retain: entry type, site, source period date range, and amounts.
* Pending entries must never be silently discarded.

*On blocked:*

* _"[Entry Type] posting for [Site] ([Source Period]) is pending — $[Amount]. Financial period [Period Name] is closed."_
* Tapping navigates to the Manage Periods page with the affected Financial period highlighted, showing pending entry count and amounts.

*On period reopen — Auto-adjust ON:*

* Pending entries post automatically.
* _"[X] pending entries ($[Total Amount]) for [Period Name] have been automatically posted. View details."_
* Tapping navigates to the journal showing the posted entries.

*On period reopen — Auto-adjust OFF (default):*

* _"[X] pending entries ($[Total Amount]) are ready for [Period Name]. Review and post."_
* Tapping navigates to the Manage Periods page with the affected period expanded, showing each pending entry with entry type, site, source period, and amounts.
* The user reviews and manually triggers the posting.

h3. 3. Notification Aggregation

* If multiple sites are affected in the same period for the same event (e.g., POS close triggers 15 sites), notifications should aggregate per period rather than per site.
* Example: _"12 entries pending across 8 sites for January 2026. $4,320.50 total."_
* Drill-down to individual sites happens on the landing page, not in the notification.

h3. 4. Reopen Period Confirmation

* If the notification navigates to a closed period and the user clicks "Reopen Period," the system must display a confirmation prompt before executing.

h3. 5. Notification Behavior

* All notifications route through the existing bell icon.
* Persistence, dismissal, and stacking behavior is governed by the existing notification infrastructure configuration. This story does not define or override that behavior.

h3. 6. Activity Log

* Activity log entries are created only when an actual reversal/repost or pending-to-posted action occurs.
* Notifications themselves do not generate activity log entries.
* Each activity log entry must include: entry type, site, period, amounts, and timestamp.

----

h2. Out of Scope

* Closed fiscal year prior period adjustments (separate story).
* Email or push notifications.
* Amount visibility on Manage Periods page (prerequisite story — must ship first).

h2. Dependencies

* FIN - Add Entry Amounts to Manage Periods Table (prerequisite — must be implemented before this story)
* FIN - Financial Period Management (dirty flag, Data Changed column, open/closed status)
* FIN - Posting Activity Log
* Existing bell icon notification infrastructure
NXT-70312.desc:
As a Child Nutrition Director, I want to be notified via the bell icon when source data changes after posting or when entries couldn't post due to a closed period, so that I can take corrective action without manually monitoring the Manage Periods page.

h3. Outcome:

* Immediate Awareness: Users are alerted to data discrepancies the moment they log in, rather than discovering them during month-end reconciliation.
* Data Integrity: Eliminates the risk of "stale" data lingering in the system after a period is thought to be finalized.
* Workflow Efficiency: Removes the need for users to manually check the "Manage Periods" screen for updates.
NXT-70312.epic_summary: Financial Period Intelligence — Automated Adjustments, Notifications & Pending Entry Resolution

NXT-70311.summary: FIN - Add Entry Amounts to Manage Periods Table
NXT-70311.ac:
h3. 1. Amount Column

* Add a "Posted Amounts" column to the Manage Periods table.
* For each period row, display the total debits (or credits — they should be equal) across all entry types for that period.
* Amounts must be formatted as currency (e.g., $4,320.50).

h3. 2. Expandable Breakdown

* When the user expands a period row, show amounts broken down by entry type and site.
* Each row in the breakdown must show: entry type, site, debit amount, credit amount.

h3. 3. Pending Amounts

* If a period has pending entries (entries that could not post because the period was closed), display the pending total separately, visually distinct from posted amounts (e.g., "$1,200.00 pending").

h3. 4. Dirty Period Amounts

* If a period is marked dirty (source data changed after posting), display both the currently posted amount and the expected amount based on updated source data, so the user can see the delta.
* Example: "Posted: $3,100.00 | Updated: $3,250.00 (+$150.00)"

----

h2. Out of Scope

* Notification behavior (separate story).
* Journal-level detail (user navigates to journal for full entries).
* Exporting amounts from this page.

h2. Dependencies

* FIN - Financial Period Management (existing Manage Periods page)
NXT-70311.desc: As a Child Nutrition Director, I want to see posted and pending entry amounts on the Manage Periods page, so that I can assess the financial impact of each period at a glance without navigating to the journal.

NXT-70309.summary: FIN - Configure GL Posting Aggregation Frequency
NXT-70309.ac:
*Configuration Options*

* The system must provide a "Posting Frequency" setting at the District Configuration level.
* *Option A: Daily (Bank Deposit Level)*
** Logic: Aggregates all transactions by "Transaction Date" and "Bank Deposit Slip Number".
** Use Case: Supports the Adams 12 "Daily transactions ONLY" requirement.
* *Option B: Monthly (Period Level)*
** Logic: Aggregates all transactions within the open Period into a single journal entry.
** Use Case: Standard districts reconciling against a monthly bank statement.
* *Option C: Weekly (Custom/Fiscal Week)*
** Logic: Aggregates transactions by a user-defined 7-day cycle (e.g., Mon-Sun).

*Fiscal Period Protection (The "Accountant's Safety Net")*

* _Crucial Validation:_ If "Weekly" is selected, the system must force a "Hard Break" at month-end.
* Example: If a week runs Mar 29 - Apr 4, the system must split this into *two* separate postings (Mar 29-31 and Apr 1-4) to ensure revenue is recognized in the correct Fiscal Period.

*Immutable History*

* Once a frequency is selected and the first transaction is posted, the setting must be locked for the remainder of the Fiscal Year to prevent reporting gaps or overlaps.

* *Retroactive Aggregation:* Changing the setting will not re-aggregate or re-post past entries; it only applies to future data.
* *Manual Override:* Users cannot "force" a daily posting if the system is set to Monthly; they must change the configuration first.

*DEPENDENCIES:*

* *Financial Periods:* Fiscal Periods must be defined to support the month-end split logic.

* *Bank Integration:* For Daily aggregation, the system depends on receiving the "Bank Deposit Slip Number" from the POS/Payment Processor.
NXT-70309.desc: As a District Nutrition Accountant I want to configure whether my General Ledger batches are generated Daily, Weekly, or Monthly So that the frequency of my automated ledger entries matches the reconciliation rhythm of my District’s specific ERP (e.g., Oracle vs. Tyler Munis).

NXT-70305.summary: FIN - Automate Prepaid Account Liability Posting via System-Defined Entry Types
NXT-70305.ac:
*AC1 — Configuration: Replace Single Data Point with Two*

_Given_ a user opens the account mapping page _When_ they view the Customer Accounts subgroup under Liabilities _Then_ the single "Prepaid Account" data point no longer exists and has been replaced by two new data points: *POS Prepaid Liability* and *Online Prepaid Liability* (site *=* {{Enrollment Site or Central Office, per district setting)}}, each with the following attributes:

||Data Point Name||POS Prepaid Liability||Online Prepaid Liability||
|Source|POS Period Close|POS Period Close|
|Type|Automatic|Automatic|
|Description|Liability account for physical funds (Cash/Check) received at the Point of Sale|Liability account for digital funds (Credit/ACH) received via Online or 3rd Party sources|

----

*UPDATED: AC2 — Entry Logic: POS Prepayments (Physical Funds)* 

_Change: Defined Aggregation by Enrollment Site._

* *Given* a POS Period Close is triggered,
* *When* the system identifies prepayment transactions where {{Source = 'POS'}} and {{Amount > 0}},
* *Then* the system retrieves the *Student's Current Enrollment Site* for each transaction.
* *And* generates journal entries grouped by that *Enrollment Site*.
* *Structure per Entry:*
** *Site:* [Student Enrollment Site]
** *Debit:* Cash in Bank (Asset)
** *Credit:* POS Prepaid Liability (Liability)
** *Amount:* Sum of all POS transactions for students enrolled at this specific site.

----

*UPDATED: AC3 — Entry Logic: Online Prepayments (Digital Funds)* 

_Change: Defined Aggregation by Enrollment Site._

* *Given* a POS Period Close is triggered,
* *When* the system identifies prepayment transactions where {{Source = 'Online'}} and {{Amount > 0}},
* *Then* the system retrieves the *Student's Current Enrollment Site* for each transaction.
* *And* generates journal entries grouped by that *Enrollment Site*.
* *Structure per Entry:*
** *Site:* [Student Enrollment Site]
** *Debit:* Cash in Bank (Asset)
** *Credit:* Online Prepaid Liability (Liability)
** *Amount:* Sum of all Online transactions for students enrolled at this specific site.

----

*AC4 — Entry Logic: Bonuses*

_Given_ bonus transactions exist for a site within the current period, _When_ Period Close fires, _Then_ the system aggregates all bonus transactions for that site and period by Source, and generates one entry per Source value according to the following decision table:

||Scenario||Source Field Value||Debit||Credit||
|All bonuses for period originated at POS|{{'POS'}}|Bonus Expense Account|POS Prepaid Liability|
|All bonuses for period originated Online|{{'Online'}}|Bonus Expense Account|Online Prepaid Liability|
|Source is null, blank, or missing on any bonus|{{null}} / {{''}}|Bonus Expense Account|POS Prepaid Liability _(default)_|

*Additional rules:*

* *Posting Amount:* Sum of all bonus transactions for the site and period matching that Source value.
* If the site has POS bonuses and Online bonuses in the same period, two separate aggregated entries are generated — one per Source. *They are never combined into a single entry.*
* *Entry Description:* {{Bonus Prepayments | [Period Month Year]}}
* *Example:* {{Bonus Prepayments POS | Jan 2026}}
* *Example:* {{Bonus Prepayments Online | Jan 2026}}

----

*AC5 — Entry Logic: Refunds*

_Given_ refund transactions exist for a site within the current period, _When_ Period Close fires, _Then_ the system aggregates all refund transactions for that site and period by the Source on the *original prepayment transaction being reversed*, and generates one entry per Source value according to the following decision table:

||Scenario||Source on Original Prepayment||Debit||Credit||
|All refunds for period traced back to POS originals|{{'POS'}}|POS Prepaid Liability|Cash in Bank (Asset)|
|All refunds for period trace back to Online originals|{{'Online'}}|Online Prepaid Liability|Cash in Bank (Asset)|
|Original Source is null or missing|{{null}} / {{''}}|POS Prepaid Liability _(default)_|Cash in Bank (Asset)|

*Posting Amount:* Sum of all refund transactions for the site and period matching that Source group.

*Entry Description:* {{Refunds at Source | [Period Month Year]}}

*Example:* {{Refunds at POS | Jan 2026}}

*Example:* {{Refunds at Online Sources | Jan 2026}}

----

*UPDATED: AC6 — Aggregation Rules* _Change: Clarified the "Site" definition._

* *Given* Period Close is triggered,
* *Then* prepayment entries must aggregate at the following granularity: one entry per *configured site level* (Enrollment Site or Central Office, per district setting), per Period, per Source

----

*AC7 — Balance Validation*

_Given_ an entry is generated, _When_ the system calculates debits and credits, _Then_ Total Debits must equal Total Credits for each individual entry.
NXT-70305.desc:
As a Child Nutrition Director I want student prepayment transactions to automatically post to the general ledger split by source (POS vs. Online/3rd Party) So that I can easily reconcile physical bank deposits against merchant processor reports before exporting to the district ERP

*BUSINESS CONTEXT:*

* *Current State:* All prepayment transactions post to a single "Prepaid Liability" account regardless of how the funds were collected, mixing physical cash and digital payments in the same GL bucket.
* *Problem:* District Finance requires these to be separated. The daily cash drawer deposit needs to reconcile independently from the monthly merchant processor settlement.
* *Pain Point:* When they're lumped together, the finance officer cannot match the GL credit to either the bank deposit slip or the processor report without doing the split manually.
* *Solution:* Replace the single "Prepaid Account" configuration data point with two distinct data points — one for POS (physical funds) and one for Online (digital funds) — so that Period Close automatically posts to the correct liability account based on the source field on each transaction.

*SUCCESS METRICS:*

* *Reconciliation Time:* Reduction in time required to match GL credits to bank deposits/settlement reports.
* *Posting Accuracy:* 100% of physical cash deposits match the "POS Prepaid Liability" ledger balance.

----

*Requirements:*

*Mapping Updates*

* Remove the single "Prepaid Account" data point from the account mapping list.
* Add new data point: "POS Prepaid Liability"
** Source: POS Period Close
** Type: Automatic
** Description: Liability account for tracking physical funds accepted at the register.
* Add new data point: "Online Prepaid Liability"
** Source: POS Period Close
** Type: Automatic
** Description: Liability account for tracking digital funds received via 3rd party or web.
* System requires both new data points to be mapped to GL accounts before posting can occur.

*Entry Logic: POS Prepayments (Cash/Check)*

* Identify all positive prepayment transactions originating from POS terminals.
* Credit (Liability): Credit the mapped "POS Prepaid Liability" account.
* Debit (Asset): Debit the mapped "Cash in Bank" account.
* Posting Amount: Equals total cash/check deposits added to student balances at the POS for the period.

*Entry Logic: Online Prepayments (Digital)*

* Identify all positive prepayment transactions originating from Online/Web/App sources.
* Credit (Liability): Credit the mapped "Online Prepaid Liability" account.
* Debit (Asset): Debit the mapped "Cash in Bank" account.
* Posting Amount: Equals total digital deposits added to student balances for the period.

*Entry Logic: Bonus / Adjustments*

* Bonus/Incentive transactions post to the "Bonus Account" (Debit) and the specific Liability account based on where the bonus originated (Credit).
* Refunds/Adjustments reverse the logic based on the original source of the funds (Debit Liability, Credit Cash).

*Entry Description & Format*

* POS Format: POS Prepayments | [Period Date Range]
* Online Format: Online Prepayments | [Period Date Range]
* Example POS: POS Prepayments | 106 - Lincoln Elementary | Jan 2026
* Example Online: Online Prepayments | 106 - Lincoln Elementary | Jan 2026

*Aggregation & Trigger*

* Entries aggregate per site, per period, per source (POS vs Online).
* Post triggering occurs on period close.

*Validation Rules*

* Posting fails if either "POS Prepaid Liability" or "Online Prepaid Liability" is unmapped.
* Total Debits must equal Total Credits for each entry type.

----

*OUT OF SCOPE:*

* Integration with specific bank APIs for auto-reconciliation
* Handling of "Bad Debt" write-offs (separate user story)
* Sales tax liability calculations

*DEPENDENCIES:*

* Existing "Cash in Bank" account mapping
* Core POS and Online Payment transaction data availability

NXT-70304.summary: FIN - Automate Sales Tax Liability Posting via System-Defined Entry Types
NXT-70304.ac:
h3. AC1 — Trigger & aggregation

*Given* a POS period is closed for a site
*When* the posting job runs
*Then* the system creates *exactly one* TAXCLCTD journal entry for that *site + POS period*.

h3. AC2 — Posting date

*Given* a TAXCLCTD entry is created
*Then* Posting Date = *last day of the POS period*.

h3. AC3 — Amount calculation (net tax)

*Given* POS provides tax collected and tax reversed (void/return) for the site and POS period
*When* TAXCLCTD is generated
*Then* Posted Amount = *Tax Collected – Tax Reversed* (same POS period).

h3. AC4 — Entry structure (2 lines only)

*Given* a TAXCLCTD entry is generated
*Then* it contains *exactly two* lines:

# *Debit:* Cash in Bank = Net Sales Tax
# *Credit:* Sales Tax Owed (Liability) = Net Sales Tax

h3. AC5 — Debits equal credits

*Then* Total Debits = Total Credits for TAXCLCTD.

h3. AC6 — POS Period Close

Appears as ‘Sales Tax Collected’ in POS Period Close job corresponding to the period. 

Updated on {{2026-03-05}}

h3. AC7 — Inactive account handling

*Given* Cash in Bank or Sales Tax Owed is inactive
*When* posting runs
*Then* skip TAXCLCTD
NXT-70304.desc:
As a Child Nutrition Director I want sales tax transactions to automatically post to the general ledger using standardized entry types So that my tax liabilities are accurately recorded and ready for remittance without manual tracking

h4. Definitions

* *Meal Sales Revenue* = the tax-exclusive revenue amount (does *not* include sales tax).
* *Sales Tax* = the tax amount collected on taxable sales (a *liability*, not revenue).
* *Total / Gross Amount* = Meal Sales Revenue *plus* Sales Tax (may exist in POS, but must not be used for revenue posting).

NXT-70296.summary: FIN - Add Cost of Foods Used to Profit & Loss Report
NXT-70296.ac:
# *Report Structure*

* Add a "Cost of Food Used" subsection in the P&L.
* Group by Valuation Category (Food & Non-Food) as already defined in the Inventory Usage Report.

# *Line Items (sourced from Inventory Usage Report)*

* Line A — Beginning Inventory
* Line B — Purchases
* Line C — Additions
* Line D — Net Transfers (Transfers In − Transfers Out)
* Line E — Ending Inventory
* Line F — Cost of Food Used ({{A + B + C ± D − E = COFU}})

# *Data Dependency*

* All Cost of Sales data is fetched live from the Usage Report. The P&L does not independently calculate these values in columns A - D.

A/C updated on {{2026-04-14}} to include ‘Add to Inventory’ (referred to as Additions)
NXT-70296.desc: *As a* District Finance Director, *I want* the Profit & Loss (Revenue & Expenditure) Report to include a detailed "Cost of Sales" section that breaks down the components of Cost of Food Used (Beginning Inventory, Purchases, Ending Inventory) based on the mapped System Entry Types, *so that* I can see the calculation of expense, not just the final number.

NXT-70295.summary: FIN - Automated GL Posting from Inventory Period Close
NXT-70295.ac:
h1. Acceptance Criteria — Inventory Period-Close GL Postings

*Module:* Financial Oversight · SchoolCafe 2.0

----

h2. Trigger & Timing

*Scenario: Automatic posting on period close*

* *Given* an inventory period for a site has transactions in scope
* *When* the inventory period transitions to Closed status
* *Then* the system automatically initiates a posting job named "Inventory Posting" for that site-period with no manual trigger required

*Scenario: Posting date equals last day of period*

* *Given* an inventory period with date range 2026-01-01 to 2026-01-31
* *When* the posting job generates ledger entries
* *Then* every entry in the batch has a posting date of 2026-01-31

*Scenario: Successful posting logged*

* *Given* a posting job completes without errors
* *When* the user views the Posting Activity Log
* *Then* a log entry confirms successful completion for that site-period

*Scenario: Failed posting logged with reason*

* *Given* a posting job fails validation
* *When* the user views the Posting Activity Log
* *Then* a log entry shows the specific failure reason for that site-period

*Scenario: No partial posting on validation failure*

* *Given* a posting batch where one or more entry types fail validation
* *When* the system attempts to post
* *Then* zero entries are created for the entire site-period batch

----

h2. Account Determination

*Scenario: GL accounts resolved from Valuation Category mapping*

* *Given* a Valuation Category has an Asset GL Account and Expense GL Account configured on the Account Mapping page
* *When* the posting job generates entries for transactions in that Valuation Category
* *Then* the system uses those mapped GL accounts for the debit and credit sides per the entry type rules

*Scenario: Missing mapping blocks all posting*

* *Given* any Valuation Category present in the posting scope is missing either its Asset GL Account or Expense GL Account mapping
* *When* the inventory period transitions to Closed status
* *Then* no posting job runs for any entry type in that site-period — including entry types that do not use the Valuation Category with the missing mapping

----

h2. Entry Aggregation & Per-Site Behavior

*Scenario: One entry per type per Valuation Category per site per period*

* *Given* Site A has withdrawals in VC "Purchased Food" totaling $2,000 and VC "USDA Foods" totaling $800
* *When* the posting job runs for Site A's period
* *Then* the system creates exactly two WD entries for Site A — one for $2,000 (Purchased Food) and one for $800 (USDA Foods)

*Scenario: Entries are scoped to a single site*

* *Given* transactions exist across multiple sites in the same period
* *When* the posting job runs
* *Then* each generated entry (PURCH, WD, ADDINV, INVTRFRFR, INVTRFRTO) is scoped to exactly one site

*Scenario: Each entry is independently balanced*

* *Given* a posting job generates multiple entries
* *When* any single entry is examined
* *Then* total debits = total credits on that entry

----

h2. Balancing

*Scenario: Unbalanced entry rejects entire batch*

* *Given* a computed entry would result in debits ≠ credits (e.g., due to rounding)
* *When* the system validates the batch
* *Then* the entire posting batch for that site-period is rejected and the error is logged in the Posting Activity Log

----

h2. Audit Trail

*Scenario: Entry detail shows source traceability*

* *Given* a posted inventory entry exists in the Journal or Ledger view
* *When* the user opens the entry detail
* *Then* the detail displays:
** Entry type (e.g., "WD — Inventory Withdrawals")
** Valuation Category
** List of source transaction IDs from the inventory module that were aggregated into the entry
** Posting date
** Site

----

h2. Journal & Ledger Visibility

*Scenario: Inventory entries appear alongside other entry types*

* *Given* inventory posting has completed for a site-period
* *When* the user views the Journal or Ledger
* *Then* the posted inventory entries appear alongside POS and manual entries

*Scenario: Inventory entries are identifiable*

* *Given* a posted inventory entry exists
* *When* the user views it in the Journal or Ledger
* *Then* it displays source module ("Inventory"), entry type code and name (e.g., "WD — Withdrawals"), site, and Valuation Category

*Scenario: Entries are filterable*

* *Given* posted entries exist in the ledger
* *When* the user applies filters
* *Then* entries are filterable by site, fiscal year, financial period, GL account, and entry type

----

h2. Dirty Flag & Reposting

*Scenario: Reopened period flags the posting batch*

* *Given* an inventory period was previously posted
* *When* the period is reopened in the inventory module
* *Then* the corresponding posting batch in the Periods view reflects this state

*Scenario: Repost triggered on re-close with setting enabled*

* *Given* a previously-posted inventory period has been reopened and the user has the repost setting enabled in Periods
* *When* the period is re-closed
* *Then* the system automatically initiates a repost for that site-period

*Scenario: Repost not triggered when setting is off*
