* *Given* a previously-posted inventory period has been reopened and the user has the repost setting disabled in Periods
* *When* the period is re-closed
* *Then* no repost is initiated

*Scenario: Reversal entries use original posting date*

* *Given* a repost is initiated for a site-period
* *When* the system generates reversal entries
* *Then* each reversal entry's posting date equals the original entry's posting date (last day of the inventory period), not today's date

*Scenario: Reversal entries invert amounts*

* *Given* a repost is initiated for a site-period
* *When* the system generates reversal entries
* *Then* all amounts are the exact inverse of the originals (debits become credits, credits become debits) and each reversal references the original entry it reverses

*Scenario: New batch follows original rules*

* *Given* reversal entries have been generated
* *When* the system generates the new posting batch from updated inventory data
* *Then* the new batch follows all the same posting rules (trigger, account determination, aggregation, balancing, audit trail)

*Scenario: Original entries are immutable*

* *Given* a repost has occurred
* *When* the user views the ledger
* *Then* the original entries remain unmodified and undeleted with their original values

----

h2. Entry Types

h3. PURCH — Purchases

*Scenario: Purchase posting*

* *Given* a site-period has purchases recorded in the Usage Report
* *When* the posting job runs
* *Then* for each Valuation Category with purchases:
** Debit → Valuation Category's Asset GL Account
** Credit → Cash in Bank GL Account (single configured account, not derived from Valuation Category mapping)
** Amount = Purchases column value from Usage Report
** One entry per Valuation Category per site per period

h3. WD — Inventory Withdrawals

*Scenario: Withdrawal posting*

* *Given* a site-period has usage recorded in the Usage Report
* *When* the posting job runs
* *Then* for each Valuation Category with usage:
** Debit → Valuation Category's Expense GL Account
** Credit → Valuation Category's Asset GL Account
** Amount = Usage value from Usage Report
** One entry per Valuation Category per site per period

h3. ADDINV — Add to Inventory

*Scenario: Add-to-inventory posting*

* *Given* a site-period has add-to-inventory transactions in the Usage Report
* *When* the posting job runs
* *Then* for each Valuation Category with add-to-inventory amounts:
** Debit → Valuation Category's Asset GL Account
** Credit → Valuation Category's Expense GL Account
** Amount = Add to Inventory value from Usage Report
** One entry per Valuation Category per site per period

h3. INVTRFRFR — Transfer Out

*Scenario: Transfer-out posting*

* *Given* a site-period has transfer-out transactions in the Usage Report
* *When* the posting job runs
* *Then* for each Valuation Category with transfer-out amounts:
** Debit → Valuation Category's Expense GL Account
** Credit → Valuation Category's Asset GL Account
** Amount = Transfers In value from Usage Report
** One entry per Valuation Category per site per period

h3. INVTRFRTO — Transfer In

*Scenario: Transfer-in posting*

* *Given* a site has received items from another site during the period per the Usage Report
* *When* the posting job runs
* *Then* for each Valuation Category with transfer-in amounts:
** Debit → Valuation Category's Asset GL Account
** Credit → Valuation Category's Expense GL Account
** Amount = Transfers In value from Usage Report
** One entry per Valuation Category per site per period

----

{quote}*Data source:* All dollar amounts come from the inventory module's valuation calculations (Valuation/Usage reports). Financials does not independently calculate inventory values.{quote}

{quote}*Known limitation:* Production-record waste (Insights) uses fair market value estimates and will not reconcile to withdrawal-based postings. Waste/spoilage via withdrawal reason codes is included in WD postings.{quote}
NXT-70295.desc:
As a Child Nutrition Director, I want the system to automatically generate balanced general ledger entries for all inventory withdrawals (including waste/spoilage), manual inventory adjustments, inter-site transfers, and internal distributions when an inventory period closes, so that my food and supply costs appear in Financials without manual journal entries and are ready for export to the district ERP.

!image-20260311-132224.png|width=818,alt="image-20260311-132224.png"!

h2. Business Context

* SchoolCafe 2.0 inventory's primary period output is Valuation + Usage reporting by *Valuation Category* (called "Valuation Group" in PrimeroEdge).
* "Commodity/USDA foods" is *not* a system flag in SC 2.0. Districts define commodity classification via Valuation Categories. 
* Receipt valuation already reflects discounts and charges in the final valuation amount. Fee breakdowns (delivery, processing) exist in underlying tables but are not surfaced as separate reportable columns today. *Financials treats the final valuation amount as the source of truth; it does not decompose it.*
* Waste/spoilage recorded via withdrawal reason codes is *included in withdrawal postings*. Production-record waste (Insights) uses fair market value estimates and *will not reconcile* to withdrawal-based postings. This is a *documented product limitation*, not a bug.

🔄 *Data source clarification:* All dollar amounts for inventory postings come from the inventory module's valuation calculations as reflected in the Valuation/Usage reports. Financials does not independently calculate inventory values; it consumes what inventory provides.

h2. Scope

h3. In Scope

* Period-close batch postings for 5 entry types: PURCH, WD, ADDINV, INVTRFRFR, INVTRFRTO.
* System-defined entry types only (no user-created rules for inventory).
* Per-site ledger entries for PURCH, WD, ADDINV, INVTRFRFR, INVTRFRTO.
* Validation gates before posting: period state, mapping completeness, balancing.
* Reposting with reversal when a previously-posted period is reopened and re-closed.

h2. Requirements

h3. R1. Trigger and Timing

🔄 *R1.1 — Trigger mechanism:* Posting is initiated automatically by the system when an inventory period transitions to *Closed* status for a site. There is no manual "Run Posting" button as of now.

🔄 *R1.2 — User notification:* When posting completes (success or failure), the system logs the result in the *Posting Activity Log*. If posting fails, the failure reason appears in the log entry. The name of the job is ‘Inventory Posting.’

*R1.3 — Posting date:* The posting date for all period-close entry types equals the *last day* of the inventory period (e.g., period 2026-01-01 to 2026-01-31 → posting date = 2026-01-31).

*R1.4 — No partial posting:* If any entry type within a posting batch fails validation, the *entire batch* for that site-period is rejected. No entries are created. The Posting Activity Log records the failure with the specific reason.

h3. R3. Account Determination

*R3.1 — Mapping source:* GL accounts for inventory postings are determined by the *Valuation Category → GL Account mappings* configured on the Account Mapping page in Financials.

🔄 *R3.2 — Required mappings per entry type:* Each Valuation Category used by any transaction in the posting scope must have Asset GL Account AND Expense GL Account.

h3. R4. Entry Aggregation and Per-Site Behavior

🔄 *R4.1 — Aggregation level:* The system creates *one ledger entry per entry type per Valuation Category per site per period.* For example, if Site A has withdrawals of items in VC "Purchased Food" totaling $2,000 and VC "USDA Foods" totaling $800, posting creates two WD entries for Site A: one for $2,000 and one for $800. Each entry is independently balanced (debits = credits).

*R4.2 — Per-site entry types:* PURCH, WD, ADDINV, INVTRFRFR, INVTRFRTO each create entries scoped to a single site.

h3. R5. Balancing and Audit Trail

*R5.1 — Balance rule:* Every created ledger entry must have total debits = total credits. If a computed entry would be unbalanced (e.g., due to rounding), the system must reject the entire posting batch and log the error.

🔄 *R5.2 — Audit trail — what the user sees:* When a user views a posted inventory entry in the Journal or Ledger view and opens the entry detail (via row click or detail action), the detail view displays:

* The *entry type* (e.g., "WD — Inventory Withdrawals").
* The *Valuation Category* the entry relates to.
* The *posting date* and *site*.

h3. R6. Journal and Ledger Visibility

*R6.1 — Appearance:* Posted inventory entries appear in both the Journal and Ledger views alongside other entry types (POS entries, manual entries, etc.).

🔄 *R6.2 — Identification:* Each inventory-generated entry must display:

* *Source module:* "Inventory" (so users can distinguish it from POS or manual entries).
* *Entry type code and name:* e.g., "WD — Withdrawals"
* *Site*
* *Valuation Category*

*R6.3 — Filtering:* Entries are filterable by site, fiscal year, financial period, GL account, and entry type. 

h3. R7. Dirty Flag and Reposting

*R7.1 — Dirty detection:* If a previously-posted inventory period is reopened in the inventory module, the corresponding posting batch in Periods is updated to display this.

*R7.3 — Repost trigger:* When the inventory period is re-closed, the system automatically initiates a repost for that site-period if the user has the setting in Periods on, otherwise no action needed.

*R7.4 — Repost mechanics:*

# System generates *reversal entries* for every entry in the original posting batch. Each reversal entry has:
#* Posting date = *the same posting date as the original entry* (last day of the inventory period), NOT today's date. 🔄
#* All amounts with reversed signs (debits become credits, credits become debits).
#* A reference to the original entry it reverses.
# System generates a *new posting batch* from the updated inventory data, following all the same rules (R1–R6).
# The original entries are *never edited or deleted*. They remain in the ledger with their original values.

----

h2. Entry Types — Detailed Specifications

h3. ET1 — Purchases (PURCH)

||Attribute||Value||
|*Posting date*|Last day of inventory period.|
|*Debit*|Valuation Category → Asset GL Account|
|*Credit*|Cash in Bank GL Account|
|*Value source*|Purchases column in Usage Report|
|*Aggregation*|One entry per Valuation Category per site per period.|
|*Note*|The credit side (Cash in Bank) is a single configured GL account, not derived from the Valuation Category mapping. This is the only entry type where debit and credit draw from different mapping sources.|

h3. ET2 — Inventory Withdrawals (WD) (equivalent to Usage)

||Attribute||Value||
|*Posting date*|Last day of inventory period.|
|*Debit*|Valuation Category → Expense GL Account|
|*Credit*|Valuation Category → Asset GL Account|
|*Value source*|Usage in Usage Report.|
|*Aggregation*|One entry per Valuation Category per site per period.|

h3. ET3 — Add to Inventory (ADDINV)

||Attribute||Value||
|*Posting date*|Last day of inventory period.|
|*Debit*|Valuation Category → Asset GL Account|
|*Credit*|Valuation Category → Expense GL Account|
|*Value Source*|Add to Inventory in Usage Report|
|*Aggregation*|One entry per Valuation Category per site per period.|

h3. ET4 — Transfer Out (INVTRFRFR)

||Attribute||Value||
|*Posting date*|Last day of inventory period.|
|*Debit*|Valuation Category → Expense GL Account|
|*Credit*|Valuation Category → Asset GL Account|
|*Value source*|Transfers In from Usage Report|
|*Aggregation*|One entry per Valuation Category per site per period.|

h3. ET5 — Transfer In (INVTRFRTO)

||Attribute||Value||
|*Includes*|Items received at this site from another site during the period.|
|*Posting date*|Last day of inventory period.|
|*Debit*|Valuation Category → Asset GL Account|
|*Credit*|Valuation Category → Expense GL Account|
|*Value rule*|Transfers In from Usage Report|
|*Aggregation*|One entry per Valuation Category per site per period.|

NXT-70194.ac:
h2. 1. Revenue Category Groups

The system displays 77 system revenue data points, grouped by category (not by program):

* Student Meal Sales – Free (6)
* Student Meal Sales – Reduced (2)
* Student Meal Sales – Paid (3)
* Adult & Staff Meal Sales – Taxable (18)
* A La Carte – Student / Tax Exempt (7)
* A La Carte – Adult/Staff/Visitor / Taxable (21)
* Federal Reimbursements (7)
* State Reimbursements (7)
* State Programs (2)
* Operating Revenue (3)
* Year-End Close (1)
* Other Revenue (user-created items)

----

h2. 2. Program Filter

A new "Program" dropdown filter is added to the existing filter bar, positioned after the Entry Type dropdown and before the Search input.

||Option||Label||
|All Programs|Default selection|
|SBP|School Breakfast|
|NSLP|National School Lunch|
|ASP|After School Snack|
|CACFP|Child & Adult Care|
|SFSP|Summer Food Service|
|SMP|Special Milk|
|FFVP|Fresh Fruit & Veg|
|State Programs|State Programs|

Behavior:

* Follows the same Apply-to-filter pattern as the existing Base Type, Status, and Entry Type filters.
* When a specific program is selected and applied, only data points associated with that program are shown across all category groups.
* Category groups containing matching data points auto-expand. Groups with no matching data points collapse or are hidden.
* Each visible category group header updates its account count to show: "{filtered count} of {total}" (e.g., "3 of 7").
* A context banner appears below the filter bar when a program filter is active:
** Program name
** Total filtered data point count with active/inactive breakdown (e.g., "12 data points — 10 active, 2 inactive")
** "Clear Filter" action: resets Program to "All Programs" and applies
* The context banner is informational only — it does not contain any actions.
* Program filter composes with existing filters: if Base Type = Revenue and Program = NSLP are both applied, results show only Revenue-typed NSLP data points.

Search behavior with Program filter active: Search results respect the applied Program filter. When search returns results, groups containing matches auto-expand; groups without matches collapse or hide.

----

h2. 3. Save Warnings

This extends the existing batch save behavior. When the user clicks "Save Changes" in the existing save bar:

* If any active data points remain unmapped, display a warning dialog before committing:
** Message: "You have {count} active data points that are not mapped to a GL account. Unmapped data points will not generate journal entries during posting. Save anyway?"
** Actions: *Save Anyway* / *Go Back*
* If all active data points are mapped, save proceeds without additional prompts.

----

h2. 4. Discard Changes

A *Discard Changes* button is added to the existing save bar, visible whenever pending changes exist. This provides a way to abandon all staged changes without needing to trigger the navigation guard.

* Confirmation dialog: "Discard all unsaved changes?"
* Actions: *Discard* / *Cancel*
* On Discard: Reverts all pending changes to last saved state. Toast: "All changes discarded."

----

h2. 5. Navigation Guard

If the user has any pending unsaved changes (from individual edits in the drawer or inline mapping) and attempts to navigate away from the page:

* Dialog message: "You have unsaved changes that will be lost."
* Actions: *Discard & Leave* (navigates away, reverts all pending changes) / *Stay on Page* (returns to page with pending changes intact)
NXT-70194.desc:
*Parent Page:* Account Mapping (Financials > Configuration > General Ledger Configuration)

*Scope:* This story adds revenue-specific category grouping and a Program filter to the existing Account Mapping page. It does not modify the existing page structure, edit drawer, inline GL mapping, Rules drawer, progress banner, filters (Base Type, Status, Entry Type, Search), section dividers, or batch save behavior.

NXT-70193.ac:
h2. 1. Entering Bulk Actions Mode

A "Bulk Actions" button is added to the page action area, near the existing "Add Data Point" button.

Clicking "Bulk Actions" enters selection mode:

* The "Bulk Actions" button changes to a highlighted/active state.
* A context banner appears at the top of the table area: "Select groups to apply bulk actions. [Exit Bulk Actions ✕]"
* A checkbox appears on every category group header across all sections.
* A "select all" checkbox appears on each section divider row (Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Revenue, Expenses). Checking a section divider selects every group in that section. Unchecking deselects them all. If only some groups in a section are checked, the section checkbox shows an indeterminate (—) state.
* Edit buttons, inline mapping, and Rules buttons are visually dimmed and cannot be clicked while in bulk actions mode.
* *These are override operations — they apply uniformly to all data points in the group, overwriting any existing mappings or statuses.*

----

h2. 2. Group Selection

* Clicking a group's checkbox selects it. The group doesn't need to be expanded.
* Users can select groups from different sections at the same time.
* If filters are active, checkboxes only appear on groups that are currently visible. Bulk actions only affect data points the user can see on screen.
* At least one group must be selected before any bulk action button becomes clickable.

----

h2. 3. Floating Action Bar

When one or more groups are selected, a floating action bar appears pinned to the bottom of the screen:

*"{X} groups selected · {Y} data points — [Map All] [Activate All] [Deactivate All] | [Cancel]"*

----

h2. 4. Bulk Actions

h3. Map All

Map All only works when every selected group belongs to the same section (e.g., all Revenue, or all Assets). If the user selects groups from different sections, Map All is grayed out with a tooltip: "Select groups within the same section to map to a single GL account."

Opens a confirmation modal:

* Header: "Bulk Map {Y} Data Points"
* Shows a list of every selected group by name and how many data points are in each one.
* Message: "All data points in the selected groups will be mapped to the chosen GL account, replacing any existing mappings. Future transactions will route through this account."
* GL account typeahead selector, showing only accounts that match the section's type (same filtering as the edit drawer).
* Actions: *Apply* / *Cancel*

On Apply: Maps every data point in the selected groups — including inactive ones — to the selected GL account. Changes go into the existing pending changes queue and don't commit until the user clicks "Save Changes."

After Apply: Toast: "Mapped {Y} data points across {X} groups to {GL Account Name}." Checkboxes uncheck. Page stays in bulk actions mode.

h3. Activate All

* Works no matter which sections the selected groups come from.
* Confirmation dialog: "Activate all {Y} data points across {X} selected groups? Activated data points become eligible for posting." Shows the list of selected groups.
* Actions: *Activate* / *Cancel*
* Changes go into the pending queue. Toast: "Activated {Y} data points across {X} groups." Checkboxes uncheck. Mode persists.

h3. Deactivate All

* Works no matter which sections the selected groups come from.
* Confirmation dialog: "Deactivate all {Y} data points across {X} selected groups? Deactivated data points will not generate journal entries during posting." Shows the list of selected groups.
* Actions: *Deactivate* / *Cancel*
* Changes go into the pending queue. Toast: "Deactivated {Y} data points across {X} groups." Checkboxes uncheck. Mode persists.

----

h2. 5. Feedback & Change Tracking

* -All bulk changes go into the existing pending changes queue — nothing commits until the user clicks "Save Changes."-
* All changes show up in the configuration change history after saving.
* -Pending indicator dots appear on affected rows, same as they do for individual edits.-

----

h2. 6. Exiting Bulk Actions Mode

Two ways to exit: "Cancel" in the floating bar OR "Exit Bulk Actions ✕"

On exit:

* Checkboxes disappear. Floating bar and banner disappear. Edit buttons, inline mapping, and Rules buttons go back to normal.
* Modified on {{2026-02-17}} after discussion with developer.

----

h2. 7. Status Change Behavior

Changes only commit when the user clicks "Save Changes." 

*Note: Bulk action counts and operations are filter-aware, so when search or any table filter is active, they only reflect and apply to the currently visible matching data points rather than the entire underlying groups (updated on* {{2026-02-11}})
NXT-70193.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-70111.summary: Auto-Reprocess Failed Posting Jobs After GL Mapping Is Completed
NXT-70111.ac:
* When a user saves missing GL mappings in {{Financials > Account Mapping}}, the system automatically reruns all previously failed posting jobs whose failure reason was missing mapping.
** This happens when the “
* The rerun is system-triggered (no manual retry), and each job logs the new result (success or new failure reason) in Posting Activity Log.
NXT-70111.desc: As a district finance user, when I complete GL mapping while POS/Inventory periods were closed, I want previously failed posting jobs caused by missing mapping to rerun automatically, so I do not need to manually retry each job.

NXT-70093.summary: Base Type Fallback When Account Category Is Blank
NXT-70093.ac:
*Fallback Logic*

* If a GL Account row in the import file has a blank or empty Account Category value, the system must use the Account Base Type column value from that row to assign the account's base type directly
* In this scenario, the rule "ignore the GL Account Base Type column during import" does not apply — the Base Type column serves as the fallback when Category is not provided
* Valid Base Type values for fallback: Asset, Liability, Revenue, Expense, Fund Balance
* "Equity" is accepted as a valid alias and maps to Fund Balance internally
* Matching is case-insensitive and leading/trailing whitespace must be trimmed before validation (e.g., "revenue", "REVENUE", " Revenue " are all valid)

*Default Category Assignment for Fallback Accounts*

* When a GL Account is imported via Base Type fallback, the system must assign it to a default category matching that base type
* The system must first check whether a category with that base type already exists on the Account Categories page
** If a matching category exists: assign the account to that category
** If no matching category exists: auto-create a default category named "[Base Type]" (e.g., "Revenue", "Expense") with the corresponding base type, and assign the account to it
* Auto-created default categories appear on the Account Categories page like any user-created category and are subject to the same logic when editing and deleting them
* Multiple accounts imported via the same base type fallback share the same default category
* Reassignment shares the same logic as normal account categories 

*Error States*

* If both Account Category AND Account Base Type are blank or empty for a GL Account row, import the row and flag it as Needs Fixing with inline error text "Category assignment required"
** The base type will then be derived from that category 
* If the Base Type fallback value does not match any valid base type or recognized alias after trimming and case normalization, import the row and flag it as Needs Fixing with inline error text "Invalid base type"

*Re-Import Behavior*

* On subsequent re-import, if the same Account Code is re-imported with a Category value provided, the Category assignment and its derived Base Type will overwrite the previous fallback Base Type
* On subsequent re-import, if the same Account Code is re-imported again with a blank Category, the system follows the same fallback logic described above using the Base Type column value from the new file
NXT-70093.desc: *Intent:* Category is the single source of truth for Base Type. The Base Type column exists only as a fallback when Category is not provided, ensuring every imported account has a usable base type.

NXT-70075.summary: FIN - Period Context in Action Modals
NXT-70075.ac:
* All period action modals (Close, Reopen, Lock, etc.) must include period context
* Format: "Are you sure you would like to [action] the [Period Name] ([Start Date] - [End Date]) Financials period?"
* Example: "Are you sure you would like to re-open the September 2025 (Sep 1 - Sep 30, 2025) Financials period?"
* Date format: MMM D - MMM D, YYYY (or MMM D, YYYY - MMM D, YYYY if spanning months)
* "Financials period" appears in all modals to distinguish from POS/Inventory periods
* Bulk actions: If user selects multiple periods, use generic text without listing specific periods: "Are you sure you would like to [action] the selected Financials periods?"
* Apply to all existing action modals: Close, Reopen, Lock, Unlock, Delete
NXT-70075.desc:
*Description:* Display specific period information in all action modals so users understand exactly which Financial period they're modifying.

*Given* I am performing an action on a Financial period
*When* a confirmation modal appears
*Then* the modal includes the period name, date range, and period type
NXT-70075.epic_summary: Financial Period Intelligence — Automated Adjustments, Notifications & Pending Entry Resolution

NXT-70074.summary: FIN - Warning When Closing Period with Open Sites
NXT-70074.ac:
# Modal appears when user clicks Close action and any sites remain open
# Modal title: "Some sites have not finished posting"
# Modal body text: "[X] sites have not closed their POS or Inventory periods. You may need to make adjustments once those sites finish posting."
# X = clickable count linking to site status drilldown (same table as FIN-Sites Drilldown story)
# Clicking count opens embedded table within modal showing Site | POS | Inventory statuses
# Modal actions: [Cancel] [Close Anyway]
# Cancel returns to Periods page without closing period
# Close Anyway proceeds with closing despite open sites
# If all sites are closed, no warning modal appears
NXT-70074.desc:
*Description:* Prevent users from accidentally closing Financial periods when upstream site periods haven't finished posting, which may require subsequent adjustments.

*Given* I am closing a Financial period
*When* one or more sites have not closed their POS or Inventory periods
*Then* I see a warning modal before the period closes
NXT-70074.epic_summary: Financial Period Intelligence — Automated Adjustments, Notifications & Pending Entry Resolution

NXT-70073.summary: Sites Column Drilldown on Periods Page
NXT-70073.ac:
*Given* I am on the Periods page viewing Financial periods
*When* I see a period with a Sites column showing "X Open, Y Closed"
*Then* I can click the text to open a popover showing individual site statuses

*Acceptance Criteria:*

# Sites column displays format: "X Open, Y Closed" as clickable text (use link styling)
# Clicking opens a popover positioned below the cell
# Popover contains a table with columns: Site | POS | Inventory
# Each row shows:
#* Site name (left-aligned)
#* POS status: ✓ Closed or ○ Open
#* Inventory status: ✓ Closed or ○ Open
# Site is considered "Closed" only when BOTH POS AND Inventory show ✓ Closed
# Sites sorted alphabetically by name
# Click outside popover or ESC key to close
# Popover max height 400px with vertical scroll if needed
NXT-70073.desc: Allow users to view site-level period status breakdown to identify which upstream periods are blocking Financial period closure.
NXT-70073.epic_summary: Financial Period Intelligence — Automated Adjustments, Notifications & Pending Entry Resolution

NXT-70069.ac:
* A standalone banner appears between the summary cards and the Ready to Post section when automatic posting is not enabled
* Banner text: "You're posting manually. Districts with automatic posting enabled only visit this page when something needs attention."
* Right side shows "Enable automatic posting →" link and an X dismiss button
* Dismissing hides the banner for the session (persists via local storage)
* Banner never renders if automatic posting is already enabled
* Banner is visually distinct (light indigo background, left accent bar) but not alarming — it's a nudge, not an alert
* Page subtitle updated to: "Your posting jobs, tracked and audit-ready."
NXT-70069.desc: As a CN Director who posts manually, I want a clear nudge toward automatic posting so I understand the time savings, without being blocked or annoyed.
NXT-70069.epic_summary: FIN - Posting Activity Log: Ready-to-Post Workflow & Operational Awareness

NXT-70068.summary: FIN - Enhanced Completed Job Detail Panels
NXT-70068.ac:
* Expanded completed POS job panels show an "Entries by GL Entry Type" table with columns: entry type, debit/credit accounts, entry count, total amount
* Reimbursement Receivable is the first row in the table, visually prominent (bold text)
* Sales Tax Collected appears as the last row only if the district has sales tax configured; styled as conditional (muted italic)
* A footnote below the table reads: "Entry types shown depend on your district's configuration."
* An "Export Entries" button (secondary style, download icon) appears in the top-right of the expanded detail area
* Clicking Export shows a dropdown with format options: CSV, PDF, ERP Format
* Completed job detail panels include an inline success message: "Entries posted and audit-ready ✓"
* The Triggered By column shows a user icon before the name when the job was triggered manually (not "System")
NXT-70068.desc: As a CN Director preparing for export, I want to see the full entry breakdown for a completed job — including reimbursements and conditional entries — and export directly from the detail panel.
NXT-70068.epic_summary: FIN - Posting Activity Log: Ready-to-Post Workflow & Operational Awareness

NXT-70067.summary: FIN - Failed Job Prioritization & Attention Routing
NXT-70067.ac:
* Failed jobs are pinned to the top of the job history table regardless of sort order
* A "Needs Attention" divider row appears above the first failed job; a "Recent Jobs" divider appears below the last failed job, before completed/running jobs
* Dividers only render when failed jobs exist; invisible when all jobs are healthy
* The "Failed" filter chip shows a red badge with the count of failed jobs (e.g., "Failed 2")
* Clicking the Needs Attention summary card scrolls to the table and activates the Failed filter
* Expanded failed job detail panels show: error message, a direct help line ("This one needs a fix before it's audit-ready. Here's what to do →"), and a Resolve Issue link
NXT-70067.desc: As a CN Director at a large district, I want failed jobs to surface immediately so I don't have to scroll through hundreds of completed jobs to find the ones that need fixing.
NXT-70067.epic_summary: FIN - Posting Activity Log: Ready-to-Post Workflow & Operational Awareness

NXT-70066.ac:
* Replace the four Phase 1 cards (Total Jobs, Completed, Failed, Running) with three cards in a horizontal grid
* Card 1 — Posting Progress: label is the current fiscal period name (uppercase), value shows "X of Y sites posted", thin green progress bar underneath proportional to completion. A site counts as "posted" only when all its posting types are complete. When all posted: "All Y sites posted ✓"
* Card 2 — Needs Attention: value shows failed/unresolved count in red. Clickable — scrolls to and filters the job history table to failed jobs. When zero: "No issues ✓" in green
* Card 3 — Posted This Period: value shows total dollar amount of ledger entries for the current period in green. Subtitle shows delta vs prior period (e.g., "↑ $4,200 vs Dec 2025")
* Cards are compact (matching Phase 1 density), white background, text only — no icons, no illustrations, no decorative graphics
* The old job counts (total, completed, failed, running) move to the job history table section header as inline muted text: "Posting Jobs (Last 30 Days) · 25 total · 21 completed · 2 failed · 1 running"
* Cards update dynamically after posting actions
NXT-70066.desc: As a CN Director, I want the top-of-page cards to tell me what matters right now — progress, problems, and dollars — not system job counts.
NXT-70066.epic_summary: FIN - Posting Activity Log: Ready-to-Post Workflow & Operational Awareness

NXT-70065.ac:
* A "Ready to Post" section appears between the summary cards and the job history table
* Section is a collapsible card with a left-border accent and a badge showing pending item count
* Section is expanded by default; collapses on header click
* Each pending item shows as a row: period name, site count, type badge (POS Posting / Inventory Posting), and two actions: Review and Post All
* Items aggregate one row per period per type, regardless of district size
* Clicking Review expands an inline panel below that row with a checklist table: checkbox, site name, expected entry types, estimated amount
* All sites checked by default; "Select All / Deselect All" toggle and search-by-site-name filter are present
* "Post Selected (X sites)" button updates count dynamically as user checks/unchecks
* Clicking Post All or Post Selected opens a confirmation modal showing a preview table of entries (site, entry types, estimated amount, total)
* On confirm: pending item animates out, a new Running job appears at the top of the job history table with Triggered By showing the current user name, badge count decrements
* When 2+ items are pending, a "Post All Pending" batch button appears in the section header; confirmation modal shows a period-level summary with totals
* When no items are pending, section shows an empty state: "All caught up. Every closed period has been posted." with muted quip underneath
* Footer includes a one-liner description when items are present: "These periods are closed and ready for ledger entries. Review or post to stay on track."
NXT-70065.desc: As a CN Director, I want to see which periods are closed and waiting for ledger entries so I can review and post them without navigating away from the Activity Log.
NXT-70065.epic_summary: FIN - Posting Activity Log: Ready-to-Post Workflow & Operational Awareness

NXT-69981.summary: FIN - Backend Posting Logic for FO
NXT-69981.ac:
h3. Sales Tax Posting (Taxable Sales)

* Taxable sales include Adult, Staff, Visitor, and taxable A La Carte transactions.
* For each taxable transaction line:
** Total Amount = Revenue Portion + Sales Tax Portion
** Credit Revenue Portion to the mapped revenue GL account selected via Account Mapping UI.
** Credit Sales Tax Portion to the system-configured "Sales Tax Owed" liability GL account.
** Debit is Cash and/or Prepaid based on payment method and settlement logic.
* Sales tax must never be credited to the mapped revenue GL account.

h3. Payment Method / Settlement (Debit Side)

* For POS period close postings:
** Debit includes Cash in Bank and/or Prepaid Account depending on payment method mix.
** Credit includes the relevant revenue account(s) mapped via Account Mapping UI.

h3. Deposit Variances (Cash Over/Short)

* When cash over/short differences are detected during Shift Close:
** Post the variance amount to the "Deposit Variances" data point.
** The mapped GL account for "Deposit Variances" is used for posting (as configured in Account Mapping).
NXT-69981.desc:
Scope

* This story defines backend posting behavior for transactions that reference FO-related data points.

NXT-69972.ac:
h3. Notification Trigger

*Given* the user has access to Financial Oversight, *When* the user logs in (or navigates to Financial Oversight for the first time in a session), *Then:*

* System checks for posting jobs since the user's last login
* If new activity exists, a notification banner displays

h3. Notification Content

*Given* there are new completed jobs since last login, *Then:* notification displays: "X posting jobs completed since your last login. View Activity Log →"

*Given* there are failed jobs since last login, *Then:* notification displays with warning style: "X posting jobs failed. View Activity Log →"

*Given* there is a mix of completed, failed, and requires-review jobs, *Then:* notification prioritizes warnings: "X jobs need attention, Y completed. View Activity Log →"

h3. Notification Behavior

*Given* the user clicks "View Activity Log →", *Then:* user navigates to Posting Activity Log, filtered to jobs since last login.

*Given* the user dismisses the notification, *Then:* notification does not reappear until next login with new activity.

*Given* no new posting jobs since last login, *Then:* no notification displays.

h3. Activity Log Error Messages

*Given* a posting job fails due to missing GL account mapping, *Then:* expanded row shows: "Missing GL account mapping for [Account Name]"

*Given* a posting job fails due to closed financial period, *Then:* expanded row shows: "Financial period [Month Year] is closed. Reopen the period to post."

*Given* a posting job fails due to missing reimbursement rates, *Then:* expanded row shows: "Reimbursement rates not configured for [Program Name] - [Federal/State]"

*Given* a posting job has status "Requires Review" due to existing system-generated entry, *Then:* expanded row shows: "A system-generated posting already exists for [Period]. Review and manually reverse if needed before reposting."

*Given* a posting job has status "Requires Review" due to existing claim posting, *Then:* expanded row shows: "A posting already exists for this claim. Review and manually reverse if needed before reposting."

h3. Job Types Included

Notification includes all posting job types:

* POS Posting
* Inventory Posting
* Reimbursement Posting

h3. Permissions

Access as per roles and permissions.

h2. Success Criteria

* Finance users are immediately aware of posting activity without manually checking Activity Log
* Failed and review-needed jobs surface prominently
* Clear, actionable error messages guide resolution
* Notification is non-intrusive and dismissible
NXT-69972.desc: As a finance user, I want to see notifications when I log in about new, failed, or review-needed posting jobs since my last login, so that I can quickly address issues and stay informed.

NXT-69959.ac:
h2. 1. Data Points & Grouping

The system displays 77 system revenue data points, grouped by category (not by program):

* Student Meal Sales – Free (6)
* Student Meal Sales – Reduced (2)
* Student Meal Sales – Paid (3)
* Adult & Staff Meal Sales – Taxable (18)
* A La Carte – Student / Tax Exempt (7)
* A La Carte – Adult/Staff/Visitor / Taxable (21)
* Federal Reimbursements (7)
* State Reimbursements (7)
* State Programs (2)
* Operating Revenue (3)
* Year-End Close (1)
* Other Revenue (user-created items)

----

h2. 2. Default Table Display

* Each category group is collapsed by default.
* Each category group header displays the group name and total item count.
* Each data point row displays: Name, Status (Active / Inactive), Created By, Entry Type (Automatic / Manual), Program.

----

h2. 3. Search matches against Data Point Name

* Search matches against *Data Point Name* and fields only.
* Matching is case-insensitive, substring-based (e.g., "NSLP" matches any data point whose name contains "NSLP").
* Search does not match against Description, Group name, or ProgramType. Program filtering is handled by the dedicated Program filter.
* *Real-time filtering:* Table results update as the user types. No Apply click is required for search.
* Search results respect any currently applied filters. (e.g., if Program = NSLP is already applied and the user searches "Paid," results show only NSLP data points matching "Paid.")
* Clearing the search field immediately removes the search constraint and returns results to the current applied filter state.

----

h2. 4. Filtered View & Program Filter

*When the Program filter is set to a specific program (not "All Programs") and filters are applied:*

* *Program filter options:*
** All Programs (default)
** SBP – School Breakfast
** NSLP – National School Lunch
** ASP – After School Snack
** CACFP – Child & Adult Care
** SFSP – Summer Food Service
** SMP – Special Milk
** FFVP – Fresh Fruit & Veg
** State Programs
* All category groups auto-expand.
* Each category group header displays the filtered item count. Display: "{filtered count} of {total}" (e.g., "3 of 7").
* A banner appears below the filter bar containing:
** Program name.
** Filtered meal sales revenue count (meal sales and a la carte accounts only — excludes reimbursement accounts), showing active and inactive counts for the current filter set.
** *Clear Filter* action: resets Program to "All Programs" and applies.

*-Program-level bulk actions-* -(always visible on the banner):-

* *-Map All:-* -Opens the bulk map modal scoped to the selected program's data points.-
** -Confirmation Dialog: “--_This will map all {count} data points in {Group Name} to the selected GL account, replacing any existing mappings. Future transactions for this group will route through this account."_- 
** -Actions:- *-Apply-* -/- *-Cancel-*
* *-Activate All:-* -Activates all data points in the selected program.-
** -Confirmation dialog: "Activate all {count} {Program Name} data points? They will become eligible for posting."-
** -Actions:- *-Activate-* -/- *-Cancel-*
* *-Deactivate All:-* -Deactivates all data points in the selected program.-
** -Confirmation dialog: "Deactivate all {count} {Program Name} data points? Deactivated data points will not generate journal entries during posting."-
** -Actions:- *-Deactivate-* -/- *-Cancel-*

*-Feedback:-* -After any program-level bulk action completes, show a toast: "{Action} applied to {count} data points."-

----

h2. -5. Category Group Actions-

-Each category group header exposes three persistent actions:- *-Map All-*-,- *-Activate All-*-, and- *-Deactivate All-*-. All three are always visible regardless of the current state of items within the group. These are override operations — they apply uniformly to all data points in the group, overwriting any existing mappings or statuses.-

*-Map All (Bulk Mapping)-*

* -Opens a confirmation modal:-
** -Message:- -_"Map all data points in {Group Name} to the same GL account? All future transactions for this group will route through the selected account."_-
** -A single GL account selector.-
** -Actions:- *-Apply-* -/- *-Cancel-*
* -On Apply: Maps every data point in the group — including inactive items — to the selected GL account. These are staged as pending changes and do not commit until the user saves.-

*-Activate All-*

* -Confirmation dialog:- -_"Activate all {count} data points in {Group Name}? Activated data points become eligible for posting."_-
* -Actions:- *-Activate-* -/- *-Cancel-*
* -On Activate: Sets all data points in the group to Active. Staged as pending changes.-

*-Deactivate All-*

* -Confirmation dialog:- -_"Deactivate all {count} data points in {Group Name}? Deactivated data points will not generate journal entries during posting."_-
* -Actions:- *-Deactivate-* -/- *-Cancel-*
* -On Deactivate: Sets all data points in the group to Inactive. Staged as pending changes.-

*-Feedback & Change Tracking-*

* -After any bulk action, show an inline toast:- -_"{Action} applied to {count} data points in {Group Name}."_-
* -All bulk changes are reflected in the configuration change history.-

----

h2. 6. Rules Drawer

*Access:* Clicking "Rules" on a data point row opens a side drawer.

*Content:* Displays the journal entry rule(s) for the selected data point.

*Required rule formats:*

||Data Point Type||Rule Name||Debit||Credit||
|Meal Sales|Meal Sale|Cash in Bank and/or Prepaid Account|Revenue account|
|A La Carte|A La Carte Sale|Cash in Bank and/or Prepaid Account|Revenue account|

*Highlighting:* The selected data point's own account is highlighted in the debit/credit display.

View the file in the description for the final list to be used. 

----

h2. -7. Unsaved Changes-

*-Navigation guard:-* -If the user has any pending unsaved changes (bulk maps, individual mapping edits, status changes) and attempts to navigate away, the system displays a confirmation dialog.-

* -Dialog message: "You have unsaved changes that will be lost."-
* -Actions:- *-Discard & Leave-* -(navigates away, reverts all pending changes) /- *-Stay on Page-* -(returns to page with pending changes intact)-
NXT-69959.desc:
As a School Business Official (SBO), when I access the Account Mapping screen, I want to see all  revenue data points organized by eligibility or program, so that I can complete my GL mapping quickly using bulk actions.

NXT-69933.summary: FIN - Post Reimbursements Due on Claim Creation (with Period Close Fallback)
NXT-69933.ac:
h2. How Entries Are Structured

All postings post at the site level with two ledger entries: 

|| ||Debit||Credit||
|Federal|Due from Federal Funds (Asset)|Reimbursement Program Revenue – Federal|
|State|Due from State Funds (Asset)|Reimbursement Program Revenue – State|

Postings are created at the site level, per program, per funding source (State/Federal), per meal type. For each eligible funding source, create one debit/credit pair (Federal and/or State).

Entry description: "[Program Name] Reimbursement Claim" (or "Accrual" or "Reversal").

----

h2. When Posting Is Allowed

Two conditions, checked per site:

# *All POS periods for that site within the claim month are closed.* Claims are always monthly. If POS periods are weekly, all weeks in that month must be closed for that site. Entries are made for sites as their POS periods are closed, referencing their site-level sub accounts in the ledger/journal. 
# *The financial period containing the last day of the claim month is open.* This is a job-level gate. If it's closed, nothing posts for any site.
# All Reimbursement-related data points are mapped, along with the other active data points that require mapping. 

*The “Posted Date” is always the last day of the claim month.*

----

h2. Two Posting Paths

*Path 1 — User files a claim.* The user clicks "Create Claim" or "Regenerate Claim" at any time (claims are not gated by POS period status). The system processes each site: if entries already exist, reverse then repost; if not, just post. 

*Path 2 — No claim exists.* When a site's last POS period for the month closes and no claim exists, the system posts an estimated accrual (meal counts × configured rates) for that site. This fires per site, not district-wide. If the user eventually files a claim, the reversal logic cleans up the accrual automatically.

----

h2. How Reversals Work

Whenever a posting job finds existing entries for the same site + program + funding source + claim period, it reverses them before posting new ones.

* The lookup does *not* filter by meal type — it matches on site + program + funding source + claim period to catch all prior entries.
* The reversal uses the original's snapshotted GL accounts. The new posting uses the current GL configuration.
* The reversal and new posting succeed or fail together.

----

h2. What Shows in the Activity Log

||Label||When||Triggered By||
|Reimbursement Claim|User filed or regenerated a claim|System|
|Reimbursement Accrual|Site's POS periods closed, no claim exists|System|
|Reimbursement Reversal|Prior entries reversed before reposting|System|

On success: entries created, total amount, sites involved, "View in Journal →" link. On failure: the specific reason, identifying which site, program, or period caused it. 

Update on {{2026-02-25}}: After discussion with Hussain, for now reversal will fall within the first two labels instead of being its separate type. 

----

h2. Validation

Checked before any entries are created:

||Check||Scope||Result||
|Financial period a claim belongs to is closed and no claim or accrual has posted|Entire job|Notify user in “Periods” 
(Out of Scope for this story)|
|GL mapping missing|Entire job|The job fails and the user is notified this is because data points need to be mapped 
(Out of Scope for this story)|
|No state-level reimbursement|That program|No entry for “Due from State Funds” for that program (Skip, not fail)|

----

h2. Permissions

Per roles and permissions.

Update on {{2026-03-04}}: 

 Naming convention for type in activity log:

* *CLAIM* — triggered from Claim Readiness
* *ACCRUAL* — auto-calculated when no claim exists
* *REVERSAL*
NXT-69933.desc: *As a* district finance manager, *I want* reimbursements due to post to the general ledger when I create a claim, or automatically when the POS period closes if I don't, *so that* expected federal/state revenue is always reflected in my financial statements.

NXT-69733.summary: Sites Column: Clickable Site Status Drilldown
NXT-69733.ac:
# *Clickable site counts*

Given the Sites column displays counts (e.g., "8 Open, 7 Closed"), When the user clicks on either count, Then a popover opens showing the site-level breakdown.

# *Popover displays site status table*

Given the popover is open, Then it displays a table with columns: Site | POS | Inventory, And each cell shows status with visual indicator (e.g., ✓ Closed, ○ Open).

# *Site closure logic*

Given a site appears in the popover, Then that site shows as "Closed" in the aggregate count only when BOTH its POS AND Inventory periods are closed, And if either POS or Inventory is Open, the site counts toward "Open."

# *Sorting/filtering (optional)*

Given the popover is open, Then sites are sorted alphabetically by default, Or optionally filterable/sortable by status to surface open items first.

----

h2. Tech Notes

_(leave empty)_

h2. Grooming Q&A

_(leave empty)_

----

Want me to combine Cases 3 and 4 into a single story since they're both deferred drilldown functionality, or keep them separate?
NXT-69733.desc: *As a* district finance user reviewing financial periods, *I want to* click on the site counts in the Sites column to see each site's POS and Inventory status, *so that I can* identify exactly which upstream periods are holding up closure for each site.

NXT-69732.summary: Data Changed Column: Clickable Site Drilldown
NXT-69732.ac:
# *Reopened source (adjustments may be in progress)*

Given a financial period where a POS or Inventory period has reopened but not yet re-closed, When the user clicks the site count link (e.g., "7 sites"), Then a popover opens listing the affected site names.

# *Re-closed source (review journal entries)*

Given a financial period where a POS or Inventory period has re-closed, When the user clicks the site count link, Then the system navigates to the Journal view, pre-filtered to:

* This financial period
* This source (POS or Inventory)
* Only entries from the affected sites

# *Multi-source scenario - both reopened*

Given both POS and Inventory periods have reopened (displayed on separate lines), When the user clicks either site count link, Then a popover opens listing only the affected sites for that specific source.

# *Multi-source scenario - both re-closed*

Given both POS and Inventory periods have re-closed, When the user clicks either site count link, Then the system navigates to the Journal view pre-filtered for that source's affected sites.

# *Multi-source scenario - mixed states*

Given one source is reopened and the other is re-closed, When the user clicks the reopened source's site count, Then a popover opens listing affected sites.

When the user clicks the re-closed source's site count, Then the system navigates to the pre-filtered Journal view.

----

h2. Tech Notes

_(leave empty)_

h2. Grooming Q&A

_(leave empty)_
NXT-69732.desc: *As a* district finance user reviewing financial periods, *I want to* click on the site count in the Data Changed column to see which specific sites are affected, *so that I can* quickly identify where adjustments may be needed and navigate directly to relevant journal entries.

NXT-69519.ac:
Requirements:

# *Mode toggle*: Segmented control offers "Template Entry" (default) or "Manual Entry". Switching modes clears all entered data with confirmation dialog if any fields are populated.
# *Location*: Required dropdown. "All Locations" creates a district-level entry; specific site selection creates a site-level entry.
# *Posted Date*: Required, defaults to today. If date falls in a closed fiscal period, show inline error: "Selected date is in a closed period" and block save.
# *Template filter*: Chips for All / Default Templates / Custom Templates. If no templates exist for selected filter, show: "No templates available. [Create one in Account Mapping]"
# *Lists all entry descriptions from account mapping.* Templates with inactive or unmapped data points show ⚠️ prefix, specific status subtitle ("Contains inactive data points", "Contains unmapped data points", or "Contains inactive and unmapped data points"), and are disabled.
# *Description*: Required textarea, max 255 characters, counter shown. Placeholder: "e.g., January breakfast program deposits"
# *Line Items grid*: Hidden until template selected (show: "Select a template to populate line items"). After selection, shows "X items from template" badge. Accounts are pre-populated but fully editable — users can change account selections, delete rows, or add new rows via "+ Add Line" button. Minimum 1 line item required. If template rows are modified, badge updates to "Modified from template" for audit clarity.
# *Amount fields*: Two decimals, no negatives, thousand separators on blur. Tab order flows: Debit → Credit → next row's Debit. Empty fields treated as 0.00.
# *Balance validation*: Validates on every field change. Totals row shows running sums. Green "✓ Balanced" when equal; red "Unbalanced" with difference amount when not.
# *Save button*: Disabled until: all required fields complete AND entry balanced. On click: if validation fails, scroll to first error. On network failure, preserve all data and show toast: "Save failed. Please try again."
# *Cancel*: If any field modified, show confirmation: "Discard unsaved changes?" with Discard / Keep Editing options.
# *Scroll*: If template has >5 line items, Line Items grid scrolls internally with sticky header row.
NXT-69519.desc: As a user, I want a template of debit/credit rules so I can quickly create manual entries for recurring transaction types.

NXT-69504.ac:
h2. Toggle Data Point to Inactive — Core Behavior

_When user attempts to toggle a data point to Inactive:_

* Provide a toggle button inside the rules side drawer to set the status of the data point (active/inactive)
* The status in the main grid updates accordingly and that data point is greyed out (inactive appears with an orange circle)
* Check dependencies - System queries GL Entry Types using this data point

h3. If dependencies exist:

* Show modal: "{{This data point is used in {X} entry type(s). If you make it inactive, posting will be blocked anywhere it’s referenced until you reactivate it.}}"
* -List the affected entry types (clickable links)-
* Two actions:
* {{Simplified modal with count only and 2 buttons (Cancel, Make Inactive).}}

*-"View Entry Types" → navigates to Entry Types page filtered to affected items-*

*"*{{Make Inactive Anyway}}*" → proceeds with warning logged*

h3. Downstream behavior (already implemented in separate story):

* -Entry types containing inactive data points get a warning badge-
* -Automatic posting attempts for those entry types fail gracefully with clear error message-
* -For user-created data points, the user can reassign position of that data point used within its associated rules to other data points-
* -Any reassignment behavior is out of scope for this story and happens in the rules side drawer only for user created rules.-

h2. Rules Drawer — Status Toggle

_Given_ a user opens the rules drawer for any data point

_Then_ display a status toggle at the top of the drawer:

* Active state: Green background, "● Active" + "{{Journal entries using this data point can post.}}"
* Inactive state: Red background, "○ Inactive" + "{{Posting is blocked for journal entries using this data point. Activate it to allow posting.}}"
* Toggle switch allows immediate status change

h3. Rules Drawer — Inactive Warning Banner

_Given_ a user views the rules drawer for an entry type that references an inactive data point

_Then_ display a warning banner at the top of the journal entries section (inside the scrollable area):

* Format: Warning icon + "{{Inactive Data Point Warning"}} title + "{{{X} journal entry/entries reference inactive data points. Activate them to allow posting.}}"
* Styling: Amber gradient background, rounded border, shadow for visibility
* Position: Sticky at top of scroll area (stays visible while scrolling through rules)
* Condition: Only displayed when inactive references exist — hidden otherwise

h3. Rules Drawer — Inactive Data Point Display in Rules

_Given_ a journal entry references an inactive data point in debit or credit

_Then_ the inactive data point displays in the debit/credit table:

* Warning icon (⚠️) inline before the data point name
** This warning should appear in *any* rule drawer where inactive data points are referenced—not only in the specific drawer where we intentionally made a data point inactive during testing.
* Strikethrough styling with muted opacity (0.6)

_Note:_ This applies to ALL rules where the inactive data point appears

h3. Rules Drawer — Legend (Bottom)

A legend is displayed at the bottom of the drawer showing:

* Warning icon + strikethrough "Name" example
* Text: "{{Inactive data point — entry won't post until active."}}

h3. Main Table — Inactive Row Display

_Given_ a data point is marked inactive

_Then_ the table row displays:

* Status badge shows "{{Inactive}}" with red styling
* Row has muted appearance

h3. -Posting Behavior-

* -No automatic posting until all referenced data points are active.-
** Removed as this is current behavior

h3. System Rules — Read-Only

_Given_ a user views the rules drawer for a system-created data point

_Then:_

* No edit button is displayed on journal entry cards
* Rules are read-only and cannot be modified
* Only user-created data points have editable rules
NXT-69504.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69493.ac:
h1. 

# *Navigation*: Financials > Activity > Posting Activity Log. Page subtitle: "Monitor POS and Inventory posting jobs and their ledger entries"
# *Summary cards*: Four cards at top — Total Jobs (30 Days), Completed (green), Failed (red), Running (orange). Values calculated from last 30 days. If zero jobs, all cards show 0.
# *Status filter*: Chips above table for All / Completed / Failed / Running. Default: All.
# *Job table columns*: Job Name, Type (pill: "POS Posting" or "Inventory Posting"), Status (Completed green, Running orange with spinner, Failed red), Last Run (format: "Jan 2, 2026 at 2:30 PM"), Triggered By ("System" or username).
# *Table behavior*: Default 10 rows, paginated. Shows "Showing 1-10 of X jobs". Refresh button reloads data. Sorted reverse-chronological (newest first), not user-sortable.
# *Expanded row (success)*: Click row to expand. Shows: Ledger Entries Created (count), Total Amount (green, sum of entry amounts), Duration. Below that, "Entries by GL Entry Type" table with columns: Entry Type, Rule (format: "Debit: [Account] → Credit: [Account]" with Debit purple, Credit green), Count, Amount. Below table: "View in Journal →" link navigates to Journal Entries filtered by job date range and entry types.
# *Expanded row (failed)*: Shows red warning container with error icon, "Posting Failed" heading, and specific failure reason (e.g., "Missing GL account mapping for Site: Washington Elementary"). No entries table shown.
## If the user runs the job again and its in progress or it is successful, make a separate entry and disable the action buttons in the failed job. 
# *Empty state*: If no jobs in last 30 days, show centered: "No posting jobs in the last 30 days" with muted icon. Summary cards all show 0.
# *Partial failure*: Jobs that partially succeed then fail display as "Failed" with error message. Partial entries are not shown.
# *Manual trigger*: Out of scope — jobs triggered from Fiscal Periods page, not here.Tech Notes

*Amount calculation:*
Each ledger entry is a balanced double-entry transaction (debit = credit). The "Amount" displayed is the value of one side, not the sum of both. For example, if a Meal Sales Revenue entry debits Cash in Bank $45,000 and credits Meal Sales $45,000, the Amount shown is $45,000.

*Total Amount:*
Sum of all individual entry amounts for the posting job.

*Entry Type Amount:*
Sum of entry amounts grouped by GL Entry Type (e.g., all Meal Sales Revenue entries for this job).
NXT-69493.desc:
As a Child Nutrition Director,
I want to view a log of all background posting jobs,
so that I can confirm my financial data is being posted correctly.

h2. Objectives

Provide visibility into background posting jobs so users can quickly identify failures and trust their ledger is current.
NXT-69493.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69488.summary: Fin - Create Custom Data Points with Business Rules
NXT-69488.ac:
h3. 1. Post-Creation Success Message

* Upon successful data point creation, a toast notification appears at the bottom of the screen
* *Exact message*: "[Data Point Name] created. Use *Edit* for GL mapping, then *Rules* for business rules."
* Toast auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
* -The newly created row is highlighted briefly (green tint) to help user locate it-

h3. 2. Accessing Business Rules (Rules Button)

* User-created data points display a *Rules* button in the row actions (document icon)
* Clicking the Rules button opens the Rules side drawer
* The drawer title shows "Data Point Rules" with the data point name as subtitle

h3. 3. Rules Drawer for User-Created Data Points

When opening the Rules drawer for a user-created data point:

* Shows "User-defined journal entry" as the description
* Displays the current rule configuration (if any):
* Rule Name
* Base Type
* Entry Type (Manual)
* Journal entry template showing Debit and Credit accounts
* If no rules are configured yet, displays placeholder text indicating rules need to be set up
* The data point must be involved in the rule 

h3. 4. Editing Business Rules (via Edit Drawer)

For user-created data points, the Edit drawer includes a "Journal Entry Template" section allowing:

* *Rule Name* (required) - Name for the journal entry rule
* *Debit Account* selection (required) - Dropdown of all data points
* *Credit Account* selection (required) - Dropdown of all data points

Users can modify these fields at any time (unlike system-created data points which have fixed rules).

h3. 5. Delete Capability

* -User-created data points display a- *-Delete-* -button (trash icon) in the row actions-
* -Delete is also available in the Edit drawer footer-
* -Clicking Delete stages the data point for deletion (row shows strikethrough with "Undo" button)-
* -Deletion is finalized when user clicks "Save Changes" in the floating save bar-
* *-Note-*-: Deletion removes the routing configuration only; historical GL entries remain unaffected-
* *-Check if that data point is involved in any rule. If so, block it and inform the user and add a warning icon next to those rules.-* 

h3. 6. Validation

* Rule Name is required when configuring journal entry templates
* Rule Name must be unique across all data points
* Debit and Credit accounts are required when saving rule configuration
* The data point in which the rule is created is involved at least once on either debit and credit

Update added on {{2026-02-12}}: 

The system must allow multiple journal lines with the same GL account regardless of side; no auto-merge, no blocking; validation at journal level (debits = credits) and account level (active + mapped) only; when 2+ lines resolve to the same account, display once per session: "X lines reference the same account [*account  name.*]." This is:

* Informational only — does not prevent save or post.
* Persistent: stays visible for the duration of the editing session,cannot be dismissed by the user. 
* Auto-removes only if the user edits the lines such that the duplicate condition no longer exists.
* Re-appears if the user later re-creates the condition.
NXT-69488.desc:
*As a* financial administrator using the Account Mapping interface,
*I want to* create custom data points with associated business rules through an "Add Data Point" action,
*So that* I can define district-specific financial entries that aren't covered by standard system data points and ensure they map correctly to our GL accounts.
