NXT-69488.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69433.summary: Fin - Show data points associated with an account on the GL Accounts page
NXT-69433.ac:
* Add a rule action button to the grid on Configuration > General Ledger Configuration > GL Accounts
** Clicking it opens a side drawer showing all the rules the data point it is mapped to if any is involved in
** If that GL account is not involved in any rule, don’t enable the action button. 
* Clicking this should reveal the data point (if any) that GL account is mapped to
** If not mapped to any, display “This GL account is not mapped to any data point. Please go to the Account Mapping page and map it to a data point if you need it.”
* This is view-only
NXT-69433.desc: As a user, I want to see the data points my account is associated with.
NXT-69433.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69422.ac:
* define “transactions” as: any posted ledger entry line that references the GL account
* if the user attempts to edit a GL account that has *no transactions* but is *actively mapped* to one or more data points, ask the user to reassign those data points in the Account Mapping page
** *if the user tries to change the category it is grouped under, restrict this to account categories with the same base type as the data point*
** Inform user “This account is mapped to a data point(s) so you can only reassign it to a category with the same base type as that data point(s).”
*** Same as applicable when deleting and editing the account category. 
* display an explicit confirmation message: reassignment routes *future postings* for those data points into the newly selected GL account(s)
* after completion, show a summary of changes (data point → old GL account → new GL account)
NXT-69422.desc: As the administrator for this application, I want to prevent users from deleting GL accounts without reassigning the associated mapped data point if any to a new GL account.
NXT-69422.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69421.summary: Fin - Global Excel Import for GL Account Categories and GL Accounts
NXT-69421.ac:
h3. Import Button Behavior

* Provide an *Import* button in *Configuration > General Ledger Accounts*
** Updated on {{2026-02-09}}
* If GL Accounts import is configured: clicking Import redirects to System > Integration > Imports and Exports > [Actual Import Page] (user uploads file directly—no extra click on import type)
** If not configured: display warning: "Configure GL Accounts import type to continue. Have your template ready." (Updated on {{2026-02-05}})

h3. Import File Requirements

* The import file must be a filled version of the system-provided template and must include
** Sheet 1: GL Accounts

h3. Column Mapping Step

* After upload, display a *Column Mapping* step that supports separate mapping for the sheet:
** Sheet 1 (GL Accounts) required mappings
*** Account Code
*** Account Description
*** Account Category
*** Account Base Type
* Prevent the user from proceeding unless all required fields are mapped for each sheet

h3. Account Categories Import Logic

* Import Account Categories rows as create/update records using this key rule
** If Category Description is present, match on Category Description
* Display imported/updated Account Categories in the *Account Categories* grid with Category Name and Base Type

h3. GL Accounts Import Logic

* Import GL Accounts rows as create/update records using Account Code as the unique key
* On GL Account update, update Description and Category assignment
* Display imported/updated GL Accounts in the *GL Accounts* grid with Account Code, Description, and Category

h3. Validation Rules

* Validate each imported GL Account Code against the configured Account Code Structure using the same validation rules as the existing UI
* Do not block the import if Account Code Structure validation fails
* Any imported GL Account failing Account Code Structure validation must display in the GL Accounts grid with the existing warning indicator showing it needs correction

h3. Duplicate Handling

* If duplicate Account Codes exist within the same import file, still import the affected rows and flag them as Needs Fixing with inline error text “Duplicate account code in file”

h3. Data Handling Rule

* Treat Account Code as a string during import to preserve leading zeros and formatting exactly as provided

h3. Import Completion

* On completion, show an Import Results (Imports & Exports > Import Monitor > ‘General Ledger Accounts’ import record >Click on View Log button)
** Add a statistics section on top
*** GL Accounts: total rows, imported/updated count, needs fixing count
** Add the ‘Activity Monitor’, ‘Source Data’, ‘History Log’ and ‘Exceptions’ tabs
NXT-69421.desc: As a *district finance/admin user*, I want to *import my GL account categories and GL accounts from an Excel template in one step*, so that I can *quickly populate and maintain my General Ledger configuration without manual data entry*.
NXT-69421.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69392.summary: Fin - Display Data Point Business Rules in Account Mapping
NXT-69392.ac:
# *Rules display for system-generated data points*  
When a user opens a new ‘Rules’ action button in the main grid for any system-generated data point, a drawer displays with an "About This Data Point" section containing:
#* A description of the data point's purpose
#* The source system or process that generates entries
#* A journal entry template showing which accounts are debited and credited
# *Multiple journal entry support*  
For data points affected by multiple transaction types (e.g., Inventory, Due from Federal Funds, Refunds, Sales Tax), the system displays all applicable journal entry scenarios, each with its own source label and debit/credit template.
# *-Editable rules for user-created data points-*  
-When viewing a user-created data point, the journal entry template section is editable, allowing the user to select which data points should be debited and credited for manual entries.-
# *Consistent terminology*  
All journal entry templates -use "This data point"- to refer to the currently selected data point, ensuring clarity regardless of the data point name's length or complexity.
## *Updated to display the actual name instead of “this data point”* 
# *Manual entry indicators*  
Data points requiring manual journal entries clearly indicate "(Manual)" in their source field (e.g., "Benefits Entry (Manual)") to remind users that these entries are not system-generated.
# -Two-step transaction completion-  
-For certain liability accounts (Returned Checks, Refunds, Sales Tax Owed), the rules display indicates that a second manual ledger entry is required to complete the transaction. These entries are identified by their GL Entry Type Code:-
#* -Pay Returned Checks (PRTNCHK)-
#* -Pay Refunds (PAYRFND)-
#* -Sales Tax Paid (TAXPAID)-
NXT-69392.desc:
*As a* financial administrator viewing the Account Mapping interface,  
*I want to* see contextual business rules for each data point when I open its details drawer,  
*So that* I can understand how the data point affects financial entries and verify the correct GL account mapping.
NXT-69392.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69388.summary: Push POS Reimbursement Claims to Financials for Automation (Backend)
NXT-69388.ac:
*Requirements:*

* Ledger entries are created per Site.
* Financial periods must be open from the date of posting to the current date.
* *When creating these ledger entries in the database, each debit/credit line must be attributed to the originating data point / data source (e.g., the specific meal sales revenue data point or reimbursement data point) so the Profit & Loss report can calculate totals by data point even when one GL account is mapped to multiple data points.*

*Reimbursement Claim*

* GLEntryTypeCd: RMBRSCLM
* Posting date is the last day of the Claim Period.
* Debit: Due from Federal Funds (Asset)
* Credit: Reimbursement Program System Accounts (Revenue)

*Reimbursement Received*

* GLEntryTypeCd: RMBRSRCVD
* Posting date is the current date.
* Debit: Cash in Bank (Asset)
* Credit: Due from Federal Funds (Asset)

*Rollbacks*

* If a claim is recreated then, the user will be able to resubmit a claim. When the resubmit button is clicked the previous Reimbursement Claims and Reimbursement Receives will have rollback ledgers generated for them and the new claim will be posted.
** Should this be a setting?
NXT-69388.desc: As a user, I want the software to create ledger entries for my reimbursement claims.

NXT-69386.ac:
h2. Meal Sales Revenue

* Create a row corresponding to every single meal sales revenue data point with the status “Active” in the mapping page.
** If a program is active but has no revenue, it should still display with $0.00 with the income in the current period section. 
* *Meal Sales Revenue (Student) value* = (total credits - total debits) for all ledger entry lines attributed to meal sales revenue data points with the status “Active” created for student programs, included in the report when the posting date (last day of the Period) falls within the report date range (Monthly / Quarterly / YTD), regardless of how many Financial periods are included in that report date range.
* *Meal Sales Revenue (Adults) value* = (total credits - total debits) for all ledger entry lines attributed to meal sales revenue data points with the status “Active” created for adult sale types, included in the report when the posting date (last day of the Period) falls within the report date range (Monthly / Quarterly / YTD), regardless of how many Financial periods are included in that report date range.

h2. Reimbursements 

* Create a row for Reimbursement Claim - Federal Sources.
** This row is the aggregation of all reimbursement data points with the status “Active” created for Federal Sources.
** *Reimbursement Claim - Federal Sources value* = (total credits - total debits) for all ledger entry lines attributed to reimbursement data points with the status “Active” created for Federal Sources, included in the report when the posting date (last day of the Claim Period) falls within the report date range (Monthly / Quarterly / YTD), regardless of how many Financial periods are included in that report date range.
* Create a row for Reimbursement Claim - State Sources.
** This row is the aggregation of all reimbursement data points with the status “Active” created for State Sources.
** *Reimbursement Claim - State Sources value* = (total credits - total debits) for all ledger entry lines attributed to reimbursement data points with the status “Active” created for State Sources, included in the report when the posting date (last day of the Claim Period) falls within the report date range (Monthly / Quarterly / YTD), regardless of how many Financial periods are included in that report date range.

h2. Other Revenue and Totals 

* Add *Additional Income* under the *Revenue* section.
* *Net Revenue (excludes sales tax)* = Cash Sales + Debit Sales + Additional Income
* *Sales Tax Collected* = Cash Tax + Debit Tax
* *Total Collected (includes sales tax)* = Net Revenue + Sales Tax Collected
* Update the Revenue section totals to use these definitions.
NXT-69386.desc: As a district user, I want the revenue section of the Profit & Loss report to include meal sales revenue, Additional Income under Revenue and calculate totals so revenue excludes sales tax while total collected includes it.

NXT-69272.ac:
* History section displays upon clicking the “History” action button in the main grid and is a side drawer
* Section header shows "History" 
* History displays as a table with five columns: Performed By, Date, Performed Action, New Value, Previous Value
* Table shows most recent events at top
* Performed By column displays the user name who performed the action
* Date column displays timestamp in MM/DD/YY HH:MM:SS AM/PM format
* Performed Action column displays the action type (e.g., "GL Account Mapped", "GL Account Changed", "Data Point Created", "Status Changed", "Name Changed")
** Include before and after values in grid for consistency 
* -System-generated events show "System" as the Performed By value-
** Only actions performed by the user 
* User-initiated events show the user's name as the Performed By value
* New Value and Previous Value show the respective values
* Empty state shows "No history available" message
* Entries are made as soon as changes are made to the data points via the listed performed actions
NXT-69272.desc: As a finance administrator, I want to view the history of changes made to each data point so that I can audit who made changes and when.
NXT-69272.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69127.ac:
Make message global for story and combine [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69084|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69084|smart-link] 

*Scenario 1: Change mapping with no posted activity*

* User can select new GL account from dropdown
* User confirms change
* Mapping is updated immediately

*Scenario 2: Change mapping with posted activity in closed periods*

* User can select new GL account from dropdown
* System displays warning: "This account has posted activity in previous periods. Past ledger entries will remain on account [old account]. Future postings will use account [new account]. Reports spanning both periods will show both accounts."
* User confirms change with "I Understand, Change Mapping" button
* Mapping is updated for future postings only

*Scenario 3: Change mapping with unposted transactions in current open period*

* User can select new GL account from dropdown
* System displays message: "The current period [month/year] has not been posted yet. When you post [month], ALL transactions will use the new account: [new account]."
* User confirms change
* Mapping is updated immediately
NXT-69127.desc: Given a system account is mapped to a GL account When the user changes the mapping to a different GL account Then the change only affects future posting operations And existing ledger entries retain their original GL account assignments

NXT-69114.summary: Fin - Complete Mapping on Account Mapping Page
NXT-69114.ac:
h2. COMPLETE MAPPING SIDE DRAWER

* Opens when clicking "Complete Mapping" button in progress banner
* Drawer slides in from right side
* Shows overlay behind drawer
* Header displays: "Complete Mapping" with X close button
* Content shows only unmapped data points
* Section title: "[X] Unmapped Accounts"
* Each unmapped account displays:
* Data Point name
* Group name (subtitle)
* Typeahead input for GL Account selection
• Selecting a GL account in drawer:
* Saves mapping
* Removes account from drawer list
* Updates main table
* Updates progress banner
• When all accounts mapped:
* Drawer shows success state with checkmark icon
* Text: "All accounts mapped!"
* Subtext: "No unmapped accounts remaining"
• Drawer closes when clicking X button, clicking overlay, or pressing Escape key

* *When all system data points are mapped, the progress banner transforms to a success state displaying "All system data points mapped!" with a green checkmark, hiding the "Complete Mapping" button and the bar*
* System only uses active data points so if a needed data point is made inactive, it is removed from the mapping progress count.
NXT-69114.desc: complete mapping side drawer from mapping page in gl config
NXT-69114.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69100.ac:
* Opens when clicking "Add Data Point" button
* Drawer slides in from right side
* Header displays: "Add Data Point" with X close button
* Description text: "Create a custom data point for manual journal entries. This won't affect automatic postings from POS or inventory."

Form Fields:

Data Point Name:

* Label: "Data Point Name" with red asterisk (required)
* Input placeholder: "Enter a unique name..."
* Validates that name is unique across all existing data points
* Error message for empty: "Please enter a name"
* Error message for duplicate: "A data point with this name already exists"

Base Type:

* Label: "Base Type" with red asterisk (required)
* Dropdown with options: Select a type..., Asset, Liability, Equity, Revenue, Expense

GL Account dropdown for that base type and required field before saving

Buttons:

* "Cancel" button (ghost style) - closes drawer without creating
* "Create Data Point" button (primary style) - validates and creates

On Creation:

* New data point added to "Other [Type]s" group (e.g., "Other Expenses", "Other Assets")
* "Other" group only appears if user-created accounts exist for that type (except for “Other Revenue” which always exists due to data points from the system)
* Created By value set to "User"
* Drawer closes
* "Other" group auto-expands
* Page scrolls to new data point
* New row briefly highlighted with purple background
* GL Account shows as unmapped (needs mapping)

Edit/Delete button:

* Provide an edit and delete button
NXT-69100.desc: Users can manually create additional GL accounts for tracking activity not covered by the auto-generated accounts.
NXT-69100.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69099.ac:
*For each valuation category, create two system accounts:*

* [Valuation Category Name] — Asset (inventory value)
* [Valuation Category Name] — Expense (cost of goods/usage)

*Business rules:*

* Both asset and expense accounts must be mapped before the category can be used in postings
* Each asset GL account can only link to one valuation category
* Each expense GL account can only link to one valuation category

*Note: This list is independent of active status within Item Management.*
NXT-69099.desc:
These data points are created based on valuation categories configured in Item Management.

*Source:* Item Management > Item Configuration > Valuation Categories
NXT-69099.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69098.ac:

* Created for each program within program config

* Created for each program within program config
* Name format: "Reimbursement - [Program Name]"

* Examples: Reimbursement - School Breakfast Program, Reimbursement - National School Lunch Program

NXT-69084.ac:
h2. PAGE HEADER & NAVIGATION

• Page accessible from sidebar under Financials > Configuration > General Ledger Configuration
• Breadcrumb displays: Financials › Configuration › General Ledger Configuration
• Page title: "Account Mapping"

h2. IMPORTANT SETUP ACCORDION

• Collapsible accordion appears at top of content area, collapsed by default
• Header displays:

* Chevron icon (>) on left that rotates 90° when expanded
* Warning triangle icon
* Bold text: "Important Setup:"
* Subtitle text: "This is where you connect SchoolCafe to your district's accounting system. Each data point represents a type of transaction—just tell us which GL account it should post to."
• Expanded content contains the following sections:

What this affects:
• How transactions flow from SchoolCafe to your general ledger
• The accuracy of your financial reports and state submissions
• Period-end closing and ledger entry generation

Automatic Data Points:
• "Meal sales and reimbursement accounts are created from System → Management → Program Configuration. Inventory accounts come from Item Management → Valuation Categories. These post automatically when you close POS or inventory periods."

Manual Data Points:
• "Accounts like salaries, wages, and benefits are not generated by SchoolCafe but are needed for complete financial reporting. Add these using manual journal entries."

Posting dates:
• "The posted date will be the last day of the closed POS or Inventory period. The entered date will be the day you performed the close."

h2. MAPPING PROGRESS BANNER

• Displays below the accordion
• Shows warning state (yellow background, amber border) when mappings incomplete
• Shows success state (green background, green border) when all mappings complete

Warning State:
• Warning triangle icon
• Headline: "[X] system data points unmapped"
• Subtext: "Ledger entries cannot post until all system-generated data points are mapped to a GL account"
• "Complete Mapping" button on right side
• Progress bar fills proportionally with amber/orange color
• Right side displays: "[X] of [Y] system data points mapped"

Success State:
• Checkmark icon
• Headline: "All system data points mapped!"
• Subtext: "Automatic ledger entries are ready to post"
• Progress bar shows 100% filled with green color
• "Complete Mapping" button hidden

Counting Logic:
• Only system-created data points count toward mapping progress
• User-created data points do NOT count toward mapping progress
• Each data point counts as 1 toward total

h2. FILTERS SECTION

Accompanied by “Apply” button to apply search filters

Base Type Dropdown:
• Label: "Base Type"
• Options: All Types, Asset, Liability, Equity, Revenue, Expense

Status Dropdown:
• Label: "Status"
• Options: All, Mapped, Unmapped

Entry Type Dropdown:
• Label: "Entry Type"
• Options: All, Automatic, Manual
• Filters by entry type (Automatic vs Manual)

Search Input:
• Label: "Search"
• Placeholder text: "Search data points..."
• Dark background input field

Add Data Point Button:
• Purple primary button with plus (+) icon
• Text: "Add Data Point"
• Located to the right of the search input
• Opens Add Data Point side drawer on click

h2. ACCOUNT TABLE STRUCTURE

* Table Columns: ATA POINT | GL ACCOUNT | ENTRY TYPE | STATUS | CREATED BY | (Edit button)

Default Behavior:
• All groups collapsed by default on page load and all data points are toggled as active
• Clicking group header expands/collapses that group

h2. SECTION DIVIDERS

• Display section divider row when transitioning between sections
• Section divider contains: colored badge with section name, description text, mapping progress on right side (e.g., "6/6 active")

Assets Section (Green Badge):
• Badge text: "ASSETS"
• Description: "Bank accounts, receivables, and inventory on hand"

Liabilities Section (Orange Badge):
• Badge text: "LIABILITIES"
• Description: "Customer accounts, taxes, and payables"

Equity Section (Purple Badge):
• Badge text: "EQUITY"
• Description: "Fund balance"

Revenue Section (Purple/Primary Badge):
• Badge text: "REVENUE"
• Description: "Meal sales, reimbursements, and other income"

Expenses Section (Blue Badge):
• Badge text: "EXPENSES"
• Description: "Cost of goods sold & selling, general and administrative expenses"

h2. COLLAPSIBLE GROUP HEADERS

• Each group has a header row showing:

* Expand/collapse chevron (rotates to indicate state)
* Group title (bold)
* Account count: "[X] accounts"
* Status summary with colored dots: Green dot with "[X] active", Amber dot with "[X] inactive" (only shown if any inactive)
• Clicking group header toggles visibility of accounts within that group

h1. ACCOUNT GROUPS BY SECTION

h2. ASSETS SECTION

Bank & Receivables (2 accounts):
• Cash in Bank (Asset)
• Due from Federal Funds (Asset)

Inventory on Hand: Valuation categories sourced from [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69099|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69099|smart-link] 

h2. LIABILITIES SECTION

Customer Accounts (3 accounts):
• Prepaid Account (Liability)
• Refunds (Liability)
• Returned Checks (Liability)

Tax & Payables (2 accounts):
• Sales Tax Owed (Liability)
• Accounts Payable (Liability)

h2. FUND BALANCE SECTION

Fund Balance (1 account):
• Fund Balance (Equity)

h2. REVENUE SECTION

Meal Sales: Sourced from [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69098|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69098|smart-link] 

Reimbursements: Sourced from [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69098|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69098|smart-link] 

-Commodities (3 accounts):-
-• Commodity Revenue (Revenue)-
-• Planned Assistance Level (PAL) (Revenue)-
-• PAL Estimated Revenue (Liability)-

Operational Revenue (3 accounts):
• Additional Income (Revenue)
• Bank Fees (Revenue)
• Deposit Variances (Revenue)

h2. EXPENSES SECTION

Cost of Goods Sold: Valuation categories sourced from [https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69099|https://primeroedgenext.atlassian.net/browse/NXT-69099|smart-link] 

Labor & Payroll (4 accounts):
• Salaries (Expense)
• Wages (Expense)
• Benefits (Expense)
• Overtime (Expense)

Operational Expenses (2 accounts):
• Account Adjustments (Expense)
• Bonus (Expense)

h2. ACCOUNT ROW STRUCTURE

Each row displays:

* Data Point name (read-only, shows pending indicator dot if changes staged)
* GL Account name (read-only, displays "Not mapped" in gray italic if unmapped)
* Entry Type badge (Automatic/Manual)
* Status badge (read-only) - displays "Active" with green dot or "Inactive" with amber dot
* Created By value ("System" for system data points, “User” for user-created)
* "Edit" button - opens Edit Data Point side drawer

h2. STATUS COLUMN - READ ONLY DISPLAY

* Each account row displays a read-only status badge
* Badge displays:
** Green dot + "Active" text when active
** Amber dot + "Inactive" text when inactive
* Status can only be changed via the Edit Data Point side drawer
* By default, all data points are Active

h2. CREATED BY COLUMN

* Displays who created the data point
• Value: "System" for all pre-installed data points
• Value: "User" for user-created data points

h2. ACTION COLUMN

* ACTION COLUMN
** Each row displays an "Edit" button that opens the Edit Data Point side drawer
* Edit Data Point Drawer:
** Rename: Users can rename any data point (user-created only) via the Name field
** GL Mapping: Users can change GL account mapping via typeahead dropdown
** Delete: "Delete" button appears only for user-created data points; system data points cannot be deleted
* Batch Save:
** All changes (renames, GL mapping updates, deletions) are staged as pending
** Pending changes are applied together when user clicks "Save Changes" in the save bar
** -Status toggle changes apply immediately (not batched)-

h2. INLINE EDITING WITH TYPEAHEAD

• Each account row has a searchable text input for GL Account selection

* EDIT DATA POINT SIDE DRAWER:
**  Opens when user clicks "Edit" button on any account row
** Drawer header: "Edit Data Point"
** Drawer content displays:
** Entry Type badge and Base Type badge (read-only info)
** Data Point Name input (editable, required)
** GL Account typeahead input (required, filtered by base type)
** Created By info (read-only)
** Drawer footer contains:
** "Delete" button (left side, only visible for user-created data points)
** "Cancel" button (closes drawer without saving)
** "Apply Changes" button (stages changes to pending queue)
** GL Account typeahead only shows accounts matching the data point's base type
** Validation: Name must be unique, GL Account is required
** Close drawer with Escape key or clicking overlay
NXT-69084.desc:
*As a* district financial administrator, 

*I want* a centralized view to see all system account mappings and edit them inline, 

*so that* I can quickly review mapping status, identify gaps, and make changes without navigating through the wizard flow.

Mockup attached here: [^Account_Mapping_View.html]
NXT-69084.epic_summary: FIN - Account mapping and data points backend

NXT-69071.summary: Configure Payroll System Accounts and Generate Ledger Entry Templates
NXT-69071.ac:
* a new settings page exists: *Account Mapping → Payroll & Manual Entries*
* when the page is opened for the first time, the system creates these payroll system accounts (if missing)
** *Salaries and Wages* (Expenditure)
** *Benefits* (Expenditure)
* the page allows users to add additional payroll system accounts as needed
** required fields: *name*, *base type* (Expenditure or Liability)
** name must be unique within the district
* each payroll system account row shows a *gl account dropdown*
** dropdown is filtered to gl accounts with the same base type as the payroll system account
** dropdown shows gl account name only
* the page also shows *Cash in Bank* as the funding account used for payroll entries
** cash in bank is pulled from the mapping completed in the wizard (read-only on this page)
** if cash in bank is not mapped yet, show a blocking message: “map cash in bank to continue”
* once payroll accounts are mapped, the system generates a *gl entry template* used by the ledger “add new entry” flow
** template rule (for each payroll account): *debit = selected payroll expense gl account, credit = cash in bank gl account*
** example: salaries and wages entry → debit salaries and wages, credit cash in bank
** example: benefits entry → debit benefits, credit cash in bank
* ledger behavior
** the ledger has an *Add New Entry* button with a *Payroll Entry* option
** selecting payroll entry lets the user choose the payroll system account (example: salaries and wages, benefits)
** the entry form auto-populates the debit and credit gl accounts using the template rule above
** user must enter at minimum: date, amount, description, site (if ledger entries are site-level in your design)
* validation
** payroll entry option is disabled until cash in bank is mapped and at least one payroll system account is mapped
** base type filtering must prevent choosing the wrong type of gl account for a payroll system account
** no duplicate payroll system account names for the district
NXT-69071.desc: District users need a simple way to configure payroll-related accounts (manual entries) so they can quickly create consistent journal entries from the Ledger.

NXT-69070.ac:
_Page Header & Navigation_

* Page accessible from sidebar under Financials > Configuration > General Ledger Configuration

_Status Banner_

* When unmapped accounts exist, show warning banner with text: "[X] mapping(s) incomplete — Ledger entries cannot post until all accounts are mapped."
* Warning banner includes secondary button "Complete Mapping" that opens wizard
* When all accounts mapped, show success banner with text: "All accounts mapped! — Ledger entries are ready to post."

_Summary Cards (4 cards in horizontal row)_

* Card 1: "Total Accounts" with dynamic count and subtext "System accounts configured"
* Card 2: "Mapped" with dynamic count (styled green) and subtext "Ready for posting"
* Card 3: "Unmapped" with dynamic count (styled amber) and subtext "Action required"
* Card 4: "GL Accounts Used" with count of unique GL codes and subtext "Unique GL accounts"
* Regular accounts count as 1 toward total
* Valuation categories count as 2 toward total (Asset mapping + Expense mapping)
* Valuation category only counts as "mapped" when BOTH Asset and Expense accounts are selected

_Filters_

* Section dropdown: All Sections, Revenue & Reimbursements, Expenses
* Base Type dropdown: All Types, Asset, Revenue, Liability, Expense
* Status dropdown: All, Mapped, Unmapped
* Search text input with placeholder "Search system accounts..."
* Reset button to clear all filters
* Filters apply immediately on change

_Account Table Structure_

* Table columns: System Account, GL Account, Status, Actions
* Accounts grouped into collapsible sections
* All groups collapsed by default on page load

_Section Dividers_

* Display section divider row when transitioning between Revenue & Reimbursements and Expenses
* Section divider shows badge with section name, description text, and mapping progress (e.g., "13/16 mapped")
* Revenue & Reimbursements description: "POS transactions, program meals, and federal reimbursements"
* Expenses description: "Inventory valuation and cost of goods"

_Collapsible Group Headers_

* Each group has a header row showing: expand/collapse chevron, group title, account count, mapping status summary
* Account count displays "[X] accounts" for regular groups or "[X] categories" for valuation groups
* Status summary shows green dot with "[X] mapped" and amber dot with "[X] unmapped" (if any unmapped)
* Clicking group header toggles visibility of accounts within that group
* Chevron rotates to indicate collapsed/expanded state

_System Accounts (from backend)_

* Each system account has a unique appAccountCode identifier
* Each system account has a baseType: Asset, Revenue, Liability, or Expense
* GL Account dropdowns filtered to only show GL accounts matching the system account's baseType

_System Account List by Group_

Bank & Receivables (Revenue & Reimbursements section):

* Cash in Bank (appAccountCode: CASHBNK, baseType: Asset)
* Due from Federal Funds (appAccountCode: DUEFEDF, baseType: Asset)

Meal Sales (Revenue & Reimbursements section):

* Meal Sales Accounts (appAccountCode: SALES, baseType: Revenue)

Reimbursements & Commodities (Revenue & Reimbursements section):

* Reimbursement Program Accounts (appAccountCode: RMBRSMNT, baseType: Revenue)
* Commodity Revenue (appAccountCode: COMMREV, baseType: Revenue)
* Planned Assistance Level (PAL) (appAccountCode: COMMAST, baseType: Revenue)
* PAL Estimated Revenue (appAccountCode: ESTREV, baseType: Liability)

POS Revenue Adjustments (Revenue & Reimbursements section):

* Additional Income (appAccountCode: ADDINCM, baseType: Revenue)
* Bank Fees (appAccountCode: BNKFEE, baseType: Revenue)
* Deposit Variances (appAccountCode: DPSTVAR, baseType: Revenue)

POS Liabilities (Revenue & Reimbursements section):

* Prepaid Account (appAccountCode: PPACC, baseType: Liability)
* Refunds (appAccountCode: REFUND, baseType: Liability)
* Returned Checks (appAccountCode: RTNCHCK, baseType: Liability)
* Sales Tax Owed (appAccountCode: STTAXOW, baseType: Liability)

POS Expenses (Revenue & Reimbursements section):

* Account Adjustments (appAccountCode: ACCTADJ, baseType: Expense)
* Bonus (appAccountCode: BONUS, baseType: Expense)

Payables (Expenses section):

* Accounts Payable (appAccountCode: AP, baseType: Liability)

Inventory Valuation (Expenses section):

* Valuation Group Inventory Asset (appAccountCode: INVENTORY, baseType: Asset)
* Valuation Group Inventory Expense (appAccountCode: INVEXP, baseType: Expense)
* Note: Each valuation category from Item Management maps to both an Asset GL account and an Expense GL account

_Validation Rules_

* Due from Federal Funds (DUEFEDF) and Cash in Bank (CASHBNK) must be mapped to different GL accounts
* Prepaid Account (PPACC) and Refunds (REFUND) must be mapped to different GL accounts
* Prepaid Account (PPACC) and Returned Checks (RTNCHCK) must be mapped to different GL accounts
* Prepaid Account (PPACC) and Sales Tax Owed (STTAXOW) must be mapped to different GL accounts
* Display inline error message when validation rule is violated
* Prevent saving when validation rules are violated

_Inline Editing with Typeahead_

* Each account row has a searchable text input for GL Account selection
* Clicking or focusing the input reveals a dropdown of matching GL accounts
* Typing in the input filters the dropdown to show only GL accounts whose name contains the typed text
* Dropdown only shows GL accounts that match the system account's baseType
* Selecting an option from the dropdown populates the input and saves the mapping
* Clearing the input and clicking away removes the mapping
* Unmapped inputs display with dashed border styling and placeholder text "Type to search GL accounts..."

_Valuation Category Rows_

* Display category name
* Two inline typeahead inputs side by side: Asset Account and Expense Account
* Asset input filters to show only Asset-type GL accounts with placeholder "Type to search Asset accounts..."
* Expense input filters to show only Expense-type GL accounts with placeholder "Type to search Expense accounts..."
* Status shows "Complete" when both mapped, "Incomplete" when one or both missing

_Auto-Save Behavior_

* Selecting a GL account from the dropdown immediately saves the mapping
* Table and all summary statistics refresh after each change
* Status banner updates based on new mapping totals

_Actions Column_

* Each row includes a history button to view mapping change history
NXT-69070.desc:
*As a* district financial administrator,

*I want* a guided wizard to map system accounts to GL accounts,

*So that* SchoolCafe can generate accurate ledger entries for revenue, reimbursements, and expenses.

NXT-69062.summary: Ledger Entry Rules for POS-related Transactions
NXT-69062.ac:
* when a pos period is set to *closed* for a site, the system creates a revenue entries batch for that *site + period* with *status = new* and *dirty = false*
* the batch stores two dates for the ledger entry:
** *entered date* = date/time the pos period was closed
** *posting date* = the pos period end date
* the system generates the following entry groups and creates the ledger lines *at the site level* using the *gl accounts mapped to the listed system accounts*
** *meal sales*
*** amount = *cash sale + debit sale* totals for the site/period from the revenue report
**** exclude any sales paid using prepaid meal balance
*** credit: the gl accounts mapped to the *meal sales revenue system accounts* for the site
**** create one credit line per mapped revenue system account (program/service + a-la-carte + adult meals), matching the revenue report split
*** the sum of all credit lines must equal the debit total within each entry
** *prepayment*
*** debit: the gl account mapped to *prepaid account (Liability)*
**** amount = total sales paid using *prepaid meal balance* for the site/period from the revenue report/rollups
*** credit: the gl accounts mapped to the *meal sales revenue system accounts* for the site
**** create one credit line per mapped revenue system account (program/service + a-la-carte + adult meals), matching the prepaid-paid split
*** the sum of all credit lines must equal the debit total within each entry
** *cash taxes collected*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Cash in Bank (Asset)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Sales Tax Owed (Liability)*
*** amount = total *cash tax* for the site/period from the revenue report (adult taxable sales only)
** *prepayment taxes collected*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Prepaid Account (Liability)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Sales Tax Owed (Liability)*
*** amount = total *prepaid tax* for the site/period from the revenue report (adult taxable sales only)
** *account adjustments (GLEntryTypeCd = ACCTADJ)*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Account Adjustments (Expenditure)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Prepaid Account (Liability)*
*** amount = total account adjustments for the site/period from rollups
** *additional income (GLEntryTypeCd = ADDINCM)*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Cash in Bank (Asset)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Additional Income (Revenue)*
*** amount = total additional income for the site/period from rollups
** *bank fees (GLEntryTypeCd = BNKFEE)*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Prepaid Account (Liability)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Bank Fees (Revenue)*
*** amount = total bank fees for the site/period from rollups
** *bonus (GLEntryTypeCd = BONUS)*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Bonus (Expenditure)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Prepaid Account (Liability)*
*** amount = total bonus for the site/period from rollups
** *deposit variances (GLEntryTypeCd = DPSTVAR)*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Cash in Bank (Asset)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Deposit Variances (Revenue)*
*** amount = total deposit variance for the site/period from rollups
** *refunds (GLEntryTypeCd = REFUND)*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Prepaid Account (Liability)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Refunds (Liability)*
*** amount = total refunds for the site/period from rollups
*** validation: *Prepaid Account* and *Refunds* system accounts must not be mapped to the same GL account
** *returned checks (GLEntryTypeCd = RTNCHCK)*
*** debit: the GL account mapped to *Prepaid Account (Liability)*
*** credit: the GL account mapped to *Returned Checks (Liability)*
*** amount = total returned checks for the site/period from rollups
*** validation: *Prepaid Account* and *Returned Checks* system accounts must not be mapped to the same GL account
* mapping validation
** if the gl mapping is missing for cash in bank, prepaid account, or any required meal sales revenue system account, the system must fail generation for that site/period and return a message listing the missing mapping(s) (no partial entries)
NXT-69062.desc: When a POS period is closed for a site, the system generates the sales and prepayment entry groups for that site and period so they can be approved and posted.

NXT-69059.summary: Create system accounts when Financials license is activated (Backend)
NXT-69059.desc: When a district activates the Financials license, the system automatically creates the required system accounts so the district can map them to GL accounts and automate ledger postings.

NXT-68770.summary: Backend Calculations for Profit & Loss Report
NXT-68770.ac:
* user selects a reporting period (example: january 1 to january 31)
* create a revenue table
* revenue table must include one row for each gl account that meets both of these conditions:
** account type = revenue
** account appears in at least one manual entry line (debit or credit) where the posting date is inside the selected reporting period
* row label must use the gl account name exactly as stored in the gl accounts table
* for each revenue gl account row, system must calculate:
** description = gl account name
** current period = (sum of credits in period) – (sum of debits in period)
** prior period = (sum of credits in prior period) – (sum of debits in prior period)
** year-to-date = cumulative (credits – debits) from fiscal year start up to the end date of the selected reporting period
** % of revenue = current period ÷ total revenue
** % change from prior period =
*** if prior period ≠ 0 → (current – prior) ÷ prior
*** if prior period = 0 → -
* total revenue = sum of all current period revenue values across all revenue gl accounts
* these exact revenue values (each row + totals) must be used by the dashboard
* create an expenses table
* expenses table must include one row for each gl account that meets both of these conditions:
** account type = expense
** account appears in at least one manual entry line (debit or credit) where the posting date is inside the selected reporting period
* row label must use the gl account name exactly as stored in the gl accounts table
* for each expense gl account row, system must calculate:
** description = gl account name
** current period = (sum of debits in period) – (sum of credits in period)
** prior period = (sum of debits in prior period) – (sum of credits in prior period)
** year-to-date = cumulative (debits – credits) from fiscal year start up to the end date of the selected reporting period
** % of expenses = current period ÷ total expenses
** % change from prior period =
*** if prior period ≠ 0 → (current – prior) ÷ prior
*** if prior period = 0 → -
* total expenses = sum of all current period expense values across all expense gl accounts
* these exact expense values (each row + totals) must be used by the dashboard
* display net income = total revenue – total expenses and subtotals
NXT-68770.desc:
This story deals with the profit and loss report that will be generated based on dev work done in Phase 1. Since we are only doing manual entries at this stage, the final report may look different. 

Sample report attached[^sample_profit_loss.pdf]

NXT-68719.summary: Backend Calculations for Profit & Loss Report
NXT-68719.ac:
The Profit and Loss report has the following sections:

# Cost of Goods Sold for Valuation Categories:
## Valuation Categories are sourced from Item Management > Item Configuration > Valuation Categories
## The formula for COGS is COGS = Beginning Inventory + Purchases + Transfers In - Transfers Out - Ending Inventory
## The table has the following columns:
### Description: This refers to the name of the valuation category and is sourced from item management. It *does not* refer to any data source from financials.
### Beginning entry:  This will be inherited from Item Management, however if there is no data then it starts from 0. 
### Purchases: This refers to new inventory purchased within that period and is determined by looking at the amount involved in manual entries marked as Inventory – Purchase.
### Inventory Transfer (In & Out): This refers to transfer of items across sites within the district and is obtained from manual entries of the type Inventory – Transfer In (from Warehouse) and Inventory – Transfer Out (to Warehouse)
### Ending Inventory: After integration, this value will be sourced from Item Management. However, since we are not there yet, we will determine this with the formula below using transactions of the type Inventory – Usage (COGS):
### Ending Inventory = Beginning Inventory + Purchases + Transfers In - Transfers Out - COGS(Usage)
# Revenue Sources:
## The revenue table is split into two sections: revenue from meal sales and all other sources besides reimbursements (e.g. catering) and reimbursements
## Data for the first section will be sourced from manual entries of the types below and each will be its own row within the revenue table:
### Revenue – Catering
### Revenue – Breakfast A La Carte
### Revenue – Breakfast Adult/Visitor
### Revenue – Breakfast Paid
### Revenue – Breakfast Reduced
### Revenue – Lunch A La Carte
### Revenue – Lunch Adult/Visitor
### Revenue – Lunch Paid
### Revenue – Lunch Reduced
## We will also be providing percentage changes here. The columns needed are:
### Description: This refers to the meal types served by that district and is sourced from accountability (?)
### Current Period
### Prior Period: This will be zero if we are starting from scratch when generating data
### % of Revenue: This refers to this revenue category’s contribution to total revenue including reimbursements for the selected period
### % Change from Prior Period: This refers to the percentage change of that revenue categoriy’s contribution to the total revenue compared to the prior period.
### Year-to-date: Cumulative amount from start of fiscal year to the date the report is run
### Item % of Category: This refers to that revenue category’s contribution as a percentage of the total revenue from all revenue sources excluding reimbursements
## Data for the second section will be sourced from the following entry types and have its own row within the table:
### Revenue – Reimbursement (Breakfast, District)
### Revenue – Reimbursement (Lunch, District)
### The table will have the following columns:
#### Description: This refers to the types of reimbursement and is sourced from accountability (?)
#### Current Period: This is the reimbursement earned for that category for the selected period and comes from the manual entry
#### Prior Period: This is the reimbursement earned for that category for the priod period and rolls over from the previous period or is zero if there is no data for the previous period
#### % of Revenue: This refers to reimbursements' contribution to total revenue including the previous section
#### % Change from Prior Period: This refers to the percentage change of that revenue categoriy’s contribution to the total revenue compared to the prior period
#### Year-to-date: Cumulative amount from start of fiscal year to the date the report is run
#### Item % of Category: This refers to that revenue category’s contribution as a percentage of the total revenue from all revenue sources excluding reimbursements
# Expenses
## The expenses section includes two sections: the cost of goods sold and the cost of operation (Selling, General & Administrative Expenses including salary and wages)
### Data for cost of goods sold is sourced the same way as in the cost of goods sold section. For each valuation category, we will indicate:
#### Description: Again, this is the name of the valuation category as configured in Item Management
#### Current Period: This should be equal to the COGS value associated with that valuation category
#### Prior Period: This is the COGS value associated with that valuation category for the prior period
#### % Change from Prior Period: This refers to how much the COGS for that valuation categorty changed compared to the prior period
#### % of Expenses: This refers to this valuation category’s percentage contribution to total expenses including SG&A expenses
#### % change from Prior Period: This refers to the percentage change in that valuation category’s contribution to total expenses compared to the prior period
#### Year-to-Date: COGS for that valuation category as of the date the report is run
#### % of Category: This refers to this valuation category’s percentage contribution to total cost of goods sold of all valuation categories
### Data for SG&A expenses is sourced from manual entries with the entry types:
#### Expense – SG&A
#### Expense – Payroll
#### Expense – Other
### The SG&A expenses table has two sections (Sales, Goods & Administrative (SG&A) and Salaries & Wages) and will have the same columns as the COGS section: 
#### Description: This refers to the name of the expense GL account involved in the manual entries of the three types above
#### Current Period: This refers to the total dollar amount for that expense GL account over the selected period
##### If a manual entry has multiple expense accounts involved, each will have their own row within the table (if not there already) and be the sum of its dollar amount over all transactions within that period for each expense account involved
#### Priod Period: This is the sourced just like current period but refers to the same for the prior period
#### % Change from Prior Period: This refers to how much the expenses associated with that GL account changed compared to the prior period
#### % of Expenses: This refers to this general ledger account’s percentage contribution to total expenses including COGS of the valuation categories
#### % change from Prior Period: This refers to the percentage change in that expense account's contribution to total expenses compared to the prior period
#### Year-to-Date: Total expenses for that account as of the date the report is run
#### % of Category: This refers to this valuation category’s percentage contribution to total cost of goods sold of all SG&A expenses (including payroll)
### For the Salaries and Wages section, we will be repeating the same process but only using expense accounts associated with manual entries of the type Expense – Payroll
# Totals:
## Provide sub-totals within each category as well as at the bottom of each column in that table
## Indicate Total Revenue at the bottom of the revenue table
## Indicate Total Expenses at the bottom of the expenses table in part 3
## Calculate Net Income by subtracting total expenses from the total revenue
### Use parantheses to indicate negative values
### All dollar values and percentages should be rounded to two decimal places
NXT-68719.desc: This story deals with the profit and loss report that will be generated based on dev work done in Phase 1. Since we are only doing manual entries at this stage, the final report may look different.

NXT-68708.summary: Add Required “Entry Type” Field to Manual Journal Entries
NXT-68708.ac:
h3. *1. New Required Field*

* Add a new field labeled *“Entry Type”* to the Manual Entry form.
* Field is *required* to save or submit an entry.
* If left blank, show:
*“Please select an Entry Type.”*

----

h3. *2. Dropdown Values (Final List)*

h4. *INVENTORY*

* Inventory – Purchase
* Inventory – Usage (COGS)
* Inventory – Transfer In (from Warehouse)
* Inventory – Transfer Out (to Warehouse)

h4. *EXPENSE*

* Expense – SG&A
* Expense – Payroll
* Expense – Other

h4. *REVENUE*

* Revenue – Catering
* Revenue – Breakfast A La Carte
* Revenue – Breakfast Adult/Visitor
* Revenue – Breakfast Paid
* Revenue – Breakfast Reduced
* Revenue – Lunch A La Carte
* Revenue – Lunch Adult/Visitor
* Revenue – Lunch Paid
* Revenue – Lunch Reduced

h4. *REIMBURSEMENT*

* Revenue – Reimbursement (Breakfast, District)
* Revenue – Reimbursement (Lunch, District)

----

h3. *3. Editing Existing Entries*

* Existing manual entries without an Entry Type must prompt the user to select one before saving.

----

h3. *4. Persistence*

* Store the selected Entry Type with the manual entry record.
* Display it in the Manual Entry detail view.
NXT-68708.desc:
To correctly categorize manual journal entries for the P&L and Inventory reports, we need to add a required *“Entry Type”* field to the Manual Entry screen.
This is an *enhancement* to the existing workflow and users must now select an Entry Type whenever creating a manual entry.

NXT-68660.ac:
h2. *1. Site-by-Site Performance Table*

h3. *Data Included*

* Revenue, expense, and net income totals for each site.
* Aggregated district total row at the bottom.
* Only posted GL transactions included.

h3. *Columns*

* *Site Name*
* *Revenue*
* *Expenses*
* *Net Income* (Revenue – Expenses)

h3. *Behavior*

* Sortable by Net Income (default: descending)
** Also by site name
* Any site with no data for the selected period:
** Show {{- }}in all columns.
** Only show data for active sites
* *Filtering Logic*
** Uses the *selected period* from the period dropdown at the top of the dashboard

----

h2. *-2. Revenue Analysis Card — Top 5 Revenue Sources-*

h3. *-Purpose-*

-Show the top 5 revenue categories contributing to the district’s total revenue for the selected period.-

h3. *-Data Rules-*

* -Use GL accounts categorized with a- *-Revenue-* -base type.-
* -Group by- *-category-*
* -Calculate:-
** -Total revenue for each category.-
** -Percentage share of total district revenue.-
* -Display- *-top 5-* -categories sorted descending by revenue amount.-

h3. *-UI Elements-*

* -Category name-
* -Dollar value-
* -Percentage contribution-

h3. *-Behavior-*

* -If fewer than 5 categories exist, show all available.-
* -If no revenue exists, show empty-state with “No revenue data for the selected period.”-

----

h2. *-3. Expense Analysis Card — Top 5 Expense Categories-*

h3. *-Purpose-*

-Show the top 5 categories driving district expense for the selected period.-

h3. *-Data Rules-*

* -Use GL accounts categorized with an- *-Expense-* -base type.-
* -Group by category.-
* -Calculate:-
** -Total expenses per category.-
** -Percentage of total district expenses.-
* -Display- *-top 5-* -categories sorted descending.-

h3. *-UI Elements-*

* -Category name-
* -Dollar amount-
* -Percentage share-
* -Progress bar-

h3. *-Behavior-*

* -If fewer than 5 categories exist, show all.-
* -If no expenses exist, show empty-state.-
NXT-68660.desc: District leadership needs a consolidated, at-a-glance view of financial performance across sites and key revenue/expense drivers. This dashboard section allows administrators to quickly identify high-performing or underperforming sites and understand which revenue streams and expense categories contribute most to the district’s financial position for the selected period.

NXT-68659.summary: Fin - Display Net Income, Revenue, and Expenses Summary Card on Financial Dashboard
NXT-68659.ac:
*Period selector is a standard dropdown labeled “Period:” in the top right of the dashboard.*

* Period options: Current Month (MMM YYYY), Current Quarter (Q# YYYY), Current Fiscal Year (FY YYYY), Custom Range.
* Default period on dashboard load: Current Fiscal Year (FY ) as of today.
* Changing the period recalculates Net Income, Total Revenue, and Total Expenses and updates the card label (e.g., “Net Income (June 2025)”).

*Current Fiscal Year date range comes from Fiscal Year Settings (start and end dates).*

* Current Month = 1st to last day of the current calendar month, within the fiscal year that includes today.
* Current Quarter uses fiscal quarters (Q1–Q4) defined by Fiscal Year Settings.
** All “Current …” options must fall inside the active fiscal year
* Period date ranges for the quarter, year-to-date and current month are system-defined and not user-editable.

*Data source is posted financial transactions from the General Ledger.*

* Calculations include all active sites.
* Only GL accounts with base type Revenue or Expense are included.
* Include all posted transactions whose transaction/posting date falls within the selected period, even if the fiscal period is still open.

*Total Revenue = sum of GL balances for all Revenue accounts in the selected date range, shown as a positive whole dollar amount.*

*Total Revenue formula: Sum of all credits minus debits for GL accounts whose base type = Revenue within the selected date range.*

*Total Expenses = sum of GL balances for all Expense accounts in the selected date range, shown as a positive whole dollar amount.*

*Total Expenses formula: Sum of all debits minus credits for GL accounts whose base type = Expense within the selected date range.*

*Net Income = Total Revenue – Total Expenses; if negative, show with a minus sign (e.g., -$125,000).*

*Net Income card title format: “Net Income ()”.*

* Main Net Income number is shown in large text with currency formatting and thousands separators.
* Total Revenue and Total Expenses are shown below as smaller values, rounded to the nearest dollar.

*If there are no posted Revenue or Expense transactions in the selected period, show “0” for the respective column and calculate net income assuming it’s value as zero.*

*Trend arrow compares Net Income for the latest completed fiscal month vs the immediately previous fiscal month.*

* A completed month = a fiscal month that has fully passed in real time.
* If latest > previous show ↑; if less show ↓; if equal show no arrow.
* Hide arrow if fewer than two completed fiscal months exist in the current fiscal year.
* Trend arrow is not affected by the selected reporting period.
* Tooltip: *“Trend compares the two most recently completed fiscal months.”*

*Site-by-site performance table shows Revenue, Expenses, and Net Income (Revenue – Expenses) for each active site, plus a district total row at the bottom.*

* Columns: Location, Revenue, Expenses, Net Income.
* For the district-level entry, use the district name.
* Net Income only appears when both Revenue and Expense totals are present; otherwise show “–”.
* Tooltip: *“Net Income is unavailable because Revenue and/or Expenses are missing for this period.”*
* Table uses only posted GL transactions and the same selected period as the dashboard.
* Default sort is Net Income descending; secondary sort by Location name.
* Sites with no data show “–” in numeric fields.
* Only show *active* sites; do not display inactive sites.
NXT-68659.desc:
h3. Business Goal

* Provide district leadership with a *single glance* view of:
** Net income
** Total revenue
** Total expenses
for a selected period (Current Month, Current Quarter, Current Fiscal Year).
* Use this card as the *primary KPI* on the Financial Dashboard for Financials 2.0.

NXT-68658.summary: FIN - Implement Financial Periods Management (Generate + Manage Tabs)
NXT-68658.ac:
*Location:* Financials > Configuration > Periods

h3. Fiscal Year

* User configures Fiscal Year with *Start Date* and *End Date*
* System auto-generates periods based on selected frequency:
** Weekly (ends on selected weekday)
** Biweekly (ends on selected weekday)
** Semi-monthly (ends on 14th/15th/16th and last day)
** Monthly (ends last day of month)
* Option to include date range in period name
* No gaps: new FY start = prior FY end + 1 day
* Lock FY dates as soon as user sets and saves them, same for frequency
* User can split, edit, add, or delete periods after generation (no overlaps allowed)

h3. Period States

* *Open* – manual entries allowed, data flowing in
* *Closed* – manual entries blocked
** POS/Inventory re-closed since; show warning badge with source + timestamp
** User can reopen that period, make adjustments and close it
* Reversals can happen at any time and will post on the date the action was performed

h3. Data Flow

* When POS/Inventory closes, entries post to Financial period containing the close date
* POS/Inventory data always flows in regardless of period state
* If period was Closed → mark Dirty, notify user
* Reversal + new entry both dated to last day of original period (entered date = today)

h3. Automatic Entry Adjustments (Setting)

* Default: *OFF*
* When ON: auto-reverse and repost when source data changes for closed periods
* User controls whether this happens automatically or manually
* History tracking of changes

h3. Closed Fiscal Year (moved to separate story)

* Incoming retroactive data creates reversal + new entry in current period
* Flagged as prior period adjustment

h3. Manual Entries

* Allowed in Open periods only and once financial periods are configured
* Blocked in Closed periods and Closed fiscal years

h3. Period Actions

* Close / Reopen
* Close All / Reopen All
* Split, Edit, Delete
* View History (drawer)

h3. Manage Periods Table Columns

* Period (name + date range)
* Sites (POS & Inventory) – e.g., "8 Open, 7 Closed" (both POS AND Inventory must be open/closed)
* Data Changed (warning if source changed since close)
* Status
* Actions

h3. History Drawer

* Status changes with timestamp + user
* Source data changes for Dirty periods (what changed, when, from which source)
NXT-68658.desc:
Districts need Financial periods to configure the start and end dates for their fiscal year, track monthly financial data, control manual entry backdating, and identify when source data has changed. 

Mockup attached here: [^Financial_Periods_Mockup.html]
NXT-68658.epic_summary: Financial Period Intelligence — Automated Adjustments, Notifications & Pending Entry Resolution

NXT-68643.summary: Fin - Revenue and Expenditure (P&L) Report – Parameters, Multi-Site Exports
NXT-68643.ac:
h3. 1. Page Header & Helper Text

* Page title: *“Revenue and Expenditure (P&L) Report”*
* Helper text directly under title:
* “Choose a fiscal year, reporting period, and sites. When multiple sites are selected, each site gets its own P&L worksheet in the Excel file.”
* Tooltip “You’ll be notified once your file is ready and can download it from the Document Center.”

----

h3. 2. Parameter Panel (top card)

h4. 2.1 Fiscal year

* Label: *“Fiscal year”*
* Control: dropdown.
* Options: fiscal years formatted like {{2024–2025}}, {{2023–2024}}, etc.
* Default to current fiscal year.
* Changing fiscal year updates the available period values (months/quarters) but does not reset sites or file format.

h4. 2.2 Reporting period

The reporting period area consists of *two controls* in the second column:

* Left dropdown (type): label *“Reporting period”*
** Options:
*** {{Single month}}
*** {{Quarter}}
*** {{Fiscal YTD}}
*** {{Custom date range}}
* Right control (value), behavior depends on type:
** *Single month* (default)
*** Value dropdown lists months ({{July}}, {{August}}, …, {{June}}) as interpreted within the selected fiscal year.
*** Period display in history: e.g., {{May 2025}}.
** *Quarter*
*** Value dropdown: {{Q1}}, {{Q2}}, {{Q3}}, {{Q4}}.
*** Period display in history: e.g., {{Q2 2025}}.
** *Fiscal YTD*
*** No separate value dropdown; UI may show disabled text like {{FY 2025 YTD}}.
*** Period display in history: {{FY 2025 YTD}}.
** *Custom date range*
*** Replace value dropdown with two date pickers: *From* and *To*.
*** Period display in history: {{MM/DD/YYYY – MM/DD/YYYY}}.
* Reporting period selection is required.

h4. 2.3 Sites/Locations selector

* Label: *“Sites/Locations”*.
* Control: multi-select dropdown with search and “select all” behavior.
* When opened, the panel contains:
** A *search box* at the top.
** A “Select all (N sites)” row.
** List of sites, each with a checkbox and site name.
** This list will vary based on the user’s permissions

*Behavior*

* *Search*
** Filters the visible site list as user types, case-insensitive.
* *Select all (filtered)*
** With no search term, “Select all (X sites)” selects all sites in the district.
** With a search term, “Select all (N sites)” selects all currently filtered sites only.
* *Closed-state summary text*
** No sites selected → {{No sites selected}}.
** All sites selected → {{All sites selected (X sites)}}.
** One site selected → show the site name, e.g., {{Lincoln HS}}.
** More than one site selected → {{Multiple (N sites)}}.

h4. 2.5 Download

* On clicking the export button, it will create a Power BI report from where the user can generate a PDF.

----

h3. 3. Buttons and Behavior

Buttons appear left-to-right: *Reset | Apply | Generate Report*

h4. 3.1 Reset



* Resets parameters to defaults:
** Fiscal year = current fiscal year.
** Reporting period = {{Single month}} + current fiscal month.
** Sites/Locations = all sites selected.
** File format = {{PDF}}.

*3.2 Generate Report*

* Triggers creation of an Excel workbook with the following rules:
** *One workbook per Generate action.*
** If a single site is selected:
*** Workbook contains one P&L worksheet named with that site (e.g., {{Lincoln HS}}).
** If multiple sites are selected:
*** Workbook contains *one worksheet per site*, each named with the site’s name.
*** If ‘All’ is selected, consolidate data from all sites within the district in one report.
*** If a line in the report doesn’t have any data, indicate so using a ‘-‘ and use zero in the backend for calculations

----

h3. 5. Consistency With GL and Journal

* Layout of the parameter card, warning line, button row, and Recent Reports table must align visually with the *General Ledger Report* and *Journal Report* pages.

For the backend side of the report:

* user selects a reporting period (example: january 1 to january 31)
* create a revenue table
* revenue table must include one row for each gl account that meets both of these conditions:
** account type = revenue
** account appears in at least one manual entry line (debit or credit) where the posting date is inside the selected reporting period
* row label must use the gl account name exactly as stored in the gl accounts table
* for each revenue gl account row, system must calculate:
** description = gl account name
** current period = (sum of credits in period) – (sum of debits in period)
** prior period = (sum of credits in prior period) – (sum of debits in prior period)
** year-to-date = cumulative (credits – debits) from fiscal year start up to the end date of the selected reporting period
** *% of Revenue:* 
*** *if total revenue > 0 → (current period ÷ total revenue) × 100, display as "XX.XX%"*
*** *if total revenue ≤ 0 → display "-" and show warning banner above report: "Total revenue is zero or negative for this period. Percentage calculations have been suppressed."*
** % change from prior period =
*** if prior period ≠ 0 → (current – prior) ÷ prior
*** if prior period = 0 → -
* total revenue = sum of all current period revenue values across all revenue gl accounts
* these exact revenue values (each row + totals) must be used by the dashboard
* create an expenses table
* expenses table must include one row for each gl account that meets both of these conditions:
** account type = expense
** account appears in at least one manual entry line (debit or credit) where the posting date is inside the selected reporting period
* row label must use the gl account name exactly as stored in the gl accounts table
* for each expense gl account row, system must calculate:
** description = gl account name
** current period = (sum of debits in period) – (sum of credits in period)
** prior period = (sum of debits in prior period) – (sum of credits in prior period)
** year-to-date = cumulative (debits – credits) from fiscal year start up to the end date of the selected reporting period
** *% of Expenses:* 
*** *if total expenses > 0 → (current period ÷ total expenses) × 100, display as "XX.XX%"* 
*** *if total expenses ≤ 0 → display "-" and show warning banner above report: "Total expenses are zero or negative for this period. Percentage calculations have been suppressed."*
** % change from prior period (if selected period is not a custom date range, e.g. Quarter 1, January) =
*** if prior period ≠ 0 → (current – prior) ÷ prior
*** if prior period = 0 → -
* total expenses = sum of all current period expense values across all expense gl accounts
* these exact expense values (each row + totals) must be used by the dashboard
* *Note: If the user selects a custom date range, do not show any columns involving comparison with prior periods.* 
* If no records are available, indicate in export that “No records were found” 
* if total revenue ≤ 0 AND total expenses ≤ 0 → "Total revenue and expenses are zero or negative for this period. Percentage calculations have been suppressed."
NXT-68643.desc: As a *District Finance User*, I need to generate a Revenue and Expenditure (P&L) report for a specific fiscal year and reporting period, for one or many sites, and receive an Excel workbook where each selected site has its own P&L worksheet.

NXT-68641.summary: Fin - Journal Report – Location Selection, Export Generation, and Recent Journal Reports
NXT-68641.ac:
* Tooltip “You’ll be notified once your file is ready and can download it from the Document Center.”
* Standard Multi-selection dropdown with search feature 
* From (Posted Date): Standard date picker labeled “From (Posted Date)”.
* To (Posted Date): Standard date picker labeled “To (Posted Date)”.
* Export info tooltip: “We’ll notify you when your document is ready. You’ll find it in the Document Center.”
* Multi-location “+X more” tooltip: On hover over “+X more”, show the remaining locations in a simple list, one per line (e.g., Adams Elementary / Jefferson HS / Central Office / Sunrise MS).
* Tooltip to indicate that all selected locations will be within one worksheet in that workbook.
* If no records are available, indicate in export that “No records were found”
NXT-68641.desc: As a *District Finance User*, I need to generate a Journal export for one or more locations (All, District-level, or specific sites). All locations in case of multi-selection will be within one worksheet in the Excel file.

NXT-68637.summary: Fin - General Ledger Report – Account Selection, Export Generation
NXT-68637.ac:
* Tooltip says “You’ll be notified once your file is ready and can download it from the Document Center.”
* Account selection uses a standard multi-select with a search bar.
* Search filters accounts by code or name after 2+ characters.
* Selected accounts appear as removable chips (code + name + “×”), with a “Clear All” action and a count label like “SELECTED (4)”.
* Search results are shown as a list
* Date range fields “From (Posted Date)” and “To (Posted Date)” use the standard date picker and are required.
* Always show the processing notice: “We’ll notify you when your document is ready. You’ll find it in the Document Center.”
* On generating, give an informative note: -“Each selected account will be exported to its own worksheet within a single Excel workbook.”-
** The success message should be “Each selected account will be exported as a separate Excel file.”
* If no records are available, indicate in export that “No records were found” 

Updates:

Put the text that says “each selected account…” above the accounts drop down. once the report is generated, display the text in the green text box you showed for consistency.
NXT-68637.desc: As a District Finance User,  I need to select a group of ledger accounts, choose a posted date range, and generate an Excel export containing account-level worksheets.

NXT-68635.summary: Fin - Reports Landing Page – Report Selection
NXT-68635.ac:
h2. *Report Cards Section*

h3. *Reports Displayed*

The page must display *three* report tiles:

# *General Ledger*
_Description:_ “Export detailed debits and credits with per-account worksheets.”
# *Journal*
_Description:_ “Export a list of all financial postings in transaction order for a selected date range.”
# *Revenue & Expenditure (P&L)*
_Description:_ “View revenue and expense activity summarized by category for a selected period.”

h3. *Tile Requirements*

Each tile must include:

* Report name
* The description stated above
* Entire tile is clickable and opens the report’s configuration page
* Sorted A-Z

Visual behavior:

* Hover state uses elevated shadow
* Click leads to the specific report’s configuration screen
NXT-68635.desc: As a *District Finance User*, I need a Reports page that clearly presents each financial report I can generate (General Ledger, Journal, Revenue & Expenditure (P&L)) so I can quickly produce required financial statements.

NXT-68570.summary: Fin - Lock GL Account Base Type After Activity Is Posted
NXT-68570.ac:
h3. Account Categories

* *When* a user attempts to edit the *Base Type* of an existing Account Category:
** *If* any GL account assigned to that category has *one or more posted transactions in any fiscal year*,
** *Then* block the save and show this error message:
** *“Base Type cannot be changed once transactions have been posted to accounts under this category.”*
** If conditions are met for category deletion, 
*** Mention : “*This category has accounts with posted transactions, so you can only reassign it to a category with the same base type.*”
*** Provide the option to bulk reassign to another category
*** Inform user that if its a new category, they have to go back to the categories page and create a new category

----

h3. Individual GL Accounts Page

* *When* a user edits a GL Account and attempts to change its *Account Category*:
** *If* that GL Account has *any posted transactions in any fiscal year*, *and*
** The new category’s *Base Type* is *different* from the account’s current Base Type,
** *Then* block the save and show this error message:
** *“This account has posted transactions, so you can only reassign it to a category with the same base type.”*
NXT-68570.desc:
As a district finance administrator,
I want the GL account “Base Type” (Asset / Liability / Equity / Revenue / Expense) to be editable *only* on accounts that have never been used,
so that we can correct configuration mistakes before go-live without silently changing the accounting behavior of accounts that already have posted history.

NXT-68549.summary: Fin - Generate Journal Excel export for selected posted date range
NXT-68549.ac:
----

* *Workbook and sheets*
** System must generate a single {{.xlsx}} file per export action.
** All transactions should be in one sheet.
** Sheet name must follow this pattern: {{Jrn_<JournalID>}} (example: {{Jrn_102345}}), truncated as needed to fit Excel’s 31-character sheet name limit.

----

* *Header section (per journal sheet)*
Layout must follow the *Journal Template*.
** *Title row*
*** Row 2 must contain a merged title across columns A–I:
**** {{Journal – [Posted Date (From)] to [Posted Date (To)] for [Location]}}.
*** {{[Posted Date (From)]}}, {{[Posted Date (To)]}}, and {{[Location]}} values must be taken directly from the UI parameters used to generate the export. 
** *Journal info block (A3:B7)*
*** A3: label {{Report}} | B3: value {{Journal}}.
*** A4: label {{Location}} | B4: location name (from UI parameter).
*** A5: label {{Journal Date}} | B5: journal header date (date of export)
*** -A6: label Fiscal Year | B6: fiscal year label for the journal.-
** *Range & totals block (D3:E6)*
*** D3: label {{Posted Date (From)}} | E3: posted date from (UI parameter).
*** D4: label {{Posted Date (To)}} | E4: posted date to (UI parameter).
*** D5: label {{Total Debits (for this report)}} | E5: sum of Debit amounts for all exported lines of this journal.
*** D6: label {{Total Credits (for this report)}} | E6: sum of Credit amounts for all exported lines of this journal.
*** {{Total Debits (for this report)}} and {{Total Credits (for this report)}} must reflect only the lines of that journal that are included in this export after applying the posted date filter.

----

* *Journal lines table (per journal sheet)*
** Row 10 must contain the section label:
*** A10: value {{Journal Lines}}.
**** This label may be merged across A10:I10 for visual clarity, but cell A10 must contain the text.
** Row 11 must contain the column headers in this exact order (left to right):
*** A11: {{Posted Date}}
*** B11: {{Transaction ID}}
*** C11: {{Account Number}}
*** D11: {{Account Name}}
*** E11: {{Description}} (manual entries for now only!) (show only first 20 characters, user needs to then click on cell to see full text)
*** F11: {{Debit}}
*** G11: {{Credit}}
*** H11: {{Entered Date}}
*** I11: {{Entered By}}
** All journal lines that:
*** belong to the journal for this sheet, and
*** fall within the selected posted date range
must be exported starting from row 12 downward.
** Sorting for journal lines must be:
*** Primary: {{Posted Date}} ascending.
*** Secondary: {{Transaction ID}} ascending (or the system’s internal posting sequence when Transaction ID is not unique).

----

* *Debits and credits integrity*
** For each journal sheet, the sum of column F ({{Debit}}) must equal the {{Total Debits (for this report)}} value in cell E5.
** For each journal sheet, the sum of column G ({{Credit}}) must equal the {{Total Credits (for this report)}} value in cell E6.
** If the underlying journal is unbalanced at the database level, the export must still reflect the true amounts:
*** {{Total Debits (for this report)}} and {{Total Credits (for this report)}} must be calculated from the exported lines without adjustment, even if they are not equal.

----

* *Date-range behavior*
** The journal export must support journals that span multiple fiscal years; the system must not block exports that cross fiscal year boundaries.
** Posted Date (From), Posted Date (To), and Location values displayed in the title and header blocks must always be taken from the UI filters used to generate the export.

----

* *Numeric formatting*
** Debit and Credit columns (F and G) must:
*** use a numeric or accounting format with exactly 2 decimal places; and
*** be right-aligned.
** Zero amounts in Debit and Credit columns must be displayed consistently across the product:
*** always as {{0.00}}

----

* *File naming*
** The exported file name must follow this pattern:
*** {{Journal_Export_<YYYYMMDD>_<HHMM>_<DistrictCode>.xlsx}}
** {{<YYYYMMDD>}} must be the export date in year-month-day format.
** {{<HHMM>}} must be the export time in 24-hour hours-minutes format.
** {{<DistrictCode>}} must be the district identifier used elsewhere in the system.
NXT-68549.desc:
When the user runs the Journal report (separate screen) and clicks *Export to Excel*, the system must generate an {{.xlsx}} workbook that contains *one worksheet per journal* included in the result set, using the *Journal Template* layout we defined:

* Journal-level summary at the top
* Detailed journal lines in a table below
* Per-journal totals for debits and credits.

Parameter selection (posted date from/to, journal filters, etc.) is defined in another story.

NXT-68548.summary: Fin - General Ledger Excel Export (Per Account)
NXT-68548.ac:
* *Workbook and sheets*
1.1. System generates a single {{.xlsx}} file per export action.
1.2. The workbook must contain *one worksheet per account* returned in the GL report.
1.3. Sheet name pattern: {{<AccountNumber> - <ShortAccountName>}} (truncated if needed to fit Excel’s 31-character limit).
* *Header section (per account sheet)*
Each sheet must follow the ledger template layout:
** Row 1 – merged title {{General Ledger}} --- [Account Number] [Account Name].-
** {{Account info}} block (cells A3:B6):
