*** A3: “Account Name” | B3: account name
*** A4: “Account Number” | B4: full account code
*** A5: “Account Type” | B5: Asset / Liability / Equity / Revenue / Expense
*** A6: “Account Category”
*** -A6: “Fiscal Year” | B6: fiscal year label (e.g., FY 2024–2025 or 2024-07-01 – 2025-06-30).-
** {{Report range & balances}} block (cells D3:E8):
*** D3: “Posted Date (From)” | E3: report {{start_date}}
*** D4: “Posted Date (To)” | E4: report {{end_date}}
*** D5: “Beginning Balance” | E5: balance from GL engine = _ending balance as of the day before_ {{start_date}} (i.e., after applying all transactions with {{posted_date ≤ start_date − 1}}, per account-type rules)
*** D6: “Period Debits” | E6: total debits in [start_date, end_date] for this account.
*** D7: “Period Credits” | E7: total credits in [start_date, end_date] for this account.
*** D8: “Ending Balance” | E8: ending balance from GL engine (as of {{end_date}}).
2.1. Values must match exactly the on-screen GL report for that account.
*** D9: “Current Balance” separate field – ending balance as of date the report is run
* *Transactions table (per account sheet)*
3.1. Row 9: label {{Journal Lines}} (or {{Transactions}}) in A9.
3.2. Row 10 must contain the column headers (frozen for scrolling):
** A10: Date (posted date used for GL calculations)
** -B10: Fiscal Year (e.g., FY 2024–2025)-
** C10: Transaction ID (unique line or document identifier)
** D10: Description (line-level description / memo)
** E10: Debit/Credit (single character or word: {{D}} or {{C}})
** F10: Amount (absolute value of the entry)
** G10: Running Balance
* 3.3. All GL lines for this account within the selected date range must be written from row 11 downward, *sorted by*:
** Posted Date ascending
* *Running Balance calculation*
1. Which accounts use which rule
** *Group A (Debit-normal accounts)*
*** Asset
*** Expense
*** *For Assets/Expenses, debits add, credits subtract from the balance.* 
** *Group B (Credit-normal accounts)*
*** Liability
*** Equity / Fund Balance
*** Revenue
*** *For Liabilities/Equity/Revenue, credits add, debits subtract from the balance.*
* The backend already knows the account type for each sheet.
** Starting point for the first line
*** Let:
**** *BeginningBalance* = value in header cell *E5* for that account.
**** For the *first transaction line* (row 13):
***** We will calculate *G13 (Running Balance)* using the rules below (Group A or Group B).
***** Group A – Assets & Expenses (debit-normal)
****** Use this logic *only if account type is Asset or Expense*:
****** *First line (row 13)*
****** If *E13 = "D"* → {{G13 = BeginningBalance + F13}}
****** f *E13 = "C"* → {{G13 = BeginningBalance - F13}}
****** *Each next line (row n > 13)*
****** If *En = "D"* → {{Gn = G(n-1) + Fn}}
****** If *En = "C"* → {{Gn = G(n-1) - Fn}}
***** For Assets/Expenses, *debits add*, *credits subtract* from the balance.
***** Group B – Liabilities, Equity, Revenue (credit-normal)
****** Use this logic *only if account type is Liability, Equity/Fund Balance, or Revenue*:
****** *First line (row 13)*
****** If *E13 = "D"* → {{G13 = BeginningBalance - F13}}
****** If *E13 = "C"* → {{G13 = BeginningBalance + F13}}
****** *Each next line (row n > 13)*
****** If *En = "D"* → {{Gn = G(n-1) - Fn}}
****** If *En = "C"* → {{Gn = G(n-1) + Fn}}
***** For Liabilities/Equity/Revenue, *credits add*, *debits subtract* from the balance.
***** What must always be true
****** If there is at least one transaction line: The *last Running Balance* value (bottom of column G) *must equal* the *Ending Balance* in header cell *E8* for that sheet.
****** If there are *no transaction lines* for that account in the period:
****** Leave column G empty.
****** *BeginningBalance = EndingBalance* in the header (E5 = E8).
* *Fiscal-year consistency*
5.1. This export assumes the date range has already passed the “single fiscal year” validation (that logic is handled by the GL UI story).
5.2. Export must *not* re-implement date validation, but it must include the correct Fiscal Year label based on the backend’s fiscal-year configuration (assume a default of 7/1 to 6/30 if nothing is set
* *Numeric formatting*
6.1. Amount and balance columns (F, G) must use an accounting/number format with 2 decimal places and right alignment.
6.2. Negative balances should appear with a leading minus sign (or parentheses if consistent with rest of product).
* *File naming*
7.1. Default filename pattern: {{GL_Export_AccountCode.xlsx}}
NXT-68548.desc:
When the user runs the General Ledger report and clicks *Export to Excel*, the system must generate an {{.xlsx}} workbook that contains *one worksheet per GL account* included in the report, using the *Ledger Template* layout we defined:

* Account-level summary at the top
* Detailed transaction lines in a table below
* Balances consistent with the GL calculation rules (assets/liabilities/equity permanent; revenue/expense fiscal-year based).

Parameter selection (which accounts, date range, etc.) is handled by another story; this story starts from the moment the backend has already computed the GL dataset.

NXT-68312.summary: Fin - Configure Site-Level GL Sub-Accounts for Each District Account
NXT-68312.ac:
*Site Account Codes Section Display:*

* Edit GL Account drawer shows "Site Account Codes" section below Base Type field
* Section header displays "Site Account Codes" in bold
* Below header, gray text reads: "-Configure sub-accounts for each location. Each site should have a unique account code that includes its site code. If a site account code is left blank, that site will use the main account code for reporting."-
** Updated to “Set unique site account codes per location; blank sites default to the main account code.”
* Section contains checkbox labeled "Use this account for the entire district" (unchecked by default)
* Purple "Autofill Site Account Codes" button appears to the right of the checkbox
* Table displays below with three columns: LOCATION, SITE CODE, SITE ACCOUNT CODE
* One row appears for each active site in the district
* A search field above the Site Account Codes table lets users filter sites by location name or site code in real time.

*Manual Site Account Entry:*

* Each table row shows location name (non-editable), site code in blue/purple text (non-editable), and editable text input field
* Site Account Code input fields have placeholder text "Enter site account code..."
* Users can type or edit values in Site Account Code fields without validation while typing
* Clicking Update Account saves all entered Site Account Code values
* Reopening the drawer shows previously saved values
* *Site-level defaults*: If a site account code is left blank, that site automatically uses the main GL account code for reporting.

*Autofill Functionality:*

* Clicking "Autofill Site Account Codes" takes the Account Code value from top of form (e.g., 1100-0001-0000-000-00000)
* System identifies the site segment position from account code structure configuration
* System replaces site segment (000) with each site's actual code
* All Site Account Code fields populate with generated values (e.g., 1100-0001-0000-602-00000 for site 602)
* Users can manually edit any autofilled value after generation
* If Account Code field is empty or invalid, error message displays: "Cannot autofill: Account Code is invalid or empty"
* No fields populate if Account Code is invalid

*Uniqueness Validation:*

* Clicking Update Account validates no two sites have identical Site Account Code values
* If duplicates exist, save is blocked
* Red error message appears at top of drawer: "Each site must have a unique account code."
* Input fields containing duplicate values display with red border and red text
* Drawer remains open with all values preserved
* User can correct duplicates and save again
* Once duplicate value is changed to be unique, red border and text color return to normal
* Uniqueness check only applies within this GL account (same codes can exist across different GL accounts)

*District-Wide Checkbox Behavior:*

* Checking "Use this account for the entire district" immediately clears all Site Account Code input values
* Checked state disables all Site Account Code input fields (gray background, not editable)
* Checked state disables the Autofill Site Account Codes button
* Informational text changes to: "This account will be used district-wide."
* Unchecking the checkbox re-enables Site Account Code fields (white background, editable)
* Unchecking leaves all fields empty (no auto-population)
* Unchecking re-enables the Autofill button
* Unchecking returns informational text to: "-Configure sub-accounts for each location. Each site should have a unique account code that includes its site code.-"
** Updated to “Set unique site account codes per location; blank sites default to the main account code.” 
* Saving with checkbox checked stores sameAccountAcrossDistrict: true and does not save site account codes
* Saving with checkbox unchecked stores sameAccountAcrossDistrict: false and saves all entered site account codes
* When "Use this account for the entire district" is checked, the Site Account Codes section (including search and grid) is hidden, and all sites use the main GL account code for reporting.

*State Persistence:*

* Reopening Edit GL Account drawer for a saved account shows checkbox state matching last save
* If district-wide was enabled: checkbox is checked, all Site Account Code fields are empty and disabled, Autofill button is disabled
* If district-wide was disabled: checkbox is unchecked, Site Account Code fields show previously saved values, Autofill button is enabled, empty fields remain empty

*Table Scrolling:*

* Districts with more than 10 sites display scrollable table
* Table header row (LOCATION, SITE CODE, SITE ACCOUNT CODE) remains fixed while scrolling
* -Maximum visible height before scrolling: approximately 400px-

*Cancel Behavior:*

* Clicking Cancel closes drawer without saving
* All changes including autofilled values are discarded
* Next time account opens, previously saved values display

*Scope:*

* Site Account Codes section only appears in General Ledger Accounts configuration tab's Edit GL Account drawer
* Other configuration tabs (Account Code Structure, Account Categories, Account Mapping, Sites, Year End Mapping) remain unchanged
NXT-68312.desc:
As a *district finance administrator*, 

I want to define GL account codes for each school site (and optionally use one district-wide account instead), 

so that transactions can post to the correct sub-accounts while still keeping a clear, maintainable chart of accounts.

*Dev Notes:*

* Site list comes from active sites in Sites in System
* Site code segment position determined by Account Code Structure configuration
* Autofill logic must respect configured segment lengths and formats from Account Code Structure

NXT-68295.summary: Track transaction reversals in data model (Backend)
NXT-68295.ac:
* System knows which transaction reversed another transaction (queryable from either direction)
* System knows if a transaction has been reversed
* Reversal metadata is stored: reason, user, timestamp

NXT-68294.summary: Fin - Reverse transactions from General Ledger (UI)
NXT-68294.ac:
h2. UI View: 

* *Reversal Action:* Users can click the reverse icon (↻) on any transaction row in the Journal or Ledger view.
* *Reversal Drawer:* Clicking the icon opens a side drawer displaying concise original transaction details (Transaction ID, Description, Amount).
* *Reason for Reversal:*
** The drawer includes a required text area for the "Reason for Reversal".
** The field is pre-filled with  *"REVERSAL OF #[Original_ID]: "* (e.g., _"REVERSAL OF #10004: Duplicate entry"_) which cannot be erased.
** Users must enter a reason after this prefix to proceed.
** In the journal and ledger export, the Description field will be prefixed with *"REVERSED BY #[Reversal_ID]: "* (e.g., _"REVERSED BY #10050: Equipment Purchase"_) for the original transaction. However, the description remains unchanged in the UI.
* *Resulting Reversal Transaction:*
* A new transaction is created immediately following the original transaction.
* Its description is set to the text entered in the reason field (e.g., "REVERSAL: Duplicate entry").
* It displays a clickable badge: *"REVERSAL OF #XXXXX"*. Clicking this badge opens the details drawer for the _original_ transaction.
* *Original Transaction Update:*
** The original transaction's description remains unchanged.
** It displays a clickable badge: *"REVERSED BY #XXXXX"*. Clicking this badge opens the details drawer for the _reversal_ transaction.
** *Visibility:* Both the original and the reversal transactions remain visible in the journal/ledger.
** *Restriction:* Reversing a transaction that is already a reversal is not allowed (implied by logic, though explicit enforcement logic might need verification if not already present).

h2. Backend View:

* System knows which transaction reversed another transaction (queryable from either direction)
* System knows if a transaction has been reversed
* Reversal metadata is stored: reason (description), user, timestamp

NXT-68291.ac:
* *Transaction ID Generation*: When user clicks "Make Entry" button, system generates a unique transaction ID for the journal entry that can be used for tracking and reference
* *Description Recording*: System saves the Description/Reason entered by the user and associates it with all line items in the transaction
* *Debit Line Recording*: For each row with a debit amount, system creates a debit record in the general ledger with the selected account, debit amount, transaction ID, date, and description
* *Credit Line Recording*: For each row with a credit amount, system creates a credit record in the general ledger with the selected account, credit amount, transaction ID, date, and description
* *Multi-Line Transaction Linking*: All debit and credit lines entered in a single submission are linked together by the same transaction ID, allowing the complete entry to be viewed as one cohesive transaction
* *Site Code Assignment - Site Level*: When a specific site is selected (e.g., "Lincoln High School - 001"), system extracts the numeric site code (001) and embeds it in the account structure as the site segment (e.g., 1000-2000-3000-001-50000)
* *Site Code Assignment - District Level*: When "Post to District Level" checkbox is checked, system uses "000" as the site segment in the account structure (e.g., 1000-2000-3000-000-50000) instead of a specific site code
* *GL Account Mapping*: System records transactions using the GL account codes selected from the account dropdown (e.g., "1010 - Cash in Bank" records as account 1010 with appropriate site segment)
* *Entry Type Recording*: System marks all entries as "Manual Entry" type for reporting and audit purposes
* *Balance Verification*: System verifies one final time that total debits equal total credits before committing the transaction to the general ledger
* *Transaction Confirmation*: After successful recording, system displays confirmation message with the transaction ID and returns user to the Journal View or Ledger View showing the newly created entry
* *Audit Trail*: System records the username and timestamp for audit and compliance tracking
* *Reversibility*: Recorded transactions can be identified by their unique transaction ID for future reversal or adjustment if needed
NXT-68291.desc:
As a School Finance Administrator, 

I want my manual journal entries to be recorded with unique identifiers and proper site allocation, 

so that I can track all transactions and ensure they are correctly attributed to the appropriate site or district level.

NXT-67677.ac:
* *When a user attempts to delete a category from the Account Categories page:*
** *Side drawer opens* displaying the category to be deleted and a warning if accounts are linked: "This category has [X] accounts assigned to it. Categories cannot be deleted until all accounts are reassigned to another category."
** *Bulk reassignment required*: A dropdown shows all available categories (excluding the one being deleted). User must select a replacement category to bulk reassign all accounts, or click Cancel to manually reassign on the GL Accounts page first. Deletion is blocked if no replacement is selected.
** *Helpful tooltips*: Instructions clarify "Choose one option: Select a category above to bulk reassign all accounts now, then delete • Or click Cancel to manually reassign accounts on the GL Accounts page first" + Note: "If you need to create a new category, click Cancel and use the 'Add Account Category' button above."
* -Accounts previously assigned to the deleted category show a warning icon in the Account Category column with blank/empty value on the GL accounts page-
* -Orphaned accounts appear in a validation warning banner: "⚠️ [X] accounts need category assignment before transactions can post."-
* -Any attempt to post a transaction to an orphaned account is blocked with error message: "Cannot post transaction - account [code] requires category assignment"-
* -Users must reassign orphaned accounts to a valid category before those accounts can accept transactions-
* When a new category is added on the Account Categories page, it immediately appears in the Account Category dropdown on the Add New Account form
* When a new category is added on the Account Categories page, it immediately appears in the Account Category filter dropdown on the GL Accounts page
* The counts on the accounted categories update based on the changes and the deleted category is no longer in the grid
* Account category column values in the GL account page update based on changes made here
NXT-67677.desc:
As a system
I need to prevent transactions from posting to orphaned accounts when their category is deleted
So that users maintain proper account categorization for financial reporting

NXT-67676.ac:
* When posting a transaction, capture the current account code and description at that moment in time
* Save this snapshot with the transaction permanently
* When generating reports, display the snapshotted account code and description from when the transaction was posted
* If a user edits an account after posting, old transactions continue showing the old account details
* Future postings capture and use the updated account details
* Historical reports always show what was true at the time of posting
NXT-67676.desc:
*As a* system
*I need to* snapshot account details when posting transactions
*So that* historical reports remain accurate even when accounts are edited

NXT-67675.ac:
* When I click "Add New Account", a popup form appears
* The form has fields: Account Code (required), Description (required, 50 character limit), Account Category (required dropdown)
* Account Code must be unique across all accounts
* Description must be unique across all accounts
** The Description field shows a live character count as I type (e.g., "35/50 characters used")
*** 50 character limit
* Form validates uniqueness before allowing save
* When I select an Account Category from the dropdown, the Base Type is automatically populated based on that category's base type
* Base Type field is read-only and displays automatically with one of these values: Revenue, Expenditures, Assets, Liabilities, Fund Balance
* After clicking Save, the system validates the Account Code against the configured state account structure
* -If the Account Code doesn't match the structure format, show a warning modal: "This account code doesn't match your configured account code structure. Do you want to continue?"- (since we will display a warning as the user is typing, this is redundant so removed by PO)
** If I click Continue in the warning modal, the account saves and the form closes
** The new account appears in the GL Accounts table with a warning triangle icon next to the account code
** When hovering over the warning icon, show tooltip: "This account code doesn't match your configured account code structure format"
** The validation warning banner count updates to reflect the new non-compliant account
* If the Account Code matches the structure, the account saves without warnings
* If validation fails for duplicate Account Code or Description, show appropriate error messages and prevent saving
* After clicking Cancel in the form or warning modal, the form closes without saving
NXT-67675.desc:
*As a* district financial manager
*I want to* add a new GL account through a form
*So that* I can expand my chart of accounts

NXT-67674.ac:
* When a user attempts to delete an account, check if it has any posted transactions
* If posted transactions exist, block deletion and show error: "This account has posted transactions and cannot be deleted"
* Are you sure? This account is mapped to [System Account Name]. You'll need to assign a new account to this system account before transactions can post.”
* If user clicks Yes in the confirmation modal, proceed with deletion
* After successful deletion, remove the account from all lists and dropdowns
* -If the deleted account was mapped to a system account, the mapping is removed-
* -A warning banner appears: "1 account requires mapping" (or updates the existing warning count)-
* -The system account that lost its mapping shows a warning indicator (page dealt with in separate story)-
* If user clicks No or Cancel in the confirmation modal, close the modal without deleting
NXT-67674.desc:
*As a* system
*I need to* prevent deletion of accounts that are in use
*So that* data integrity is maintained

NXT-67541.summary: Story 6: Save Account Code Structure with Effective Dates (UI)
NXT-67541.ac:
*Save Changes:*

* Clicking "Save Changes" button opens confirmation modal
* Modal includes:
** Header: "Save Account Code Structure"
** Warning: "This will create a new version and apply this structure to all past and future transactions. This cannot be undone."
** Version number that will be created (e.g., "This will be saved as Version 4")

*Save Behavior:*

* When user confirms:
** Create new version (Story 5 handles backend)
** Recode all past transactions and apply to all future transactions
** Close modal and return to Account Code Structure tab
** Show success message: "Account Code Structure saved successfully as Version [X]"
* "Cancel" button closes modal without saving
NXT-67541.desc:
*As a* district business administrator
*I want to* save my account code structure changes with effective dates
*So that* the correct structure applies to transactions from specific time periods

h3. Background

When users save changes to their account code structure (from Story 1), they need to specify when this version becomes active. This allows for retroactive application and mid-year changes.

NXT-67540.summary: User Story 5: Store and Manage Account Code Structure Versions (Backend)
NXT-67540.ac:
*What Gets Tracked:*

* Account Code Structure changes (segment additions, modifications, deletions)
* Category changes (creates, updates, deletes, imports)
* Account changes (creates, updates, deletes, imports)
* Mapping changes (creates, updates, deletes)
* System Account changes (creates, updates, deletes)

*History Record Storage:*

* Each change creates a history record with:
** Change Type (Account Code Structure, Category, Account, Mapping, System Account)
** Action (Created, Updated, Deleted, Imported)
** Changed By (user ID)
** Change Date (auto-set timestamp)
** Record values based on action type:
*** For Creates: Store the new values (after state)
*** For Updates: Store both before and after values of all changed fields
*** For Deletes: Store the values that existed before deletion (before state)
*** For Imports: Store the new values (after state) and batch ID to group related imports
** Batch ID (for imports only, to group related changes)

*History Retrieval:*

* System can retrieve all history records for a district
* Records can be filtered by Change Type, Action, and Date Range
* Records are sorted by Change Date descending (newest first) by default
* System supports pagination for large history sets

*Detail Display:*

* For Account Code Structure: Store segment configuration (before/after based on action)
* For Categories: Store category code, description, base type (before/after based on action)
* For Accounts: Store account code, description, category (before/after based on action)
* For Mappings: Store system account code, GL account ID/code (before/after based on action)
* For System Accounts: Store system account name (before/after based on action)
* For Imports: Store batch ID to retrieve all items imported together, along with their values
NXT-67540.desc:
*As a* system
*I want to* track all changes to account code structure, categories, accounts, mappings, and system accounts
*So that* users can view a complete audit trail of modifications

*Background:* Users need visibility into what changed, when, who made the change, and what the before/after values were across all general ledger configuration areas.

NXT-67539.ac:
"History" tab exists as the last tab in General Ledger Accounts page that tracks all changes across account code structure, categories, accounts, mappings, and system accounts.

*Tab Access:*

* Clicking "History" tab from the General Ledger Accounts page opens this view
* Info banner at top explains: "All changes to your account code structure, categories, accounts, mappings, and system accounts are tracked with timestamps. Click 'View Changes' to see full details of each modification."

*Filters:*

* Filter row at top with dropdowns for:
** Change Type (Account Code Structure, Category, Account, Mapping, System Account, All)
** Action (Created, Updated, Deleted, Imported, All)
** Date Range (date pickers for from/to)
* RESET and APPLY buttons on the right

*History Table:*

* Table displays all changes with columns:
** Change Type (e.g., "Account Code Structure", "Category", "Account", "Mapping", "System Account")
** Action (e.g., "Created", "Updated", "Deleted", "Imported")
** Changed By (user name)
** Change Date (date and time, sortable with arrow indicator)
** View Changes (link or icon button)
* Each column has a filter icon dropdown for quick filtering
* Changes are sorted by Change Date descending (newest first) by default
* Pagination at bottom showing "1 - 5 of 7 items" with page navigation controls
* Items per page dropdown selector

*View Changes Modal:*

* Clicking "View Changes" opens a modal showing:
** For Account Code Structure changes: Read-only view of segment configuration before and after the change
** For Category changes: Before and after values of category code, description, and base type
** For Account changes: Before and after values of account code, description, category, and base type
** For Mapping changes: Which system account was mapped/unmapped to which GL account (full codes and names)
** For System Account changes: Details of what was created or modified
** For Imported changes: List of all items imported in that batch with their details
* Modal includes timestamp and user who made the change
* All views are read-only with no edit controls

*Change Type → Action Mapping:*

*Account Code Structure:*

* Created: New segment added to the structure
* Updated: Existing segment modified (name, length, type, order changed)
* Deleted: Segment removed from the structure

*Category:*

* Created: New account category added manually
* Updated: Category code, description, or base type changed
* Deleted: Category removed from the system
* Imported: Account categories added via bulk import

*Account:*

* Created: New GL account added manually
* Updated: Account code, description, category, or parent account changed
* Deleted: GL account removed from the system
* Imported: GL accounts added via bulk import

*Mapping:*

* Created: System account mapped to a GL account for the first time
* Updated: System account remapped from one GL account to a different GL account
* Deleted: System account unmapped (mapping removed)

*System Account:*

* Created: New system account added to the module
* Updated: System account name changed
* Deleted: System account removed from the module

!image-20251028-012933.png|width=1082,alt="image-20251028-012933.png"!
NXT-67539.desc:
*As a* district business administrator
*I want to* view all changes made to my general ledger configuration
*So that* I can track them

NXT-67534.ac:
Loading a template:

* *"Load State-Defined Template" section includes:*
** -State dropdown (pre-populated with the district's state, shows all available states)-
*** Only show the state associated with that district and that cannot be edited. Hitting apply should still be an action the user takes. 
** "Apply Template" button
** The pre-selected state's template is not automatically loaded; the user must click "Apply Template" to load it.
** -Provide a banner saying “Quickly start with a draft based on publicly available state requirements. Please confirm with your finance department before saving.”-
*** Banner saying “This draft is based on publicly available state guidance. Please confirm with your finance department before saving.”
** -If the selected template is generic, display a warning: "We don't have a state-specific template on file. This is a commonly used structure, but please verify it matches your finance department’s requirements." (generic mapping done in separate story)-
*** No generic state in dropdown, no separate warning for generic template being applied 

Applying a template:

* If segments exist beyond the Site segment, clicking "Apply Template" opens a confirmation modal: "Applying this template will replace your current segments. Are you sure?"
* If only the Site segment exists, apply the template directly without confirmation.
* Upon confirmation, template segments are copied into the district's structure, replacing existing segments.
* After applying, all segments can be edited according to Story 1 rules.
* A new version is created only when the user clicks "Save Changes" (per Story 1 versioning).

Delete all segments:

* Clicking the "Delete All Segments" button opens a confirmation: "This will delete all segments except Site. Are you sure?"
* Upon confirmation, all segments except Site are deleted.
NXT-67534.desc:
*As a* district business administrator
*I want to* apply my state's template to my Account Code Structure
*So that* I can start from a compliant baseline and customize it

h3. Background

Districts need a starting point for their account code structure. We provide drafts of state-specific templates (from Story 2) that they can apply as their foundation.

!image-20251029-150858.png|width=1082,alt="image-20251029-150858.png"!

NXT-67533.ac:
h3. Template Storage:

* System stores one account code template per US state (identified by 2-letter state code)
* Each template contains three separate account structures:
** Revenue account structure
** Expense account structure
** Fund balance account structure
* For initial launch, all three structures will be identical within each state template
* The three-structure design supports future state requirements where revenue/expense/fund balance may differ

h3. Account Structure Definition:

Each account structure (revenue, expense, or fund balance) is defined by a list of segments. Each segment has:

* *Description* - Label for the segment (e.g., "Fund", "Function", "Object")
** Must be unique within that account structure
** Can repeat across the three structures (revenue can have "Fund" and expenses can also have "Fund")
* *Type* - Either "Numeric" or "Alphanumeric"
* *Segment Length* - How many characters this segment contains (must be at least 1)
* *Separator* - Character that appears after this segment (e.g., "-" or "."), or none if it's the last segment
* *Position* - Order of the segment (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.)
** Must start at 1 and increase sequentially with no gaps

h3. Business Rules:

* Each account structure must have at least one segment
* No limit on maximum number of segments
* Position numbers must be sequential (1, 2, 3...) with no skipped numbers
* Each state can only have one template

h3. Generic Templates:

* Some templates can be marked as "generic" for states where we don't have state-specific requirements
* When a district selects a generic template, they see this warning: _"We don't have a state-specific template on file. This is a commonly-used structure, but please verify it matches your finance department's requirements."_

h3. Template Usage:

* When districts set up their accounts (Story 3), they copy one of these state templates as their starting point
* Once copied, districts own their configuration—future updates to state templates don't affect them
* Districts always get the current version of the template when they copy it

!image-20251030-165800.png|width=948,alt="image-20251030-165800.png"!
NXT-67533.desc:
*As a* system administrator
*I want to* store state-defined account code templates
*So that* districts can copy them as a starting point for their account code structure

h3. Background

Each state has its own required account code structure for school nutrition reporting. We need to store these templates as reference data so districts can select and copy them into their own configuration (Story 3).

NXT-67532.ac:
----

h3. *A. Header Panel (Informational Banner)*

* Title: *Account Code Structure*
* Collapsible info box at top with this text (copy/paste-ready):

{quote}*Important Setup:* Think of your account code structure like creating a filing system for all your school nutrition transactions. This determines how every dollar coming in and going out gets categorized and tracked.

*What this affects:*

* How your financial data appears in state reports and board presentations
* The way meal program transactions are recorded and tracked
* Your ability to see spending and revenue by school, program, etc.

*State-Based Setup:* We’ll suggest a structure based on your state’s requirements, but you can modify it to match your district’s specific needs.

*Required Site Tracking:* Every account code includes a Site segment so you can track transactions by individual school location. This cannot be removed but can be repositioned in your code structure.

*Example:* {{199-3500-6100-001}} might represent General Fund – Food Services – Food Purchases – Lincoln Elementary

*Why this matters:* This structure helps you answer questions like “How much did Lincoln Elementary spend on food last month?” or “What’s our district’s total breakfast revenue?” The better organized this is up front, the easier your reporting will be all year.

*Before you start:* Check that this matches your district’s chart of accounts and state reporting requirements.{quote}

----

h3. *B. Add New Segment Form*

||Field||Details||
|*Description*|Text, required, ≤ 50 characters, unique. Character counter shows remaining chars.|
|*Type*|Dropdown: Numeric / Alphanumeric.|
|*Segment Length*|Positive integer.|
|*Separator*|Single character or “none”.|
|*Add Segment* button|Appends row to Segments table (bottom of current list).|
|*Load State-Defined Template* section|Dropdown “Select State” + disabled “Apply” button until state is chosen (handled in later stories).|
|*Character counter for segment*|Displays “X of 50 characters used”.|

----

h3. *C. Segments Table*

||Column||Behavior||
|Position|Drag handle (::) to reorder segments; order immediately reflected in preview.|
|Segment Description|Text (label). Site row is read-only for name.|
|Type|Badge (Numeric/Alphanumeric).|
|Segment Length|Displays configured integer.|
|Separator|Shows character or “(none)”. (special character only)|
|Segment Characters|Auto-calculated (length)|
|Edit|Pencil icon → inline edit modal.|
|Delete|Trash icon → confirmation modal; disabled for Site.|

Additional Rules:

* *Site segment* always present on first load as the first and only segment; cannot be deleted or renamed but may be reordered.
* Adding/editing segments re-computes total characters per row and preview in real time.
* Inline validation appears on blur or Save.
* No character limit on the total structure.

----

h3. *D. Live Account Code Preview*

* Shown below segments table.
* Updates immediately when segments change (order, separator, type, length, or Site settings).
* Displays two examples:
** *-Account Code Example:-* -structure without Site.-
** *Site Account Code Example:* appears only when “Include Site segment” is checked and shows Site placement + separator toggle effect.
* Help text below:
Site segment uses numeric site code (e.g., 345 for NHS). Site sub-codes are appended to create sub-accounts (e.g., 1000-2000-3000-345-50000.NHS).

----

h3. *-E. Site Segment Options-*

-(two checkboxes immediately below preview)-

# *-Include the Separator that comes before the Site segment in the Account Code-*
- – Visible only when “Include Site” is checked.-
# *-Include the Site segment in the Account Code-*
- – Checked by default.-
- – Unchecking hides the Site example and disables #1.-

----

h3. *F. Validation & Warnings*

* Description ≤ 50 chars and unique.
* Structure check before Save
** If the *total number of segments (including Site)* is *less than or equal to 3*, display this *gentle warning* (non-blocking):
**“Please make sure this structure matches what your finance team expects.”*
** This message appears just above the Save button in a yellow banner (not a popup) and does *not* block saving.
* Inline errors for invalid length or separator (> 1 char).
* Unsaved-changes guard (on navigate away).

----

h3. *G. History Panel (separate story but FYI):*

Located below the Add New Segment form or as a side drawer.

* A new version is created *only when the user clicks “Save Changes.”*
* Display at bottom of page: “"Last Updated on September 24, 2025 at 4:22 PM. View changes in the [History] tab."
(hyperlink on "History" opens the History tab in General Ledger Accounts page)

----

!image-20251028-005224.png|width=1082,alt="image-20251028-005224.png"!

!image-20251028-005251.png|width=1082,alt="image-20251028-005251.png"!
NXT-67532.desc:
*As* a district business administrator
*I want* to define and manage the account code structure
*So that* my district stays compliant and can report at site level

NXT-67005.ac:
* Display header: "Map Required System Accounts"
* Include explanatory text:
** *What is this?* "Mapping connects your district's specific account codes to our system's standard categories. It's like creating a translation guide between your filing system and ours."
** *Why needed?* "This ensures your financial data flows to the right places in reports and helps track federal program requirements accurately."
* Display progress indicator: "Progress: 0 of X required accounts mapped" (updates in real-time as user confirms mappings)

*System Account Cards:*

* Display each of the X required system accounts as individual cards containing:
** System account name (e.g., "Cash in Bank", "Food Purchases")
** Description of the account's purpose (e.g., "Primary operating cash account for food service")
** Confidence badge when suggestion available (e.g., "High Confidence", "Medium Confidence", "Low Confidence")
** Suggested mapping or dropdown selector

*For accounts with confident suggestions:*

* Display "Suggested: [Account Code] - [Account Description]" (e.g., "Suggested: 11000 - Operating Cash Account")
* Show two action buttons:
** "Confirm" button (primary/purple styling)
** "Choose Different" button (secondary/gray styling)

*For accounts without confident suggestions:*

* Display dropdown selector with placeholder text "Select an account"
* Dropdown options filtered by matching base type (Asset, Liability, Revenue, Expense, Equity) of the system account
* Dropdown shows: [Account Code] - [Account Description] for each option
* Dropdown includes search/filter capability

*When user clicks "Confirm":*

* Mapping is saved
* Card updates to show confirmed mapping with green checkmark icon
* Progress counter increments (e.g., "Progress: 1 of 28 required accounts mapped")
* "Confirm" and "Choose Different" buttons are replaced with "Change Mapping" button

*When user clicks "Choose Different":*

* Dropdown expands showing all imported accounts filtered by matching base type
* User selects an account from the dropdown
* Once selected, mapping is saved automatically
* Card updates to show confirmed mapping with green checkmark icon
* Progress counter increments
* Display "Change Mapping" button

*When user clicks "Change Mapping":*

* Card returns to selection state
* If there was an original suggestion, show "Suggested:" line again with "Confirm" and "Choose Different" buttons
* If there was no original suggestion, show dropdown selector
* Progress counter decrements

*Skipping accounts:*

* User can scroll past unmapped accounts without selecting anything
* "Next" button remains enabled even if not all X accounts are mapped
* When user clicks "Next" with unmapped accounts remaining, display warning modal:
** Title: "Incomplete Mapping Detected"
** Message: "You have [Y] required accounts that are not mapped. This may affect functionality and report accuracy. Reports relying on these accounts will show incomplete or incorrect data."
** List unmapped account names in the modal
** Two action buttons:
*** "Go Back and Complete" (secondary button)
*** "Continue Anyway" (primary button with warning styling)

*If user chooses "Continue Anyway":*

* Store list of unmapped accounts
* Proceed to Step 4
* Display persistent warning banner on Step 4: "[Y] required accounts remain unmapped. You can complete mapping later in Settings > GL Account Mapping"

*Navigation:*

* Include "Exit Wizard", "Back", and "Next" buttons
* "Back" button returns to Step 2 (Import Accounts) without losing confirmed mappings
* Store timestamp when user completes this step (when they click "Next")

*Technical notes:*

* System uses semantic matching to suggest mappings based on:
** Similar keywords in account names/descriptions
** Account type matching (Asset to Asset, Expense to Expense, etc.)
* Confidence level displayed based on match strength (specific algorithm TBD with dev team)
* All confirmed mappings persist if user exits wizard and returns later
NXT-67005.desc:
*As a* nutrition director setting up Financial Oversight

*So that* I can quickly complete the mapping process while maintaining control over which accounts are used for each financial category

NXT-67003.summary: Create financial periods as an option for period management
NXT-67003.ac:
* Financial periods are now an option under System → Management → Periods in ‘Generate Periods’ and ‘Manage Periods’.
* These periods include all functionalities available for POS and Inventory Periods.

For any fiscal year, under ‘Generate Periods’, you will see:

* Period name
* Period type: ‘Financial’
* Start Date and End Date
* Option to Split
* Edit, Delete, and view history of edits showing user actions

For any fiscal year, under ‘Manage Periods’, you will see:

* Select Period Type: ‘Financial’, Site Type, and a list of sites available for that customer for filtering
* Upon selecting a financial period, you will see:
** Period Name
** Period Type: ‘Financial’
** Start Date, End Date
** Status (X Open, Z-X Closed for Z number of sites, responding to site type and selection; for individual sites, it will only show Open/Close)
** Action to ‘Close All’ or ‘Reopen All’ (the ‘Reopen All’ option is greyed out if any site has an open period)
** History of actions along with the user

*So* I can enforce audit rules.

NXT-66929.ac:
<Remove this>

*Given* I am logged in as a Cafeteria Manager
*When* I access the Financial Oversight module
*Then* I should have the following permissions:

*Dashboard Access:*

* I can VIEW the Financial Oversight dashboard
* I cannot EDIT, ADD, DELETE, or have FULL access to dashboard elements

*Configuration Module - LIMITED VIEW ACCESS:*

* I can VIEW configuration settings but cannot modify them
* I do not have FULL access to the Configuration module
* GL Code Structure: VIEW only
* Account Mapping: VIEW only  
* Import/Export Templates: VIEW only
* Version History: VIEW only
* I cannot EDIT, ADD, or DELETE any configuration items

*Transaction Management Module - LIMITED VIEW ACCESS:*

* I can VIEW transaction management functions but cannot modify them
* I do not have FULL access to Transaction Management
* Manual Entries: VIEW only
* Post from POS/Inventory: No access (cannot VIEW, EDIT, ADD, or DELETE)
* Transaction Search: VIEW only
* Void/Reverse: No access (cannot VIEW, EDIT, ADD, or DELETE)
* I cannot EDIT, ADD, or DELETE any transactions

*Reports Module - VIEW ONLY ACCESS:*

* I can VIEW all reports but cannot modify them
* I do not have FULL access to reports
* Profit & Loss: VIEW only
* Balance Sheet: VIEW only
* Site-Level Analysis: VIEW only
* Export to ERP: No access (cannot VIEW, EDIT, ADD, or DELETE)
* I cannot EDIT, ADD, or DELETE any reports

*Period Management:*

* I have no access to period management
* I cannot VIEW, EDIT, ADD, DELETE, or have FULL access to period settings

*System Behavior:*

* Most permission checkboxes remain unchecked when Cafeteria Manager role is selected
* Only VIEW permissions are enabled for accessible modules
* Master toggle shows "Enable All" by default (since most permissions are disabled)
* I receive appropriate "Access Denied" messages when attempting restricted actions
* My limited permission set is maintained when switching between roles and returning to Cafeteria Manager
NXT-66929.desc: As a Cafeteria Manager, I need limited read-only access to Financial Oversight functions so that I can view relevant financial information without the ability to modify critical financial data

*Dashboard Access:*

* Import/Export Templates: FULL access (VIEW, EDIT, ADD, DELETE)
* Version History: FULL access (VIEW, EDIT, ADD, DELETE)

*Transaction Management Module - FULL ACCESS:*

* Manual Entries: FULL access (VIEW, EDIT, ADD, DELETE)
* Post from POS/Inventory: FULL access (VIEW, EDIT, ADD, DELETE)
* Transaction Search: FULL access (VIEW, EDIT, ADD, DELETE)

*System Behavior:*

NXT-66928.desc: As a Director, Central Office user, or SchoolCafe Administrator, I need full administrative access to all Financial Oversight functions so that I can oversee and manage all financial operations without restrictions

NXT-66898.ac:
* Display header: "Import Accounts"
* Include explanatory text:
** *What is this?* "Your Chart of Accounts is a complete list of all the 'buckets' your district uses to categorize money. It's like a filing system for every dollar that comes in or goes out."
** *Why import?* "Instead of typing hundreds of accounts manually, you can upload your district's existing list to save hours of setup time."
* Display the System import workflow with these steps:
** *Pre-Upload step:* Import Type dropdown (defaulted to "GL Accounts")
** *File Upload step:* Drag and drop file upload interface with "Drag and drop your file here or click to browse" text
** *Field Mapping step:* Interface for mapping uploaded file columns to system fields
** *Review step:* Final review and validation of the import

*File Upload:*

* When user clicks upload area or "click to browse", file explorer opens
* Progress bar displays while file uploads
* System automatically validates file against configured account code structure

*Validation Results:*

* Display validation results summary showing:
** Total accounts processed
** Number passed validation
** Number failed validation with specific error details
* Show detailed error messages like "Expected XXX-XXX-XXX format, got XXX-XXXXX format for 77 accounts" (customized to their account structure)

*If all accounts pass validation:*

* Accounts automatically imported
* Success confirmation displayed
* "Next" button enabled to proceed to Step 3

*If any accounts fail validation:*

* Display two options:
** "Fix and re-upload now" (allows immediate file correction and re-upload)
** "Continue with current import" (proceeds with flagged accounts)

*If user chooses "Continue with current import":*

* Display prominent warning: "X accounts were imported with validation errors and are flagged for correction. This may affect report accuracy until fixed."
* User can download original file with additional validation status column
* "Next" button enabled to proceed to Step 3

*Additional features:*

* User can download detailed error report
* All accounts imported regardless of validation status (valid and invalid)
* System stores audit trail (upload timestamp, username, validation results)
* If user re-uploads, new file replaces previous active Chart of Accounts

*Navigation:*

* Include "Exit Wizard", "Back", and "Next" buttons
* Store timestamp when user completes this step
NXT-66898.desc: *As a* nutrition director setting up Financial Oversight *I want to* import my district's Chart of Accounts and see validation results within the guided setup *So that* I can ensure my account codes match the expected format and have all my accounts available for mapping without leaving the setup wizard

NXT-66897.ac:
* The UI includes a banner at the top with the text at the bottom of the acceptance criteria
* The account code structure is set to the default structure for the customer's state based on mapping in the backend, or a general default template if no state-specific default exists
* The user can reorder, rename, edit, delete existing segments, add new segments
** The site segment cannot be deleted
* Display an example account number that updates as user makes changes to the structure
* Add a save, reset, and "skip for now" button:
** Save: saves changes and proceeds
** Reset: resets to the default account code structure
** Skip for now: displays a warning modal with the following text, then proceeds without saving: *Warning Modal Text:* "Warning: Proceeding without proper account structure setup may result in audit compliance issues and manual work for your finance team. You can complete this setup anytime under Configuration > Account Structure." *Modal should have two buttons:*
*** "Go Back and Set Up Now" (closes modal, returns to setup)
*** "Skip Anyway" (closes modal, proceeds without saving)
* The timestamp for when the user saves their account code structure and finishes the configuration wizard should be stored in the backend (will be used in another story)
* Include the following text in a banner at the top:

*"Set Up Your Account Code Structure"*

h2. The Real Problem:

{quote}*Auditors require consistent account formatting across all district financial systems.*

Without proper account structure setup:

* Auditors may flag inconsistent account formatting
* Data exports won't match your district's chart of accounts
* You'll need to manually create individual site accounts{quote}

h2. The Solution:

{quote}*Tell us your account format once:*

* Audit-ready reports that meet compliance standards
* We auto-generate all site accounts and related transactions in your format
* Exports work seamlessly with your finance system{quote}

h2. Simple Example:

_"If your accounts look like '100-51-6641-001', tell us what each part means. We handle the rest."_
NXT-66897.desc:
As a user setting up configuration,

I want to understand what this step does

So that I know what information to provide

NXT-66896.ac: * Upon launching the configuration wizard, the user sees an explanation of what they will have to do in the coming steps.
NXT-66896.desc:
As a user using the configuration wizard, 

I want to see the how and why of the process at a quick glance

So that I clearly understand why I need to follow this process.

NXT-66797.summary: GL Account Re-upload and Replacement Logic
NXT-66797.ac:
* *When user uploads a new file* (either from the "Fix and re-upload now" option or initiating a fresh upload):
** System processes the new file through the same validation workflow
** User sees the same decision point: continue with this upload or fix and re-upload
** *If user chooses to proceed* (whether validation passed completely OR had errors but they chose "Continue with current import"), this becomes the new active Chart of Accounts and replaces the previous active one
** *If user chooses to fix and re-upload again*, this file is discarded (not saved)
* *Only files that users choose to "proceed with" are saved* (regardless of validation status) with:
** Upload timestamps
** Username of uploader
** Validation results from when they chose to proceed
** The accounts that were actually imported (including flagged ones)
* *If user has changed the account code structure configuration* between uploads:
** System validates the new file against the current/updated account code structure
* *Previous active accounts are replaced* when a new file is implemented
NXT-66797.desc: As a district finance administrator, I want to be able to upload a new Chart of Accounts file that replaces my currently active account structure, so that I can correct validation errors or update my accounts while maintaining a complete audit trail of all files I've actually implemented.
NXT-66797.epic_summary: Import Feature for Charts of Accounts

NXT-66796.summary: GL Account Import Processing and Error Handling
NXT-66796.ac:
* *All accounts are imported regardless of validation results* (valid and invalid accounts alike)
* *If all accounts pass validation*: User receives success confirmation and all imported accounts are ready to use
* *If any accounts fail validation*:
** User sees specific error messages like "Expected XXX-XXX-XXX format, got XXX-XXXXX format for 77 accounts" (customized to their actual account code structure)
** User receives detailed error report showing exactly which accounts failed and why
** *User is presented with two options:*
*** *Fix and re-upload now*: User can correct the file and upload again immediately
*** *Continue with current import*: User accepts the current import with flagged accounts
* *If user chooses "Continue with current import"*:
** Display prominent warning: "X accounts were imported with validation errors and are flagged for correction. This may affect report accuracy until fixed."
** User can download their original file with an additional column indicating validation status for each account
** User proceeds to use the system with flagged accounts (can fix individual accounts later in the UI)
* *System stores audit trail data* for compliance (UI in separate story)
NXT-66796.desc: As a district finance administrator, I want all my Chart of Accounts to be imported even if some have validation errors, so that I don't lose my work and can fix problematic accounts later while still being able to use the valid ones.
NXT-66796.epic_summary: Import Feature for Charts of Accounts

NXT-66795.summary: GL Account File Upload and Basic Validation
NXT-66795.ac:
* The *Import* button is available on the _Import GL Accounts_ tab
* When the user clicks *Import*, a file explorer opens so they can select and upload their Chart of Accounts file
* A *progress bar* is displayed while the file is uploading, showing the status in real time
* After the upload completes, the system automatically validates the file against the configured account code structure
* The user sees a *validation results summary* showing total accounts processed, passed validation, and failed validation counts
NXT-66795.desc: As a district finance administrator, I want to upload my Chart of Accounts file and see validation results, so that I can understand whether my account codes match the expected format before importing.
NXT-66795.epic_summary: Import Feature for Charts of Accounts

NXT-66550.ac:
*Given* I am setting up my fiscal year in the Financials module
*When* I navigate to the fiscal year configuration page
*Then* I should see a field labeled "Starting Fund Balance" with a dollar input box

*Given* I enter my starting fund balance amount (e.g., $250,000)
*When* I save the configuration
*Then* the system should store this value and use it for all fund balance calculations

*Given* I have entered a starting fund balance and have YTD revenue/expenses
*When* I view any financial report or dashboard
*Then* my current fund balance should display as: Starting Fund Balance + YTD (Revenue - Expenses)

*Given* I have P&L data for the current fiscal year
*When* I generate compliance reports
*Then* the system should calculate months of operating expenses using the current fund balance

*Technical Notes:*

* Field should accept positive or negative values (some districts may start with deficit)
* Should be editable only by users with fiscal year setup permissions
* Value persists for entire fiscal year until next year's setup
* Used in fund balance calculations across all reports and dashboards
NXT-66550.desc:
*As a* school nutrition director
*I want to* enter my starting fund balance for the fiscal year
*So that* I can track my current fund balance and ensure compliance with the 3-month operating expense limit

NXT-66389.ac:
* Preconditions: Same as usage.
* Posting date = last day of inventory period.
* *Ledger entries*:
** _Withdrawal/Waste (GLEntryTypeCd: WD or WASTE depending on setup)_ → Debit Expense, Credit Asset.
* Rollbacks reverse entries.
* Entries flow into GL and financial statements as waste expense.
NXT-66389.desc:
As a *district inventory manager*,
I want withdrawals and waste to generate ledger entries,
So that unusable stock is removed from assets and recorded as expense.

NXT-66388.ac:
* Preconditions: Same as usage.
* Posting date = last day of inventory period.
* *Ledger entries*:
** _Warehouse Receives Commodity Fees (GLEntryTypeCd: None, Multisite)_ → Debit Site Expense, Credit Warehouse Expense.
* Rollbacks reverse entries.
* Entries flow into food cost/operational expense reporting.
NXT-66388.desc:
As a *district inventory manager*,
I want warehouse fees (delivery, processing) to create ledger entries,
So that costs are correctly captured as expenses.

NXT-66387.ac:
* Preconditions: Same as usage.
* Posting date = last day of inventory period.
* *Ledger entries*:
** _Warehouse Receives (GLEntryTypeCd: WHIRCV, Multisite)_ → Debit Site Asset, Credit Warehouse Asset.
** _Warehouse Commodity Value (GLEntryTypeCd: None, Multisite)_ → Debit Site Asset, Credit Warehouse Asset.
* Rollbacks reverse entries.
* Entries visible in GL journal/View Ledger, net effect is zero across district but shifts balances between locations.
NXT-66387.desc:
As a *district inventory manager*,
I want inventory transfers between sites/warehouses to generate ledger entries,
So that movement of stock is tracked without distorting total district-level assets.

NXT-66386.ac:
* Preconditions:
** Financial accounts mapped for valuation groups (_Inventory Asset, Inventory Expense_).
** All inventory periods up to posting date closed.
** Financial period open through posting date.
* Posting date = last day of inventory period.
* *Ledger entries per site*:
** _Usage (GLEntryTypeCd: WD)_ → Debit Expense, Credit Asset.
** _Commodity Usage (GLEntryTypeCd: COMMUSG, Value = Standard Price)_ → Debit Expense, Credit Asset.
* Rollbacks: Reverse entries if reposted.
* Entries visible in GL journal/View Ledger, flow to P&L as food expense and Balance Sheet as reduced inventory.
* The availability of Approve, Post, and Reapprove follows the checkbox state matrix (see attached rules).

!image-20250910-012415.png|width=545,height=145,alt="image-20250910-012415.png"!
NXT-66386.desc:
As a *district inventory manager*,
I want inventory usage (withdrawals for production) to create ledger entries,
So that food costs are captured as expenses and inventory balances decrease accordingly.

NXT-66385.ac:
* *Preconditions for posting*
** All required GL account mappings for reimbursement system accounts are configured:
*** _Due from Federal Funds_ (Asset, DUEFEDF)
*** _Cash in Bank_ (Asset, CASHBNK)
*** _Reimbursement Program System Accounts_ (Revenue, RMBRSMNT)
** Financial period is open from posting date through current date.
** POS reimbursement programs are configured for the district.
* *Posting date rules*
** _Reimbursement Claim_ (GLEntryTypeCd = RMBRSCLM) entries use the last day of the claim period as posting date.
** _Reimbursement Received_ (GLEntryTypeCd = RMBRSRCVD) entries use the current date as posting date.
* *Ledger entries (by site)*
** *Reimbursement Claim*
*** *Debit:* Due from Federal Funds (Asset)
*** *Credit:* Reimbursement Program System Accounts (Revenue)
** *Reimbursement Received*
*** *Debit:* Cash in Bank (Asset)
*** *Credit:* Due from Federal Funds (Asset)
* *By-site creation*
** Ledger entries are created by site for each claim and reimbursement.
* *Visibility and reporting*
** Entries are visible in the General Ledger journal and View Ledger pages.
** Users can drill down to see site-level breakdowns.
** Reimbursement claim and received amounts flow into financial statements as revenue and assets.
* *Rollbacks*
** If a claim is recreated and resubmitted, rollback ledgers are automatically generated before new postings.
** Rollback entries reverse the original postings:
*** For a *Reimbursement Claim* rollback:
**** *Debit:* Reimbursement Program System Accounts (Revenue)
**** *Credit:* Due from Federal Funds (Asset)
*** For a *Reimbursement Received* rollback:
**** *Debit:* Due from Federal Funds (Asset)
**** *Credit:* Cash in Bank (Asset)
** After rollback entries are posted, the new Claim and Reimbursement entries are created using the updated data.
NXT-66385.desc:
As a *district business administrator*,
I want reimbursement claims and payments from state and federal sources to flow into the general ledger,
So that my program’s revenue and receivables are accurately reflected in financial statements.

NXT-66384.summary: Fin - Push Meal Sales Revenue from Accountability to Financials for Automation
NXT-66384.ac:
*Requirements:*

* All Meal Sales Revenue accounts mapped to GL Accounts
* Sub-Accounts created for the different sites (look at how this is worded in the actual account code in financials)
* Ledger Entries are created by site.
* The last day of the POS Period is used as the Posting date. 
* Financial periods must be open from the date of posting to the current date.
* All POS periods that fall in the selected Financial period’s date range need to be closed.
* *When creating these ledger entries in the database, each debit/credit line must be attributed to the originating data point / data source (e.g., the specific meal sales revenue data point or reimbursement data point) so the Profit & Loss report can calculate totals by data point even when one GL account is mapped to multiple data points.* 

*When a POS period is closed:*

* Backend accesses the <meal sales revenue source> from the accountability database
* Ledger Entries are created in the tables associated with the given district with the following details:
* *Transaction Code:* MEALSLS
** *Transaction Description:* Meal Sales Revenue
*** *Accounts Debited:* <Cash In Bank GL Site Sub Account> AND <Prepaid Account GL Site Sub Account>
*** *Amount Debited:*
*** *Accounts Credited:* <Meal Sales AND A La Carte Sales GL Site Sub Accounts>
*** *Amount Credited:*
*** The *meal sales account* must align to the *student revenue source* (e.g., School Breakfast Program), and the *a la carte account* must align to the *student meal type* (e.g., Breakfast).

* Note that there may be multiple accounts involved on either side of the transaction. Validate that debit and credit totals are equal.
NXT-66384.desc:
*As a* nutrition director
*I want* POS meal sales to automatically post to the general ledger when I close a POS period
*So that* my meal revenue is accurately reflected in financial reports and I can track cash flow by payment method.

NXT-66380.ac:
* The *Import* button is available on the _Import GL Accounts_ tab.
* When the user clicks *Import*, a file explorer opens so they can select and upload their Chart of Accounts file.
* A *progress bar* is displayed while the file is uploading, showing the status in real time.
* After the upload completes, the system automatically validates the file against the configured account code structure.
* The user sees a *validation results summary* showing:
** Total accounts processed
** Number of accounts/account categories that passed validation
** Number of accounts/account categories that failed validation with specific error details
* *If all accounts pass validation*: The accounts are automatically imported and the user receives a success confirmation.
* *If any accounts fail validation*:
** User sees a message like “Expected XXX-XXX-XXX, got XXX-XXXXX for 77 accounts” customized as per their actual account code structure
** Give users the option to either fix it and upload again and continue anyway
*** If the user decides to continue anyway, display a prominent warning mentioning that this may affect report accuracy and that they should fix those accounts ASAP
** User receives a detailed error report showing exactly which accounts failed
*** They are able to download their file with an additional column indicating which accounts were and were not validated
** If the user uploads the file again
* Users can *download the error report*
NXT-66380.desc:
*As a* user

*I want* to import my chart of accounts

*So that* the journal entries reflect my actual accounts
NXT-66380.epic_summary: Import Feature for Charts of Accounts

NXT-66379.summary: Import script for chart of accounts (Proposed #3)
NXT-66379.ac:
* When the user uploads a Chart of Accounts template, the system reviews it against the district’s configured account code structure.
* The system identifies whether each account category and individual account aligns with the configured structure.
* The system produces a list showing which account codes are valid and which are not.
* The results are displayed in a table linked to the customer’s account, with a clear indicator of each account’s status (valid/invalid).
* If changes are made to the uploaded accounts, the table reflects the updates and their current validation status.
NXT-66379.desc:
*As a* user

*I want* to import my chart of accounts

*So that* the journal entries reflect my actual accounts

NXT-66123.ac:
* Create a table with these columns:
** *Site Name*
** *Month*
** *Status* (Not Ready, Ready to Post, Posted, Error)
** *Actions* (Post, View, Repost, Remove)
* Selecting a site displays a summary of various Inventory transactions.
* *Given* I'm attempting to post inventory data *When* the required periods are not properly configured (closed/open as needed) *Then* I should receive the message “To Approve or Post, close all open Inventory Periods up to the selected Financial Period's end date.
To Approve or Post, open all Financial Periods from the start date of the selected Financial Period to today's date.” via a tooltip with an info icon.

NXT-65486.ac:

NXT-65486.desc:

*So that* I can view accurate counts for each meal category for the current period, prior period, year-to-date totals, and related percentages without manual calculations.
