NXT-65485.summary: Create Stored Procedure to Apply Debit/Credit Rules and Post Transactions to General Ledger
NXT-65485.ac:
* When the stored procedure runs, it looks at the source transactions (e.g., meal sales, inventory postings, payroll entries) for the period you give it.
* For each transaction, it finds the matching debit and credit rule from our central rules table.
* It replaces the system account codes from the rule with the correct GL accounts for that customer, using their account mapping.
* It creates one debit line and one credit line for each transaction, making sure the amounts match so the ledger stays balanced.
* If a transaction can’t find a matching rule or account mapping, it still shows in the results, but is marked as needing a fix.
* The output will show all the information needed for the ledger entry — which accounts are debited and credited, the amount, the date, and where the transaction came from.
* Running the procedure again for the same period and data will always give the same results.
NXT-65485.desc:
*As a* finance/platform owner
*I need* a stored procedure that converts source transactions into *debit/credit lines* by applying *global rules keyed to system accounts* and resolving them to *tenant GL accounts* via the mapping table,
*So that* all modules post as per accounting rules and reports can rely on one source of truth.

NXT-65483.ac:
* *Columns to Display*
** Current Period
** Prior Period
** Current CPM (Cost per Meal)
** Prior CPM
** Current % of Revenue
** % Change from Prior Period
** Year-to-Date Total
** YTD CPM
** Item % of Category
* *Expense Categories*
** *Inventory-related expenses* (e.g., Cleaning Supplies, Supplies) from *Inventory Posting* module.
** *Payroll expenses* (e.g., Professional Salaries, Wages) from *payroll imports* or manual GL postings.
** *Purchased goods and services* (e.g., Professional Services, Operational Supplies) from *Accounts Payable* when invoices are posted to GL expense accounts.
** *Miscellaneous expenses* from *manual journal entries* or adjustments posted to expense codes.
* *Data Sources*
** General Ledger postings for accounts classified as “Expense” in the Chart of Accounts.
** Only include postings within the selected fiscal period that are in *open financial periods*.
** Group and display expenses by GL account code and description.
NXT-65483.desc:
*As a* district finance administrator
*I need* to view all operating expenses for the current, prior, and year-to-date periods in the Profit & Loss report
*So that* I can monitor costs, compare them against prior periods, and reconcile them to the general ledger.

NXT-65482.ac:
* *Display Columns (Data is sourced from Item Managemnt > Item Configuration > Valuation Categories*
** Valuation Category
** Valuation Method (e.g., FIFO, Last Received Price, Moving Average, Replacement Value, Standard Price)
** Asset Account (GL account for inventory value) (this is blank and to be filled in by the user, make it a searchable dropdown of all GL accounts so they can pick the relevant one)
** Expense Account (GL account for usage) (this is blank and to be filled in by the user, make it a searchable dropdown of all GL accounts so they can pick the relevant one)
** Data Source (e.g., Local, State Level, Food Distribution)
** Edit and Delete actions for the asset and expense accounts
* *Business Rules*
** Every valuation category must have *both* an Asset Account and an Expense Account mapped before it can be used in postings.
** Changes to account mappings or valuation method only affect *future postings* and do not retroactively change historical posted values.
** Each asset and expense account can only be linked to one valuation category.
* *Filters & Sorting*
** Ability to sort by any column (e.g., Valuation Group Name, Valuation Method).
** Ability to filter by Data Source.
* *Validation during account deletion:* 
**  If the user tries to delete an asset or expense account linked to a valuation category on the GL Accounts page, block it and inform the user that they must first assign a different account to that valuation group 
* *Change Management:*
** Once a valuation category has any posted activity, its Asset and Expense GL accounts become permanently locked and cannot be edited.
** If a district needs to change the GL accounts, they must create a new valuation category and begin using that going forward; historical categories remain frozen for audit integrity.
** Provide warning “This valuation category already has posted activity, so its GL accounts can’t be changed. To use different accounts, create a new valuation category and move the items to it. Historical mappings are locked for audit integrity.”
** Provide this tooltip also at the time of assignment
NXT-65482.desc:
*As a* district inventory manager
*I need* to view and maintain the list of inventory valuation categories
*So that* I can control how inventory items are categorized, valued, and mapped to the correct general ledger accounts for reporting and posting.

NXT-65481.summary: Income Statement Pt.3 Cost of Goods Sold
NXT-65481.ac:
*Columns to Display*

* Beginning Inventory
* Purchases
* Transfers (net in/out)
* Ending Inventory
* Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)

*Calculation*

* COGS = Beginning Inventory + Purchases + Transfers − Ending Inventory

*Category Breakdown*

* Show results by inventory category (e.g., Main Dish, Dairy, Bread, Supplies).
* Provide a total line for the selected scope (district, area, or site).

*Period Controls*

* Values reflect the fiscal month and year selected in the UI.
* Report results respect the user’s scope selection (District or Site).

*Functional Notes*

* COGS values also flow through to the Profit & Loss report under the *Expenses section* as *Cost of Sales*.
* This report reconciles to the General Ledger, since it uses the same ledger postings that drive financial statements.
* If the selected parameters return no data, generate an empty report with the columns
NXT-65481.desc:
*As a* district finance admin
*I need* a monthly Cost of Sales report by inventory valuation group (e.g., Main Dish, Dairy)
*So that* I can see Beginning Inventory, Purchases, Transfers, Ending Inventory, and *COGS* and reconcile to the GL

!image-20250811-210058.png|width=682,height=262,alt="image-20250811-210058.png"!

NXT-65480.ac:
# *Data Source*

* Pull reimbursable meal counts from the *Meal Count Report*, ensuring it includes all active federal and state meal programs (e.g., *SNP*, *CACFP*, *SFSP/Summer Meals*, *Afterschool Snack*, and any other district-participating programs).
* Use the *program-specific USDA/state reimbursement rates* in effect for the given period.
* Include separate line items by program in the backend for transparency, but aggregate to a *Total Government Reimbursements* figure in the main summary.

# *Calculation*

* For each program:
{{Earned Reimbursement = Σ (Meal Count × Applicable Rate)}}
* Total Government Reimbursements = Sum of all programs’ earned reimbursement amounts.

# *Accounting Treatment*

* Recognize revenue in the *current service period* (accrual method):
** Debit: _Due from Federal Funds (Asset)_
** Credit: _Reimbursement Program System Accounts (Revenue)_
* When payment is received:
** Debit: _Cash in Bank_
** Credit: _Due from Federal Funds_

# *Output Columns*


Match “Meal Sales Revenue” section’s columns for consistency:

* Current Period
* Prior Period
* Current CPM
* Prior CPM
* Current % of Revenue
* % Change from Prior Period
* Year-to-Date Total
* YTD CPM
* Item % of Category

# *Business Rules*

* Financial periods must be open from posting date to current date.
* All POS periods in the selected financial period’s range must be closed.
* Program meal counts must exclude non-reimbursable meals.
* Handle mid-year reimbursement rate changes by applying rates valid for the service date of each meal.
NXT-65480.desc:
As a district administrator,
I need to see total earned government reimbursements for the current and prior periods,
So that I can track all program reimbursements in the correct accounting period, regardless of when claim payments are received.

NXT-65475.ac:
*Data Collection & Metrics*

* For *every program and meal type defined in the district’s configuration* (e.g., NSLP Lunch, SBP Breakfast, CACFP Supper, SFSP Snack, Adult Meals, Second Meals, Milk, or any other configured type), display the following columns:
** Revenue for *Current Period*
** Revenue for *Prior Period*
** *Current Cost-per-Meal*
** *Prior Cost-per-Meal*
** *Percentage of Total Revenue* (current period)
** *Change from Prior Period* (percentage and/or dollar change)
** *Year-to-Date Total Revenue*
** *Year-to-Date Cost-per-Meal*
** *Percentage of Meal Sales Revenue* (YTD contribution)

*Period & Scope Controls*

* User selects *Fiscal Year* and *Period (month)*.
* Report includes only *closed POS periods* within the selected financial period.
* Results reflect scope selection: *District total (default)* with option to filter to *Area(s)* or *Site(s)*.

*Data Integrity Rules*

* Report reflects only postings generated with *required GL account mappings* (Cash in Bank, Meal Sales System Accounts, etc.) configured.
* Posting dates follow POS posting rules: last day of the POS period is used as the posting date.
* If parameters return no data (e.g., no closed periods for that range), display a *“No data available”* message.

*Output Requirements*

* Display metrics in a *tabular format*, grouped first by *Program*, then by *Meal Type*, with clear labels for Current Period, Prior Period, and YTD.
* Inline calculations must show:
** *Percentage of total revenue*
** *Percentage change from prior period*
* Allow *export* to CSV and PDF, preserving program + meal type groupings.

*Functional Notes*

* Report uses the same dataset that drives the *Accountability → Revenue Report* for consistency.
* Metrics must reconcile with POS postings in the General Ledger (ensuring alignment with financial statements).
* Drill-down supported from *District totals → Program totals → Meal type → Site level* when multiple sites are included.
NXT-65475.desc:
As a district administrator,
I need to view revenue and cost-per-meal metrics for the different types of meals served by the district,
So that I can compare current period performance against prior periods, monitor year-to-date trends, and evaluate the percentage contribution of each meal type to total revenue.

NXT-65133.ac:
h2. UI Section:

* -*Navigation*: "Add New Entry" button on Ledger/Journal View pages navigates directly to the Add New Entry form -
* *Informational Guide*: Page displays info box with text: "💡 Quick Guide: Use Quick Entry mode by selecting a GL Entry Type to quickly populate accounts based on common transaction patterns, or enable Manual Entry mode to build fully custom entries from scratch. All entries must balance (debits = credits)."
* -*Site Selection*: Searchable Site dropdown (required with red asterisk) displays all sites in "Site Name - Code" format with type-to-filter functionality-
* *District Level Posting*: "🏛️ Post to District Level" checkbox below Site field with italic helper text "Use this for district-wide transactions that don't belong to a specific school site". When checked, Site field is disabled, set to "District Level", and the site is no longer required
* -Provide a field where user can select the Posted Date which cannot exceed today’s date -
* -System must auto-capture the actual timestamp when the user saves the entry (entered timestamp), which should populate the “Entered Date” column in the general ledger and journal display table.-
* -*Entry Table*: Multi-line entry table with columns (Account, Debit, Credit, Actions). Default shows one row with "+" button to add rows and "−" button to remove rows (last row cannot be removed)-
* -*Real-Time Validation*: System calculates total debits and credits in real-time as user types, displays balance status, and enables "Make Entry" button only when debits equal credits and both totals are greater than zero-
* *-GL Entry Type Dropdown-*-: Required dropdown populated with all configured GL Entry Types. When no type is selected, account dropdowns show placeholder "Select an entry type first..."-
** This will be dealt with once the system rules are configured and is non-functional for now
* *Quick Entry Mode (Default)*: -When GL Entry Type is selected, all account dropdowns filter to show only the template-defined accounts from System Rules for that specific entry type, providing guided repeatable entry creation-
** This will be dealt with once the system rules are configured and is non-functional for now
* *Manual Entry Checkbox*: "📝 Manual Entry (build from scratch)" checkbox below GL Entry Type field. When checked, GL Entry Type dropdown is disabled/grayed out and no longer required (red asterisk hidden)
** Default behavior for now is that the manual entry checkbox is selected and cannot be unselected
* *Manual Entry Mode*: When Manual Entry checkbox is checked, all account dropdowns populate with complete list of GL accounts from GL Accounts configuration page with searchable type-to-filter functionality
* *-Mode Switching-*-: Unchecking Manual Entry re-enables GL Entry Type dropdown (makes it required again) and account dropdowns revert to showing template-defined accounts based on selected entry type-
** This will be dealt with once the system rules are configured and is non-functional for now
* -*Account Dropdown Behavior*: All account dropdowns are searchable with type-to-filter functionality allowing users to type and find accounts quickly-
* -*Dynamic Row Accounts*: Newly added rows automatically inherit current filtering rules (Manual Entry shows all GL accounts)-
* -*Form Validation*: All required fields must be completed and debits must equal credits before "Make Entry" button is enabled-
* -*Balance Display*: System displays current debit total, credit total, and balance status message to user in real-time as amounts are entered-

h2. Backend Section:

* -*Transaction ID Generation*: When user clicks "Make Entry" button, system generates a unique transaction ID for the journal entry that can be used for tracking and reference-
* -*Description Recording*: System saves the Description/Reason entered by the user and associates it with all line items in the transaction-
* -*Debit Line Recording*: For each row with a debit amount, system creates a debit record in the general ledger with the selected account, debit amount, transaction ID, date, and description-
* -*Credit Line Recording*: For each row with a credit amount, system creates a credit record in the general ledger with the selected account, credit amount, transaction ID, date, and description-
* -*Multi-Line Transaction Linking*: All debit and credit lines entered in a single submission are linked together by the same transaction ID, allowing the complete entry to be viewed as one cohesive transaction-
* -*Site Code Assignment - Site Level*: When a specific site is selected (e.g., "Lincoln High School - 001"), system extracts the numeric site code (001) and embeds it in the account structure as the site segment (e.g., 1000-2000-3000-001-50000)-
* -*Site Code Assignment - District Level*: When "Post to District Level" checkbox is checked, system uses "000" as the site segment in the account structure (e.g., 1000-2000-3000-000-50000) instead of a specific site code-
* -*GL Account Mapping*: System records transactions using the GL account codes selected from the account dropdown (e.g., "1010 - Cash in Bank" records as account 1010 with appropriate site segment)-
* -*Entry Type Recording*: System marks all entries as "Manual Entry" type for reporting and audit purposes-
* -*Balance Verification*: System verifies one final time that total debits equal total credits before committing the transaction to the general ledger-
* -*Transaction Confirmation*: After successful recording, system displays confirmation message with the transaction ID and returns user to the Journal View or Ledger View showing the newly created entry-
* -*Audit Trail*: System records the username and timestamp for audit and compliance tracking-
* -*Reversibility*: Recorded transactions can be identified by their unique transaction ID for future reversal or adjustment if needed-
NXT-65133.desc:
As a School Finance Administrator, 

I want to create journal entries with flexible account selection and validation, 

so that I can efficiently record both template-based and manual transactions while ensuring financial accuracy.

Dev Notes

* Only manual entries are in scope for this story

NXT-65132.ac:

* select account to debit and credit (can be multiple)

NXT-65132.desc: As a user, I want to make manual entries so that I can see other transactions on the ledger.

NXT-65124.ac:
h1. *Page Header:*

* Page title displays "View Ledger"

h1. *Help/Info Section:*

* Collapsible info panel at top with header "What does this page show? (Click to expand)"
* When expanded, shows two sections side-by-side:

*Left section - "What is a Ledger?"*

* Header with bar chart emoji: "📊 What is a Ledger?"
* Text: "Think of a ledger like your bank account statement. It shows you the running balance for a specific account (like 'Student Prepaid Accounts' or 'Cash in Bank') over time. Every time money goes in or out, you see exactly how it affects your total balance."
* Example text in italics: "Example: If you want to see how much money students have in their lunch accounts right now, and what transactions changed that amount, you'd look at the Prepaid Account ledger."

*Right section - "Journal vs Ledger - What's the difference?"*

* Header with pencil emoji: "📝 Journal = Daily Diary"
* Text: "Shows ALL transactions that happened on specific days, like 'June 15th: Lincoln High lunch sales, Kennedy Middle supply delivery, etc.'"
* Header with bar chart emoji: "📊 Ledger = Account History"
* Text: "Shows how ONE specific account changed over time, like 'Everything that affected the Student Prepaid Account balance.'"

*Quick Tip:*

* Light bulb icon with text: "💡 Quick tip: Use the filters above to pick which account you want to see, then view how deposits and purchases affected that account's balance over your selected time period."

h1. *Filter Options:*

* Searchable dropdown labeled "Account" with red asterisk (required field)
* Dropdown populated with all GL accounts from the chart of accounts
* User can type to search/filter accounts
* Date range picker with two fields:
** "From (Posted Date)" date field (format: MM/DD/YYYY)
** "To (Posted Date)" date field (format: MM/DD/YYYY)
* Purple "Apply" button to execute filters
* When account is changed, date filters remain the same

h1. *Ledger Summary Line:*

* Below filters, shows: "Account: [Account Name] | Date Range: [MM/DD/YYYY] to [MM/DD/YYYY]"
* Account name is plain text (not clickable)

h1. *Ledger Table Display:*

* Table with columns (left to right):
** Posted Date (format: MM/DD/YYYY, sortable with ↕ icon)
** Transaction ID
*** Clicking the transaction ID shows the complete GL account breakdown for this transaction.
** Location (text, e.g., "001 Lincoln High School" or "District")
** Description (text)
** Entered Date
** Entered By (username)
** Debit (currency format with $ and commas, shows empty/blank if $0)
** Credit (currency format with $ and commas, shows empty/blank if $0)

*Reversal Display:*

* Original transactions that have been reversed show yellow badge with text "REVERSED" next to description
* Original transaction shows clickable link "Reversed by #XXXXX" (where XXXXX is transaction ID, displayed as brown/orange hyperlink that scrolls to that transaction in the current ledger view)
* Reversal transactions show yellow badge with text "Reversal of #XXXXX" (where XXXXX is clickable brown/orange hyperlink that scrolls to the original transaction in the current ledger view)
* Reversal transaction descriptions are prefixed with "REVERSAL: " followed by user-entered reason

*Account Balance Bar (Fixed at Bottom):*

* Bar fixed at bottom of viewport (stays visible when scrolling), styled consistently with existing UI theme elements
* Shows: "Account Balance for [Account Name]: [Balance Amount]"
* Below header, displays four rows vertically stacked with labels left-aligned and amounts right-aligned: 
** Top Line (in header): “Current Balance (as of MM/DD/YYYY):” followed by dollar amount
*** Date shown is today’s date (system date when the page is rendered/refreshed).
*** Amount is calculated using all posted transactions for this account (and selected location) through today, following the account-type balance rules.
*** Displayed in large, emphasized text with positive-number styling per UI theme. This is the primary focal point of the balance section.
** Row 1: “Beginning Balance (MM/DD/YYYY):” followed by dollar amount
*** Date shown is the From (Posted Date) from the filters.
*** Amount is calculated based on account type using all posted transactions before the From date (see balance calculation rules).
** Row 2: “Total Debits:” followed by dollar amount
*** Sum of all debit amounts for this account within the filtered Posted Date range (inclusive).
** Row 3: “Total Credits:” followed by dollar amount
*** Sum of all credit amounts for this account within the filtered Posted Date range (inclusive).
** Row 4: “Ending Balance (MM/DD/YYYY):” followed by dollar amount
*** Date shown is the To (Posted Date) from the filters.
*** Amount represents the net balance at the end of the filtered period, calculated from Beginning Balance + period debits/credits per account-type rules.
*** Displayed with standard emphasis (secondary to the Current Balance at the top)
* All dollar amounts align vertically on the right side of the bar. Labels are left-aligned. All amounts use standard accounting format with dollar sign, commas, and two decimal places.
* If any balance amount (beginning balance or ending balance) is negative, display in red with parentheses: ($1,250.00)
* Balance Calculation Rules by Account Type: Account type (Asset/Liability/Equity/Revenue/Expense) is automatically determined by the account's assigned category in the chart of accounts.
** Beginning Balance Calculation: 
*** For Asset accounts (Cash, Inventory, Prepaid, Receivables): - Beginning Balance = SUM(all debits - all credits) WHERE posted_date < start_date filter
*** For Expense accounts: - Beginning Balance = SUM(all debits - all credits) WHERE posted_date < start_date filter AND posted_date >= current_fiscal_year_start_date - If start_date filter is at or after the current fiscal year start date, beginning balance reflects activity since fiscal year start - If start_date filter is before the current fiscal year start date, beginning balance = $0.00 (expense accounts close at fiscal year end)
*** For Liability accounts (Accounts Payable, Accrued Expenses): - Beginning Balance = SUM(all credits - all debits) WHERE posted_date < start_date filter
*** For Equity accounts: - Beginning Balance = SUM(all credits - all debits) WHERE posted_date < start_date filter
*** For Revenue accounts: - Beginning Balance = SUM(all credits - all debits) WHERE posted_date < start_date filter AND posted_date >= current_fiscal_year_start_date - If start_date filter is at or after the current fiscal year start date, beginning balance reflects activity since fiscal year start - If start_date filter is before the current fiscal year start date, beginning balance = $0.00 (revenue accounts close at fiscal year end)
** Ending Balance Calculation: 
*** For Asset accounts: - Ending Balance = Beginning Balance + Total Debits - Total Credits 
*** For Expense accounts: - Ending Balance = Beginning Balance + Total Debits - Total Credits 
*** For Liability accounts: - Ending Balance = Beginning Balance + Total Credits - Total Debits 
*** For Equity accounts: - Ending Balance = Beginning Balance + Total Credits - Total Debits 
*** For Revenue accounts: - Ending Balance = Beginning Balance + Total Credits - Total Debits

*Sorting Behavior:*

* Entries are sorted by Posted Date in descending order (newest first) by default
* User can click column headers to sort by that column
** Entered Date
** Transaction ID
** Debits
** Credits
** Username
** Allow user to type in to filter down to what they need
* Sorting selection persists when changing date filters (but resets when changing account)

*Pagination:*

* Ledger entries are displayed with pagination controls at bottom (Previous / Page X of Y / Next)
* Each page loads a limited number of entries (10 per page)
* Pagination maintains the current filter and sort state across pages

*Empty State Handling (No Transactions in Selected Date Range):*

* *When triggered*
** Condition: there are *no transactions with Posted Date between From and To (inclusive)* for the selected account / location.
* *Transaction table*
** Do *not* show any rows.
** Instead, show the message centered in the table area:
“No transactions found for this account in the selected date range.”
* *Account balance bar behavior*
** The balance bar *shows and uses the normal balance logic*.
** Values are calculated as if the table had zero rows in the period:
*** *Beginning Balance (From Date)*
**** Calculated per account-type rules using *all posted transactions before the From date*.
**** This may be *non-zero* if there were transactions before the selected period (e.g., July activity when August is selected).
*** *Total Debits*
**** {{0.00}} (because there are no transactions in the filtered date range).
*** *Total Credits*
**** {{0.00}} (because there are no transactions in the filtered date range).
*** *Ending Balance (To Date)*
**** Equal to the *Beginning Balance*, since there is no activity in the selected range.
**** Calculated as: {{Ending Balance = Beginning Balance + period debits/credits (which are zero)}}.
*** *Current Balance (as of today)*
**** Unchanged; always computed from *all posted transactions through today*, independent of the selected date range.
* *True “no history” case* (optional explicit rule)
** If the account has *no posted transactions at all* (before or within the filter range):
*** Beginning Balance = 0.00
*** Total Debits = 0.00
*** Total Credits = 0.00
*** Ending Balance = 0.00
*** Current Balance = 0.00
NXT-65124.desc:
Users need to view the transaction history and running balance for a specific GL account over a selected time period.

*Dev Notes:*

* This sprint covers MANUAL entries only
* System-generated entries (automated postings from POS/Inventory modules) will be implemented later
* Base type (Asset/Liability/Equity/Revenue/Expense) is determined by the account's assigned category in the general ledger configuration
* Balance calculation must follow GAAP standard accounting rules based on base type
* -Default fiscal year to begin from 7/1 to 6/30-

NXT-65123.ac:
*Page Header:*

* Page title displays "View Journal"

*Help/Info Section:*

* Collapsible info panel at top with header "What does this page show? (Click to expand)"
* When expanded, shows two sections side-by-side:

*Left section - "What is a Journal?"*

* Header with pencil emoji: "📝 What is a Journal?"
* Text: "Think of a journal like a daily diary of all money activity. It shows EVERY transaction that happened on specific dates, across ALL accounts and schools, in the order they occurred."
* Example text in italics: "Example: 'On June 15th: Lincoln High had lunch sales, Kennedy Middle got food delivery, District Office paid utilities' - all mixed together chronologically."

*Right section - "Journal vs Ledger - Quick Difference"*

* Header with pencil emoji: "📝 Journal = Daily Diary"
* Text: "ALL transactions by date: 'What happened June 15th across the entire district?'"
* Header with bar chart emoji: "📊 Ledger = Account History"
* Text: "ONE account over time: 'How did Student Prepaid Accounts change this month?'"

*Quick Tip:*

* Light bulb icon with text: "💡 Quick tip: Use filters to narrow down to specific dates or schools. Click any journal entry to see the detailed breakdown."

*Filter Options:*

* Searchable dropdown labeled "Location" with options:
** "-- ALL --" (default, shows all transactions)
** "District" (shows only district-level transactions)
** Individual sites (e.g., "001 Lincoln High School", "002 Kennedy Middle School")
*** Enable multi-select
** User can type to search/filter locations
* Date range picker with two fields:
** "From (Posted Date)" date field (format: MM/DD/YYYY)
** "To (Posted Date)" date field (format: MM/DD/YYYY)
** This refers to the post date
* Users can filter to see only reversed transactions (displays reversed and reversal pair)
* Purple "Apply" button to execute filters

*Paginated Journal List:*

* Journal entries are displayed with pagination controls at bottom (Previous / Page X of Y / Next)
* Each page loads a limited number of journal entries of 10 per page
* Pagination maintains the current filter state across pages
* User can navigate between pages without resetting filters

*Journal List Display:*

* Table with columns (left to right):
** Expandable arrow (▶)
** Posted Date (format: MM/DD/YYYY)
** Transaction ID (blue hyperlink, numeric format like "10001")
** Location (text, e.g., "001 Lincoln High School" or "District")
** Description (text - this comes from the user-entered description when creating manual entry)
** Entered Date (format: MM/DD/YYYY)
** User Name (text)
** Total Amount (currency format with $ and commas, e.g., "$450.00")
** Reverse icon (↻ symbol)

*Expandable Row:*

* Clicking arrow or anywhere on row expands to show line-level details
* Expanded section has light purple/lavender background
* Shows table with columns:
** Location
** Account Code (alphanumeric, e.g., "A-4100.1547")
** Account Description (text, e.g., "Purchased Food" - this comes from the GL account master data, NOT user-entered description)
** Debit (currency format, shows "-" if no value)
** Credit (currency format, shows "-" if no value)

*Reversal Display:*

* Original transactions that have been reversed show yellow badge with text "REVERSED" next to description
* Reversal transactions show yellow badge with text "REVERSED" next to description
* Reversal transaction descriptions are prefixed with "REVERSAL: " followed by user-entered reason

*Data Accuracy:*

* Sum of debit amounts in expanded view equals sum of credit amounts
* Total in summary row matches the net of debits/credits

*Reverse Function:*

* Clicking reverse icon (↻) opens side drawer from right
* Side drawer title: "Reverse Transaction"
* Side drawer shows original transaction details:
** Transaction ID
** Posted Date
** Location
** Description
** Total Amount
** Debit/Credit breakdown
* Side drawer has required text field labeled "Reason for reversal" (cannot be empty)
* Side drawer has two buttons: "Cancel" (gray) and "Reverse Transaction" (purple/primary)
* After clicking "Reverse Transaction", side drawer closes and page refreshes showing both original (now with REVERSED badge) and new reversal transaction

*Sorting & Filter Behavior*

* *Default sort*
** Grid is *sorted by Posted Date in descending order (newest first)* by default.
** Applied on initial load and whenever filters are cleared.
* *Sortable columns*
** User can change sort by clicking any sortable column header (e.g., Posted Date, Entered Date, Transaction ID, Username).
** Sorting persists when navigating between pages until the user changes it or clears filters.
* *Searchable / filterable fields*
** The grid supports column-level filters for:
*** *Entered Date* (date picker / range)
*** *Posted Date* (date picker / range)
*** *Transaction ID* (exact or “starts with” text search)
*** *Username / Entered By* (text search, case-insensitive, “contains” match)
** Filters can be combined; results reflect the *intersection* of all active filters.
** When filters change, the current sort order (e.g., Posted Date ↓) is preserved unless explicitly changed by the user.

*Permissions & Visibility:*

* Only users with appropriate permissions can see the reverse icon (↻)
* Users without reverse permission do not see the icon at all
* User roles determine visibility of financial data (e.g., district-wide vs. location-specific access based on assigned locations)

*Empty State Handling:*

* If no journal entries match the filters, display message: "No results found"
* Message appears centered in the table area where results would normally display

*Action Button:*

* Purple "Generate Report" button in bottom right corner (for exporting journal data)
** Functionality of generate report button is out of scope for this story
NXT-65123.desc:
As a user,
When I toggle to journal view, I want to view journal entries by site, date, and account details,
So that I can track financial transactions and verify posting accuracy for audits and reconciliation.

*Dev Notes:*

* This sprint covers MANUAL entries only
* System-generated entries (automated postings from POS/Inventory modules) will be implemented later

NXT-65011.summary: GL Account Mapping in UI (not wizard)
NXT-65011.ac:
*Dashboard/Summary Section:*

* Dashboard shows mapping progress with counts: Total GL Accounts, Mapped, Unmapped, Structure Issues
* Users can search all accounts by name, code, or description
* Users can filter by account category to focus on specific account types

*Mapped Accounts Section:*

* Displays successful mappings with System Account, GL Account, Parent Account, and View Details columns
* Users can view and edit existing mappings between system accounts and imported GL accounts
* Users can delete existing mappings

*Unmapped System Accounts Section:*

* Shows system accounts that haven't been mapped yet
* Users can search system accounts by description or code
* Users can filter system accounts by type
* Table shows System Account, System Code, Type, and Action columns
* Users can click "Map" button to open mapping modal for individual system accounts
* Users can toggle system accounts on/off to enable/disable accounts they don't want to use
* Users can add new custom system accounts to the mapping list

*Mapping Modal:*

* Allows users to search and select from imported GL accounts
* Shows system account details (description, code, type, category) and selected GL account details
* Users can create mappings between system accounts and imported GL accounts

*Structure Issues Section:*

* Identifies mapped accounts where the imported account code doesn't follow the defined account code structure
* Users can view and fix structure compliance issues for mapped accounts

*Contextual Information:*

* An info icon appears next to each system account; hovering shows contextual help text explaining where the data is sourced from.
NXT-65011.desc:
*As a district financial manager,*
I want to map my general ledger accounts to the system accounts used by the software
so that I can generate accurate financial reports like the P&L, Balance Sheet, and Site-level breakdowns.

NXT-65010.ac:
* I see one grid showing all my GL accounts with columns: account code, description, base type, account category, and actions
** Sorting on every column
* Tooltip at the top that says “📊 GL Accounts: Manage your general ledger accounts imported from your Chart of Accounts. Individual GL accounts must be linked to Account Categories for proper financial reporting. For each account, you can configure site-specific sub-accounts or use a single account district-wide. Transactions are posted to these accounts, not to categories.”
* Account descriptions are limited to 50 characters
* I can search accounts by code or description
* I can filter accounts by base account type dropdown with options: All Types, Revenue, Expenditures, Assets, Liabilities, Fund Balance or with account categories
* I can click “Add New Account” to manually create an account by entering:
Account Code, Description, Account Category, Base Type (Asset / Liability / Revenue / Expenditure / Fund Balance) (comes from category and is greyed out, blank while no category is assigned)
* I can click "Edit" on any account to update its details
* -I can click "Delete" on any account to remove it-
** -If I try to delete an account with posted transactions, I see a warning message: "This account has posted transactions and cannot be deleted"-
* If any accounts have validation issues during import or after manual creation, I see a persistent banner: "8 accounts need structure corrections" with a count like "42/50 accounts validated"
* Invalid accounts show a warning triangle icon next to their account code
* Once I fix an invalid account, the warning icon disappears and the banner count updates
* When all accounts are valid, the warning banner disappears
* -I can clear filters to see all accounts again-
* -If an account category is deleted elsewhere in the system, accounts that were assigned to that category show a warning triangle icon under the account category column indicating they are orphaned-
NXT-65010.desc:
*As a* district financial manager
*I want to* view and manage my GL accounts in the "GL Accounts" section
*So that* I can keep my chart of accounts organized and ready for mapping

NXT-65009.ac:
* Users are able to see the account categories that they imported into the system and their base type (sorted by alphabetical order based on category description)
* Users are able to edit the list of account categories
* When adding new account categories, a base type must be assigned (Revenue, Expenditures, Assets, Liabilities, Fund Balance) along with a unique description with a 50-character limit
* Add collapsible accordion at the top that reads "📁 Account Categories: Required custom groupings for detailed financial reporting.
** Every GL account must be assigned to a category within its base type (Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, Expenditures, Fund Balance)
** Create sub-groups where needed (e.g., split Revenue into 'Food Sales', 'Catering', 'Vending')
** Account codes for categories are optional - we recommend matching your defined account code structure for better reporting
** Transactions post to individual accounts only, not categories
* Users can search account categories by code or description using the search box
* Users can filter account categories by base type using the dropdown filter
* Display shows total count of account categories and child accounts for each category
* Account categories are displayed in a paginated table format with columns:
** Category Description (name of the account category, required, must be unique)
** Base Type (Revenue, Expenditures, Assets, Liabilities, Fund Balance in dropdown, required)
** Category Code (optional, may be blank)
** Linked Accounts (count of accounts under this category)
** Actions (Edit icon, Delete icon)

Empty State (No Account Categories):

* When no account categories exist (imported or created), display:
** The three help text accordions/textboxes as described above
** Empty table with column headers visible
** "Add Account Category" button
** No special empty state message or banner

Add Account Category Functionality:

* "Add Account Category" button positioned top-right of table near search/filter controls
* Clicking opens side drawer with fields:
** Category Description* (required, max 50 chars, must be unique)
** Base Type* (required dropdown: Revenue, Expenditures, Assets, Liabilities, Fund Balance)
** Category Code (optional)
*** If provided and doesn't match district's configured format: shows warning
*** Warning message: "⚠️ This code doesn't match your configured format. You can still save, but we recommend using the same structure for better reporting"
*** If provided, must be unique

Edit Account Category Functionality:

* Click edit icon in Actions column to open edit sidedrawer
* Edit side drawer pre-populates with existing values
* Same fields and validation as Add side drawer
* Success notification after edit: "Account category updated successfully"

What Blocks Saving:

* Category Description already exists
* Category Code already exists (only if code is provided)
* Required fields are empty

What Shows Warning But Allows Saving:

* Category Code doesn't match the configured account code structure (if provided)

Table Display:

* Successfully created/edited categories appear in table
* Categories without codes show blank in Category Code column
* Any category with non-matching format code shows ⚠️ icon next to Category Code
* Tooltip on warning icon: "This category code doesn't match your configured account structure format"

Success Messages:

* Add: "Account category created successfully"
* Edit: "Account category updated successfully"

Error Messages:

* Duplicate description: "This category description already exists. Please use a unique description."
* Duplicate code: "This category code already exists. Please use a unique code."
* Save failure: "Unable to save account category. Please try again."
NXT-65009.desc:
*As a* district business administrator configuring Financial Oversight 

*I want* to view and manage account categories imported from our Chart of Accounts 

*So that* I can organize GL accounts under the correct categories (Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, Expenses) and ensure proper financial reporting structure

Note: This is Parent Accounts in the PrimeroEdge version but renamed

!Screenshot 2025-11-06 at 9.53.48 AM-20251106-155353.png|width=761,alt="Screenshot 2025-11-06 at 9.53.48 AM-20251106-155353.png"!



!image-20251028-040216.png|width=1082,alt="image-20251028-040216.png"!

NXT-65006.ac:
* *Authoritative catalog available*
A single, [complete catalog|https://cybersofttechnologies-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/rahul_mehta_cybersoft_net/EZdA4m_hj4BPpv72wA8A82oB9MCtBeHu-uVUeTprpifx4Q?e=xc7pet] of system accounts exists and is used by all features that need it (e.g., GL mapping, reports).
* *Standard categories*
Each account belongs to one of the defined categories: All Meal Sales, All Inventory (Asset), All Inventory (Expense), Materials Used, All Reimbursements, All A La Carte Sales, Core Financial Accounts.
* *Context/help note (“source of data”)*
Each account can include a short help note explaining where the data comes from (e.g., “This data is sourced from XYZ report”). If no note exists, nothing is shown in the UI. 
* *Toggle behavior*
Accounts can be toggled on or off
* *Search & filter*
• filter accounts by category,
• search by words in the account’s description or code/label,
• accounts toggled on vs off 
* *Extensible without disruption*
New accounts can be added later without impacting existing mappings or reports.
* *Consistency across districts*
By default the catalog is global/standardized for all districts
NXT-65006.desc:
*As a product manager,* 

*I want* developers to create and maintain a system accounts database 

*so that* district financial managers can view all available system accounts and make informed mapping decisions.

NXT-65000.ac:
* Users can view a *list of entry types* that the system will post transactions for (e.g., Meal Sales, Adjustments, Inventory).
* For each entry type, users can see:
** The *system accounts* involved,
** The *account type/category* (e.g., All Meal Sales, All A La Carte Sales), and
** The *credit/debit designation* for each account.
* Users can expand any entry type to see the *detailed transaction rules* (e.g., “Debit Cash in Bank OR Prepaid Account, Credit Meal Sales”).
* Users are able to *add new accounts* under an entry type.
* Users are able to *add new templates* for ledger entry types to customize posting rules.
* The UI clearly indicates *read-only system rules* versus those that can be edited or extended.
NXT-65000.desc:
*As a* district business administrator configuring Financial Oversight,
*I want* to view the debit/credit rules tied to each type of ledger entry,
*So that* I can clearly understand how transactions will be posted and ensure correct account mappings.

Note: In terms of functionality, this is the GL Entry Types tab under configuration in Financials for PrimeroEdge

The full list of rules for system accounts can be found [here|https://cybersofttechnologies-my.sharepoint.com/:b:/g/personal/rahul_mehta_cybersoft_net/EVJ13VdumHpGv0PL82CL2mEB-bTzNUuLco71gdE6-sLonw?e=KeHWB7]

NXT-64976.ac:
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

* *State Template Selection*: I can select my state from a dropdown to auto-populate segments with state-compliant structure
* *Template Modification*: After selecting a state template, I can edit, add, or remove segments as needed
* *Reset to Template*: I can reset back to the original state template if I made changes
* I can add any number of segments with custom descriptions (Fund, Location, Department, etc.)
* For each segment, I can set: description, type (numeric/alphanumeric), length, and separator character
* Real-time preview shows how my segments build the complete account code format
* I can reorder segments by changing position numbers
* Site segment configuration includes options for separators and inclusion in account codes
* "Preview Account Codes" generates examples using my actual segment structure
* Can edit or delete segments at any time
* Real-time character count updates as segments are added/modified
* Account code examples update immediately when segments change
* Drag and drop reordering works for position changes
* Includes a mandatory 'Site' segment that can be reordered but not deleted or renamed

Note: Use branding elements (e.g. fonts, colors) as per design guidelines for SchoolCafe 2.0, consistent with the rest of the product.
NXT-64976.desc:
*As a* district business administrator configuring the Financial Oversight module
*I want* to configure the state-required accounting code structure
*So that* I can stay in compliance with audit requirements

h3. Context: 

This page is similar to the GL Code Segments page in PrimeroEdge’s Financials module. There are a few key differences, however:

* We will refer to the page as ‘Account Code Structure’ instead of ‘GL Code Segments’
* We will provide helpful information in a text box so the user understands what is expected of them here
* Let users pick from state-mandated templates to speed up onboarding

Note that there will always be a Site segment that the user cannot delete and will help them see transactions at the site level
